text: A genome-wide association study analyzed 425 foxtail millet samples from Xinjiang Academy using 1,304,248 SNPs. 77 QTL regions were detected across three environments. Analysis of LD, genetic structure, clustering, and phylogeny showed regional differences among foxtail millet subgroups.
ABA Insensitive 5 (ABI5) is a basic leucine zipper transcription factor. There is minimal research on the ABI5 family in foxtail millet.

Molecular markers identified chromosomal regions with powdery mildew resistance genes and estimated each locus's resistance effect. Twenty-eight F-1 hybrids and eight parental lines from a barley diallel study were inoculated with five isolates of E. graminis.

Marker-assisted breeding is important for genetic improvement. This study aimed to identify QTL associated with bioenergy-related traits in sweet sorghum, including flowering time, plant height, total biomass, stem diameter, stem moisture percentage, and brix.

CRISPR components targeting the sorghum genome were delivered to protoplasts via polyethylene glycol-mediated transfection, both plasmid-mediated and ribonucleoprotein-based. Targeted deep sequencing analyzed frequencies of small insertions and deletions induced by sgRNAs targeting four endogenous sorghum genes.

Protoplasts were isolated from sorghum leaves. Plants were cultivated under three conditions. Cultivation with seven days of a 16-h light/8-h dark photoperiod followed by three days of continuous darkness yielded the highest viable protoplasts and transfection efficiency.
Clust analysis found 2987 genes correlated with increasing CC and RWC. Pathway analysis identified ribosome as a positive regulator of RWC and thermogenesis as a positive regulator of CC.
Over 240 QTLs are reported for drought tolerance traits in sorghum for marker discovery. Identifying traits and understanding their physiological and genetic mechanisms may enhance HT tolerance.
GsNAC2 was analyzed by bioinformatics methods. Sorghum plants were treated with saline-alkali stress solution at 2 weeks old. GsNAC2 belongs to the NAC gene family.

The drought-resistant cultivar JiaYan 2 (JIA2) and water-sensitive cultivar BaYou 9 (BA9) were subjected to three water gradient treatments during the booting stage: 30% field capacity (severe stress), 45% field capacity (moderate stress), and 70% field capacity (normal water supply). After 7 days, root samples were collected for transcriptome and proteome analyses.

Quinoa seedlings were treated with 24-epibrassinolide (EBR). CqBIN2 was transiently transferred to the quinoa seedlings' leaves using VIGS technology. EBR treatment increased root growth parameters, antioxidant enzyme activities, and osmolyte content. It decreased H2O2, O2 center dot-, and malondialdehyde content.

Four candidate genes for sdw3 were identified within barley BAC sequences. They co-segregated with sdw3 or were adjacent to co-segregating genes. This identification provides tools for marker-assisted selection.
12 QTL for leaf Delta C-13 were detected in the W x I RILs. 5 QTL were detected in the M x H RILs. For the W x I RILs, a major QTL was located on chromosome 3H near marker Bmag606.

This study identified 5 QTLs for thousand grain weight (TGW), 9 for grain length (GL), and 1 for grain width (GW) using data from 202 RIL populations. One co-located QTL for TGW was discovered on chromosome 1. Transcriptome analysis revealed 59 candidate genes with significant expression differences during grain development.

A recombinant inbred line population derived from a cross between DZZ and KL10 was developed. A high-density genetic map was constructed using genotyping-by-sequencing data, and QTL analysis was performed across five environments.
Several SiLRR-RLK genes are involved in stress response pathways. SiLRR-RLK genes show differential expression in response to drought, salinity, and pathogen infection.
RT-qPCR verified the transcriptome data. Inhibiting SiPME67 expression with antisense Oligonucleotide caused abnormal pollen tube subapical growth.

GWAS identified MTAs for oat nutritional quality, agronomic, and milling traits in spring oat germplasm. High-density sequence-based markers (15,037) were identified in 1,092 lines evaluated in 11 field conditions over three years.
FtbHLH2 localizes in the nucleus. Its overexpression in Arabidopsis increases cold tolerance.

Comparative genomics and QTL analysis revealed the genetic basis of the easily dehulled trait in a particular variety. This variety was artificially selected by the Wa people, who cultivate Tartary buckwheat as a staple food to prevent lysine deficiency.

Sorghum plants were exposed to abiotic stress treatments. Hsf expression was not observed with ABA treatment. Hsf1 showed high expression during high temperature and cold stresses. Hsf4 and Hsf6 showed expression during salt stress. Hsf5, Hsf6, Hsf10, Hsf13, Hsf19, Hsf23 and Hsf25 showed expression during drought stress.

We phenotyped 590 diverse sorghum accessions from West Africa in 10 environments under drought stress conditions: preflowering water stress (WS1), postflowering water stress (WS2), well-watered (WW), and rainfed (RF) over 4 years. Traits measured include days to 50% flowering (DFLo), aboveground dry biomass (DBM), plant height (PH), and grain yield components: grain weight (GrW), panicle weight (PW), grain number (GrN) per plant, and 1000-grain weight (TGrW). Genome-wide association studies (GWAS) were conducted. Broad-sense heritability for biomass and grain yield was 33-92% across environments.

Markers for chlorophyll content and leaf traits were linked to genes including PDCD4, LEA proteins, and Cytochrome P450. These findings provide molecular markers and genotypes for genomics-assisted breeding in sorghum.
Of 8,361 high-quality InDels longer than 20 bp developed as molecular markers, 180 were validated with 91.5% accuracy. SSR41 and these markers were used to map the white leaf sheath gene SiWLS1.
GsNAC2 overexpression upregulated key glutathione biosynthesis genes. It increased GR and GSH-Px activities and accumulated more GSH after saline-alkali treatment.
bZIPs are crucial for abiotic stress resistance. Setaria italica is a model for understanding stress resistance mechanisms.

Jingu 21 resistance to 31 herbicides was categorized into five levels: extremely weak, weak, moderate, strong, and extremely strong. Jingu 21 showed extremely strong resistance to lactofen, butachlor, and anilofos.
The stay green line SC56 is from a different source than B35. Two stay green QTLs identified in this study correspond to stay green QTL regions in maize.

SNPs induced by EMS were unevenly distributed across all 18 chromosomes, with an average mutation frequency of 91.2 SNPs/Mb. A positive correlation between SNP number and chromosome length was identified.

A GA(3) concentration of 50 mg/L is optimal for sorghum (Jitian 3) under salt stress. Whole-transcriptome analysis between GA(3)-treated and control sorghum leaves under salt stress identified 1002 DE-mRNAs, 81 DE-lncRNAs, 7 DE-circRNAs, and 26 DE-miRNAs in sorghum following GA(3) treatment.

The genetic effect of lnt2 was verified in two other genetic backgrounds, explaining variances of 86.43% and 91.01% in tiller numbers between lines with lnt2 mutant and wild-type alleles. A large F2 population was constructed and lnt2 was fine-mapped.
PUB genes play a role in regulating salt stress in sorghum. PUB genes might serve as targets for breeding salt-tolerant sorghum.
Foxtail millet is a reserve crop for extreme weather and a model crop for C4 gene research. Analyzing its flowering regulatory network is important for cultivating early-maturing varieties.
Drought tolerance traits, stay green, and grain yield have been studied. Traits such as restricted transpiration and root architecture need to be explored and used in breeding.

This analysis included 337 QTL for abiotic stress tolerance traits. It identified 79 metaQTL (MQTL): 26 for drought, 11 for low temperature, 22 for salinity, 17 for water-logging, and 3 for mineral toxicity and deficiency. The MQTL distribution was similar to the initial QTL distribution.

833 DEGs were found in cultivar JS6 and 2166 DEGs in cultivar NM5 under simulated drought using 20% PEG-6000 for 6 hours (JS6T6, NM5T6) and 24 hours (JS6T24, NM5T24). The DEGs in JS6T6 and JS6T24 were 0.298-fold and 0.754-fold higher than in NM5T6 and NM5T24, respectively. More DEGs were found in the T6 treatments than in the T24 treatments compared to their controls.

BY utilized sugars more effectively via sugar consumption. Amino acids strengthened metabolism and upregulated lignin, which were positive responses of BY roots to saline-alkali stress. This led to higher osmotic adjustment of solute concentrations and cell growth. YZY mainly used soluble sugars and flavonoids combined with sugars to form glycosides as osmotic regulatory or antioxidant substances to cope with saline-alkali stress.

RNA-sequencing analysis of foxtail millet pollen, ovule, glume, seedling, and root revealed 940 up-regulated genes in pollen. These up-regulated genes are involved in pollen tube growth processes including receptor kinases, small peptides, calcium signaling, small G proteins, vesicle transport, cytoskeleton, cell wall correlation, and transcription factors.

Restriction site polymorphisms were used to investigate co-location of candidate genes with QTL for seedling drought stress-induced premature senescence in cowpea. Genomic DNA from 113 F-2:8 RILs of drought-tolerant IT93K503-1 and susceptible CB46 genotypes was digested with EcoR1, HpaII, Mse1, or Msp1 restriction enzymes and amplified with primers from 13 drought-responsive cDNAs.

Early season cold affects seed germination, seedling vigor, and root architecture in sorghum. Late-season frost reduces fertility, yield, and nutrition quality and content in sorghum. An association mapping study was conducted to delineate the genetics of early season cold and late-season frost tolerance in sorghum.

The genetic structure of 142 sorghum parent lines was analyzed using a model-based approach with SSR markers. Forty-one SSR markers were selected from 103 markers. These markers generated 189 alleles, ranging from 2 to 11 per marker, with an average of 4.6.

The locus N1 controls the covered/hulless character. N1 affected most measured traits. Additive QTL models with N1 as a covariate were superior to models based on separate covered and hulless sub-populations.

Promoter analysis revealed abundant hormone- and stress-responsive cis-elements. Tissue-specific RNA-seq data showed root-enriched expression of SbDIR2, SbDIR4, SbDIR18, SbDIR39, SbDIR44, and SbDIR53, implicating their roles in root development.

SbCASP4 improves salt tolerance in sweet sorghum by enhancing the root apoplastic barrier and blocking sodium ion transport to the shoot. Sweet sorghum is a C4 crop tolerant to salt, drought, and waterlogging.
Hsfs4 and Hsfs22 are highly expressed in panicle. Hsfs4 and Hsfs9 are expressed in seedling.

Quinoa is an economic crop. Drought affects quinoa yield. Clarifying quinoa adaptation to drought is conducive to cultivating drought-tolerant varieties. The study of quinoa drought stress-related metabolism and identification of related metabolites are unknown.

443 genotype-specific high NH4+-responsive DEGs with sequence variation were identified by integrating transcriptome and genome re-sequencing data. TFs including MYB, MADS, and LBD genes and RLKs such as FtBAM1/3 may help adapt to high NH4+.

DEP in Bai5 is classified into protein processing in the endoplasmic reticulum, estrogen signaling pathway, antigen processing and presentation, longevity regulating pathway-multiple species, arginine and proline metabolism, beta-alanine metabolism, vitamin B6 metabolism, salmonella infection, chloroalkane and chloroalkene degradation, and limonene and pinene degradation. DEP in Vao-9 are classified as ribosome and carbon fixation pathways in prokaryotes, pentose and glucuronate interconversions, GABA ergic synapse, and taurine and hypotaurine metabolism.

F-1 and F-2 plants inoculated with C. graminicola isolates showed that resistance to anthracnose in sorghum accession G 73 segregated as a recessive trait in a cross with susceptible cultivar HC 136. A PCR-based SCAR marker was developed for marker-assisted selection in sorghum breeding.
Two minor QTLs (qBX3.1 and qBX7.1) for Brix (BX) were mapped on chromosomes 3 and 7. The QTLs in two clusters (qPH7.1/qBX7.1 and qPH7.1/qFBW7.1) overlapped for PH, FBW and BX.

A drought tolerance QTL on chromosome 2 was transferred from barley cultivar 'Tadmor' to cultivars 'Baronesse' and 'Aydanhanim'. The QTL's effects on drought tolerance and other traits were studied using near-isogenic lines in controlled environments and field trials.

The two AFLP markers were converted to STS markers. The STS markers are useful for marker-assisted selection of plants with non-brittle pedicel and for map-based cloning of the non-brittle pedicel gene.

Transcriptome data showed high expression of genes VfbZIP1, VfbZIP2, VfbZIP5, VfbZIP7, VfbZIP15, VfbZIP17, and VfbZIP18 in a drought-tolerant cultivar under drought stress. qRT-PCR confirmed consistent expression of VfbZIP1, VfbZIP2, and VfbZIP5. Ectopic overexpression of VfbZIP5 in tobacco using a PVX vector enhanced drought tolerance.

The transcriptome findings suggested that 2253 differentially expressed genes (DEGs) are drought-responsive genes influenced by SiNRX1. The DEGs were enriched in pathways including phenylalanine, tyrosine and tryptophan biosynthesis, plant hormone signal transduction, phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, and plant-pathogen interaction.

The Milletdb database contains GWAS results for 20 yield-related traits. These traits were measured under three environmental conditions: field (no stress), early drought, and late drought over two years.

Sacaca and QQ57 exhibited high salt tolerance thresholds, low salt sensitivity index, and high Na+ sequestration capacity into vacuoles. A positive association was detected between salt tolerance degree and yield parameters, saponins (SAPs), and minerals content.

Inhibiting the expression of the FtIST1 gene in Tartary buckwheat by VIGS decreased the activities of antioxidant enzymes SOD, POD, and CAT. It increased plasmalemma permeability and leaf Na+ content. This indicates that loss of FtIST1 gene expression leads to decreased antioxidant capacity, increased cell membrane damage, increased shoot Na+ content, and decreased salt tolerance. FtIST1 is a salt tolerance gene associated with salt tolerance in Tartary buckwheat.

Increasing the training population size from 41 to 163 improved prediction accuracies for all traits. With the partial model, prediction accuracies ranged from 0.03 for thousand kernel weight to 0.58 for grain yield. With the full model, accuracies ranged from 0.06 for thousand kernel weight to 0.67 for grain yield. Genomic prediction is an effective tool for predicting sorghum hybrid performance based on parental genotypes.
Sobic.005G165632 and Sobic.005G165700 were located in the same LD block for qPC5-1. Sobic.006G149650 and Sobic.006G149700 were located in different LD blocks for qPC6.

Transcriptome and metabolite analyses identified 652 differentially expressed genes and 571 differentially expressed metabolites in FA-treated oat leaves under drought stress. These genes and metabolites are involved in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis and glutathione metabolism pathways.

Parent lines should be selected for higher heterosis based on different agronomical traits and distant genetic distance. Hybrid breeding should consider groups clustered by agronomical traits and molecular markers.

Sorghum overcomes limitations of model species like Arabidopsis thaliana. Advances in metabolomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, phenomics, population genomics and pangenomics expand understanding of Sorghum's drought resilience mechanisms.
An F-2 population was generated from a cross between sweet sorghum cv. 'Erdurmus' and grain sorghum cv. 'Ogretmenoglu'. A genetic map was constructed from SNPs discovered by ddRAD-seq.
Sorghum callus has metabolites. The epigenetic mechanism regulating metabolic biosynthesis in sorghum is unknown.
The marker OPA 12(383) was 6.03 cM from the locus for anthracnose resistance. The marker OPA 12(383) was cloned and sequenced.
Flowering time was recorded to confirm identification of known QTL. The study combined field visual ratings and reflectance data to identify GWAS marker-trait associations using GBS data.

Drought and HT tolerance can be improved by understanding tolerance mechanisms, screening germplasm to identify tolerant lines, and incorporating those traits into elite breeding lines. Systems approaches identify the best tolerance donors for SSA and SA sorghum breeding programs.

Overexpression of SiCDPK24 in Arabidopsis enhanced drought resistance and improved survival under drought stress. It activated the expressions of stress-related genes RD29A, RD29B, RD22, KIN1, COR15, COR47, LEA14, CBF3/DREB1A, and DREB2A.

The genetic variation and basis of A, E and A:E were described among 70 recombinant inbred lines (RILs) of sorghum. Experiment 1 used 40% and 80% of field capacity as water regimes. Experiment 2 used 80% field capacity.

The sweet sorghum SbWRKY50 gene (Sb09g005700) encodes a typical class II WRKY family protein. SbWRKY50 localizes to the nucleus and has transcriptional activation activity. Its expression in sweet sorghum was reduced by salt stress. Ectopic expression of SbWRKY50 reduced salt tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana plants.

For sorghum SSR markers, 2,113 primer pairs were designed from 81,342 public genomic contigs. Screening eight sorghum lines identified 1,758 polymorphic primers. Of these, 1,710 SSR markers were predominantly polymorphic and detected from two to eight alleles.
369 SNPs were associated with six grain traits. 30 SNPs were detected in multiple years.

Twenty-five sorghum accessions were evaluated. The LW7 marker identified accessions with the male sterility gene rf4 in Suphan Buri1 and 03B cultivars. These genotypes lacked the 779 bp band. The cross between Suphan Buri1 and the male-sterile line 03A produced a sterile male, confirming the marker's usefulness in breeding.

entities.text: Suphan Buri1 and the male-sterile line 03A
entities.label: CROSS

text: Two sorghum varieties, drought-sensitive Jinza 35 (V1) and drought-tolerant Longza 24 (V2), were studied under drought conditions. Transcriptomic analysis revealed distinct molecular mechanisms between the varieties. Both varieties showed drought-responsive changes in photosynthesis-related pathways.

The identified QTL include those in regions linked with seedling resistance genes Pc48, Pc58a, Pc68, Pc71, Pc91, and PcKM, and with adult-plant resistance and adaptation-related QTL. QTL on linkage groups Mrg03, Mrg08, and Mrg23 were identified in regions not previously associated with crown rust resistance.

The drought-tolerant variety V2 showed enrichment in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, starch-sucrose metabolism, and plant hormone signaling pathways. Variety V1 activated ribosome metabolism and cell cycle regulation pathways.

Hexaploid amphiploids have the AACCDD genome composition, while octoploid amphiploids have AACCCCDD, AAAACCDD, or AABBCCDD compositions. The A, B, C, and D oat genomes differ in involvement in non-centromeric, intercalary translocations.

entities.text: A, B, C, and D oat genomes
entities.label: CHR

text: Sweet sorghum accumulates sugars in the stem parenchyma. Soluble acid invertase (SAI) is involved in sucrose accumulation. Characterization of SAI genes and development of functional markers are important for marker-assisted selection.

Drought stress increased malondialdehyde (MDA) and hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) levels, with the highest accumulation in L3. Cultivar D maintained higher antioxidant enzyme activities (CAT, POD, SOD, APX), better photosynthetic performance, and greater chlorophyll retention than L3.

The study identified two zinc finger knuckle genes, Ca04468 and Ca07571, as candidates for drought response in chickpea. Sanger sequencing, DArT, molecular markers, RT-qPCR, and field trial seed trait associations were used for their analysis.

In the cultivar BY5, most metabolite concentrations increased after exposure to 150 mM NaCl but decreased after exposure to 300 mM NaCl. Transcriptomic analysis revealed that gene expressions in BY5 were significantly affected under 300 mM NaCl exposure (34040 genes up-regulated and 14757 genes down-regulated).

Salt-tolerant and salt-sensitive quinoa cultivars showed induced expression levels of CqGSTs genes in roots under salt treatment. Most quinoa bHLH transcription factors have high homology with Arabidopsis thaliana bHLH family members.

Dehydration response element binding proteins (DREBs) are transcriptional regulators that enhance plant tolerance to abiotic stress. This study analyzed the DREB gene family (SiDREBs) in foxtail millet (Setaria italica) using bioinformatics and gene expression under abiotic stress.

APR provides broad-spectrum resistance to Pca populations. The 11 oat genotypes with APR are desirable resistance sources for breeding programs developing durable crown rust resistant cultivars for eastern Canada.
Yellow endosperm sorghum contains carotenoids. The objective was to study the genetic basis of carotenoid variation by mapping QTL associated with carotenoid content and endosperm color.

Nineteen genes for anthocyanin biosynthesis were annotated in this genomic region. Gene Si1g06530 encodes a SANT/Myb domain protein and was highly expressed in developing panicles and seeds. It is a candidate gene for the brown hull color of Jigu 31.

The two materials show distinct accumulation patterns of secondary metabolites phenylpropanoids, including hydroxycinnamates and flavonoids. This may contribute to the improved drought resistance of the novel tartary buckwheat cultivars.

BC lines had moderate to high CLS and PM resistance compared to susceptible parent KING. BC line H3 had similar or superior agronomic traits to KING and higher yield (18.0-32.0%) under CLS and PM outbreaks.
SNP marker technologies and haplotype-based GWAS are applied methods for molecular breeding in barley. CRISPR-Cas9 technology uses genetic information for gene editing, as in the cv. Golden Promise.

The variable genes are enriched with environment responsive genes. 53% of genes display presence-absence variation. Some variable genes are predicted to be functionally associated with drought adaptation traits.

QTLs for biological yield, days to heading, and kernel weight on chromosome 2H; QTL for harvest index on 1H; QTLs for kernel weight on 2H, 4H, and 5H. For rainfall less than 450 mm, QTL for grain yield on 6H; QTLs for kernel weight on 2H and 5H; QTLs for seed per head on 1H and 6H; QTL for days to heading on 2H.

A study identified the Alkaline tolerance 1 (AT1) gene in sorghum. AT1 is associated with alkaline tolerance and encodes a G protein gamma-subunit. Editing AT1 enhances yields of sorghum, rice, maize, and millet in alkaline soils.

A high quality chromosome-scale genome assembly of little millet comprises 18 chromosomes and 59,045 genes. Eleven chromosomes are assembled from telomere to telomere, revealing an 850 Mb tetraploid genome.

Six sterile and six restorer lines of sorghum and 36 hybrid sorghum combinations from incomplete double-row crosses were tested. The root bleeding sap of the F1 hybrid sorghum showed high heterosis for soluble sugar content and amino acid content at flowering stage, with average high-parent heterosis of 129.34% and 74.57%, respectively.
A linkage map used 149 markers (RAPD, AFLP, ISSR, SSR, and 2 morphological markers) on 11 linkage groups (LGs). Analysis of variance for FT was significant across locations and years.

Under saline-alkali stress, SiDREB153 expression differed between resistant and susceptible materials. Under drought, saline-alkali, and shade stress, SiDREB80 expression increased. Under saline-alkali and high temperature stress, SiDREB4, SiDREB129, and SiDREB131 expression increased. Under herbicide and saline-alkali stress, SiDREB159 expression increased.

Lodging reduces grain yield in cereal crops. Stem height, diameter, and strength are crucial for lodging resistance, grain yield, and photosynthate transport in barley. Understanding the genetic basis of stem benefits barley breeding.
One CslF2 gene and 32 transcription factors were identified. Five modules were positively correlated with β-glucan accumulation.

The gene expression was higher in the high-resistant material than in the sensitive material under flooding stress. The quinoa C2H2 gene showed tissue specificity and was induced under flooding stress, indicating it plays a role in flooding stress in quinoa.
There are 20 distinct AsCAMTA genes. They are clustered into three subfamilies. Chromosomal mapping shows an uneven distribution across 10 chromosomes.

Maize-peanut intercropping enhanced carbon fixation and carboxylation in maize leaves compared to maize monocropping. In intercropping, apparent quantum yield (AQY), light-saturated net photosynthetic rate (LSPn), light saturation point (LSP), and light compensation point (LCP) increased by 11.6%, 9.4%, 8.9%, and 32.1%, respectively. Carboxylation efficiency (CE), CO2 saturation point (Cisat), Rubisco maximum carboxylation rate (Vcmax), maximum electron transfer rate (Jmax), and triose phosphate utilization rate (TPU) increased by 28.5%, 7.3%, 18.7%, 29.2%, and 17.0%, respectively. The CO2 compensation point (Γ) decreased by 22.6%.

The transgenic Arabidopsis seedlings had greater root length and mature plants had higher survival rates under drought conditions. The transgenic plants also had higher proline content and antioxidant activity compared to the control.

The quinoa cultivar Shelly seeds and seedlings were treated with NaCl solution. Physiological, biochemical and agronomic traits were investigated. The response patterns of three salt stress-responsive genes (SSRGs) were determined by real-time PCR.
Two statistical models, MLM and BLINK, identified 44 shared SNPs. Notable candidate genes include FPA for flowering time (VRADI10G01470; chr.

Salt or alkali treatment for 3 days inhibited plant growth and increased Na+ levels in the whole plant, leaves, sheath, and roots of both genotypes. Under alkali stress, K+ levels decreased in the whole plant, leaves, sheath, and roots of both genotypes. M-81E roots accumulated higher Na+ levels than leaves, while 314B leaves accumulated higher Na+ levels than roots.
Heterologous transformation of Arabidopsis with SbMYBHv33 produced a similar phenotype. Loss of function of the Arabidopsis homolog of SbMYBHv33 resulted in longer roots and increased salt tolerance.

Sorghum genes exhibit downregulation in response to drought stress. The review explores drought tolerance in sorghum, including its morphological characteristics, physiological mechanisms, and the identification and selection of its functional genes.

Sorghum is an important staple crop in arid regions. Sorghum has tolerance to abiotic stresses including drought, salt, alkaline, and heavy metal stressors, making it valuable research material for understanding molecular mechanisms and mining genes for genetic improvement of abiotic stress tolerance.

The expression levels of drought-associated genes were significantly increased in SbANK56 transgenic plants. Analysis of natural variation in SbANK56 revealed a significant positive correlation between SbANK56Hap4 and both its differential expression and drought stress tolerance.
A genetic map was constructed with 1065 markers and a total length of 1191.7 cM. 19 QTLs were detected for five traits: one QTL for CP, five for NDF, three for ADF, two for ADL, and eight for HC.

Totally 228 alleles were detected, with an average of 6.16 alleles per locus. The polymorphism information content (PIC) value for each locus ranged from 0.413 to 0.847, with an average of 0.697. A positive correlation was found between PIC and number of alleles and between PIC and number of repeat unit [0.802 and 0.429, respectively (P < 0.01)].
Pc98 is a seedling crown rust resistance gene from the wild oat Avena sterilis accession CAV 1979. It is effective at all oat growth stages. Virulence to Pc98 is low in tested Pca populations.

FtMYB30 overexpression under drought and salt stress reduced oxidative damage in transgenic plants by increasing proline content and antioxidant enzyme activity, and upregulated RD29A, RD29B, and Cu/ZnSOD expression, enhancing drought/salt tolerance. FtMYB30 overexpression also reduced ABA sensitivity in transgenic plants.

QTL were identified for all traits and co-located to five locations: SBI-01, SBI-03, SBI-05, SBI-06 and SBI-10. QTL alleles from R9188 increased sucrose content and sugar content on SBI-01, SBI-05 and SBI-06.

2019 and 736 differentially expressed genes were obtained under salt stress in salt-sensitive and salt-tolerant accessions, respectively. The transcription factor families bHLH, WRKY, AP2/ERF, and MYB-MYC play critical roles in foxtail millet's response to salt stress.

FtNAC31 overexpression lines had sharply increased superoxide dismutase, peroxidase, and catalase activities under stresses. The expression of RD29A, RD29B, RD22, DREB2B, NCED3, and POD1 was dramatically upregulated.
QTL for DON were found on chromosomes 5C, 9D, 13A, 14D and unknown_3. A QTL for FHB was found on 11A. Several DON/FHB QTL coincided with QTL for DH, DM and/or PH.

entities.text: QTL for DH, DM and/or PH
entities.label: QTL

relations.head: QTL for DH, DM and/or PH
relations.label: LOI

text: A linkage map was constructed using QTL cartographer with 24 polymorphic SSR markers for 96 population lines. Seven linkage groups (LGs) were generated. Five LGs remained unlinked at position 0. LG1 and LG7 were grouped.

Simple sequence repeat markers showed high-quality polymorphism between Fulghum and Norline. They were examined in 128 recombinant inbred lines. Sixty-five new SSR markers, four SNP markers, and one CAPS marker were added to the Fulghum x Norline linkage map.
The transcriptome profiles of two salt tolerance cultivars were compared under 0.8% NaCl treatment for 0, 48, and 72 hours.

SSR markers and agronomical traits analyzed genetic diversity of 142 sweet sorghum parent lines. The lines were clustered into 5 groups based on agronomical traits. Each group is characterized by morphological traits and main origins.
2326 transcripts contained at least one SSR, with a frequency of one SSR per 1.17 kb. Mono-nucleotide repeats were the most abundant type (45.48%), followed by tri-nucleotide repeats (35.17%).
SiNF-YC2 interacted with SiCO in the nucleus. SiNF-YC2 promotes flowering and improves resistance to salt stress.

Pc39 is a major race-specific crown rust resistance gene found in an Israeli accession of the wild hexaploid Avena sterilis. Its effectiveness in Europe has decreased but remains relatively high. Molecular markers would aid its mapping and deployment in breeding programs.

Many cultivated soybeans with yellow seed coats lack PAs or anthocyanins. Some soybean cultivars have coloured seed coats that contain these compounds. We analyse the transcriptional control of PA and anthocyanin biosynthesis in soybean.

The study identified the quinoa trihelix family and families associated with salt stress, including Na+/H+ antiporter (NHX) and calcineurin B-like (CBL). Expression analysis identified Cqtrihelix23 as responsive to salt stress. Transient transformation experiments revealed that Cqtrihelix23 enhanced quinoa's tolerance to salt stress by promoting root development, maintaining the antioxidant system, and reducing the Na+/K+ ratio.
Anthocyanin is a natural pigment with functional roles in plants. Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) is a nutritional crop.

We identified 160 MTAs and 44 durable QTL for nine traits. Each explained 3.7%-43.9% of the variation. Haplotypes from the three most predictive QTL per trait increased the variation explained to 16%-46%.

Integrative breeding targets early maturity, high harvest index, and water use efficiency (WUE) to improve sorghum productivity and mitigate drought effects. These traits guide sorghum improvement for drought tolerance.
The genetic map was constructed with 85 markers (SSRs, AFLPs, STSs, and Dhn genes) using the MULTIPOINT algorithm. QTL mapping was conducted with the MULTIQTL package.

T2 transgenic 35S:VrNHX1 lines maintained a higher K+/Na+ ratio in aerial parts under salt stress and accumulated higher [Na+] in roots than wild-type. They had lower lipid peroxidation, hydrogen peroxide and oxygen radical production, but higher relative water content and proline, ascorbate and chlorophyll contents.

SbMYBAS1 (SbMYB119) expression decreased under salt stress. SbMYBAS1 is located in the nucleus. Under salt stress, Arabidopsis plants overexpressing SbMYBAS1 had lower dry/fresh weight and chlorophyll content, and higher membrane permeability, MDA content, and Na+/K+ ratio than wild-type Arabidopsis plants.

Approximately 60% of the yield potential in foxtail millet breeding over 60 years is attributed to changes in varieties, and approximately 40% to improvements in cultivation measures. The genetic gains in grain yield were 35.79 kg ha-1 year-1 in 2023 and 29.29 kg ha-1 year-1 in 2024. The genetic gain in yield potential was 0.96% YOR-1.
Under terminal stress, a QTL for TKW was linked or pleiotropic to DH and DM. This link suggests early maturity favors post-anthesis grain growth, increasing grain size and yield.

11 flag leaf width QTLs were identified on chromosomes 3, 5, and 6 using a high-density genetic map with 3795 Bin markers. qFLW5-2 was a major QTL, detected across 3 environments and explaining a large proportion of FLW variation.
An EMS-mutagenized population was created in the barley landrace TX9425. A TILLING population of 2000 M-2 lines was constructed using CEL I enzyme and polyacrylamide electrophoresis.
The QTL QSc/Sl.cib-7H has strong effects on spike length (SL) and spike compactness (SC). This was validated in two pairs of near isogenic lines (NILs) and diverse genetic backgrounds.

The study assessed the impact of salt concentrations (0, 50, 100, 150, and 200 mmol L-1) on agronomic traits, photosynthetic characteristics, physiological and biochemical properties, and leaf anatomical structures in covered oat cultivars and naked oat cultivars. The measured parameters indicate the salt tolerance level in Avena sativa and Avena nuda, which is influenced by salt concentration and cultivar.
High salinity inhibited germination more at 10-20°C for both species. Alkali stress caused more stress at 25-35°C even at low concentration.
312 significant MTAs were identified, with 70 MTAs for 100-seed weight. 18 SNPs from 5 genes (ERECTA, ASR, DREB, CAP2 promoter, AMDH) were associated with traits.
Identified MTAs have a pyramiding effect important for breeding. Desirable alleles and superior genotypes are valuable for foxtail millet improvement through marker-assisted selection.

Composite interval mapping was used for QTL analysis with a framework map of 272 molecular markers. Four QTLs, BYDq1, BYDq2, BYDq3 and BYDq4, for BYD tolerance were identified on linkage groups OM1, 5, 7 and 24, respectively. All except BYDq2 were consistently detected across both years.

Some domestication- and improvement-related genes involved in stress resistance lost functions or reduced expression levels due to trade-offs between stress resistance and productivity. This study provided novel genetic materials for molecular breeding of broomcorn millet varieties with improved agronomic traits.

MAPK signaling and oxidative metabolism were key pathways in quinoa under cold stress. Enhanced activities of antioxidant enzymes alleviate lipid peroxidation of membranes and promote accumulation of osmotic adjustment substances, enabling seeds to resist oxidative damage under cold stress.

The agronomic performance and chromosome behavior of autotetraploid sorghum inbreds and F1 hybrids were investigated. Autotetraploids have commercial use and can serve as bridging species in crosses to natural tetraploids.

117 candidate genes were mapped to these QTNs. They are associated with six major traits: 20 for flowering time, 16 for maturity, 16 for plant height, 17 for seed number per plant, 38 for seed yield, and 10 for seed weight. Key genes include Sobic.001G196700 for flowering time and Sobic.005G176100 for stress responses. Two important regulatory genes, SbMADS1 and SbFT, have roles in flowering regulation.
Eight loci were mapped: one each on chromosomes 1, 2, 4, and 6, and two on chromosomes 5 and 9. All loci contained one to three candidate genes.

146 SNPs associated with drought tolerance were detected. 26 candidate loci associated with multiple traits were selected. 215 candidate genes were identified at these loci, including eleven transcription factor genes, seven protein kinase genes, and other protein coding genes that may respond to drought stress.

164 Recombinant inbreed lines were created from a cross between Longgu7 and Yugu1. 1,047,978 SNPs were identified between the parents via resequencing. 3413 bin markers developed from SNPs were used to construct a binary map, containing 3963 recombinant breakpoints and totaling 1222.26 cM with an average distance of 0.36 cM between adjacent markers.

Salt stress upregulated and downregulated transcripts involved in metabolic functions. At 1 and 7 h, most transcripts were highly upregulated compared to 3 h. Commonly enriched KEGG pathways included the MAPK signaling pathway and peroxisome pathway.

Two genes, Seita.5G251300 and Seita.8G036300, exhibit drought-tolerant haplotypes. These haplotypes have an advantage in 1000 grain weight and main panicle grain weight, without penalty in grain weight per plant.

Colocation analysis associated these QTNs with known stay-green QTLs. 47 putative genes near these QTNs potentially influenced drought tolerance traits. Accession selection should consider multiple indices.
In Tartary buckwheat, 12 ABA receptors were identified, designated as FtRCAR1 through FtRCAR12. FtRCARs are divided into three subfamily.

Drought causes yield loss in sorghum. This study aimed to identify drought-tolerant sorghum lines and genomic regions using 216 sorghum accessions in stressed and non-stressed environments at two locations.
The 'Longli-4' variety was treated with ethyl methanesulfonate (EMS) at 0.8% for 8 h.

Two loci were on chromosome 1, two on chromosome 2, two on chromosome 6, one on chromosome 8, and one on chromosome 10. The loci Pm 2-1 and Pm 2-2 on chromosome 2 were detected in 7 and 5 of 11 environments, respectively.
ABA upregulated CHS, CHI, F3'H, F3H, and FLS transcript levels, while downregulating 4CL and PAL. Transcription factors mediating these genes were also regulated by ABA.

A major QTL with 13.75% contribution rate is located on barley chromosome 7H (GBM1303-GMS056). A minor QTL with 6.01% contribution rate is located on chromosome 2H (Scssr03381-Scssr07759). It comes from the high-value parent ZGMLEL.

A recombinant inbred line (RIL) population of 333 foxtail millet lines was grown in 13 trials. Quantitative trait loci (QTL) controlling nine agronomic traits for panicle architecture and grain yield were identified. Panicle weight, grain weight per panicle, panicle length, panicle diameter, and panicle exsertion length varied across locations.

One to five QTLs were detected for kernel morphology traits and groat percentage, explaining 7.0-60.7% of phenotypic variance. A QTL for kernel length in each population and a QTL for kernel width in one population colocated with a QTL for groat percentage, indicating kernel size or shape influences groat percentage.

An F-2 population was derived from a cross between the grain sorghum variety SA2313 and the Sudan-grass variety Hiro-1. Seven QTLs for 100-grain weight were detected. One QTL, qGW1, was consistently detected over 2 years and contributed 20 to 40% of the phenotypic variation.
Most PgB3 genes show upregulated expression under drought and high-temperature stresses. The RAV subfamily gene PgRAV-04 was cloned and overexpressed in tobacco.

13 DEGs and 16 DEGs occurred with differential alternative splicing under DR1 and DR2 conditions, respectively. This work provides a resource on drought-responsive genes and contributes to drought tolerance improvement in sorghum.

QTLs for kernel area, kernel length, kernel width, and groat percentage were mapped in two populations of 137 recombinant inbred lines using RFLP and AFLP analysis to evaluate marker-assisted selection (MAS). Phenotypic correlations between kernel morphology traits and groat percentage were not significant.
No QTL were detected for CGR. Two environmentally consistent QTL were found for P-R. One environmentally consistent QTL was found for E-G.
SiNP1 encodes a glucose-methanol-choline oxidoreductase. The SiNP1 protein localizes to the endoplasmic reticulum and is expressed in panicle.

After genome-wide SLAF marker analysis, 12 QTLs for 7 traits were located on five chromosomes. QTLs for plant height (qPH1, qPH2, qPH3) were detected on Chr 6 and Chr 9, with PVE of 27.13-29.02%. Two QTLs for fresh stem weight (qFSW1, qFSW2) were detected on Chr 6 and Chr 9, explaining 27.10% and 16.08% of phenotypic variation, respectively.

Digital RNA sequence analysis indicated that ST 47 maintained better Na+ and K+ homeostasis by coordinated regulation of transporter genes. The abundance of transcripts involved in chlorophyll metabolism and photosynthesis pathways was higher in ST 47 than in SS 212, which contributed to biomass accumulation. ST 47 resists Na+ toxicity via coordinated physiological, morphological, and molecular mechanisms.
Anthracnose, caused by Colletotrichum sublineolum, is a destructive disease of sorghum (Sorghum bicolor). The most effective control strategy is incorporating resistance genes.

Genetic and expression analyses in two inbred populations examined the genetic control of domestication-related differences in flowering time and growth habit between domesticated chickpea and its wild progenitor Cicer reticulatum. A major QTL for flowering time under short-day conditions, DTF3A, was mapped to a 59-gene interval on chromosome three. This interval contains a cluster of three FT genes, which showed upregulated expression in domesticated parent lines relative to wild parent lines.
Genotype A. Sacaca exhibited the highest saponins content (3.84 mg.g-1) and the highest amounts of K, Ca, P, and Fe.
Five markers were mapped into three linkage groups on the oat consensus map. Genomic regions for heading date and plant height in oat are conserved in Oryza sativa and Brachypodium distachyon.
Genetic analysis identified molecular markers, genomic regions and genes associated with barley traits. SNP marker technologies and haplotype-based GWAS support molecular breeding in barley.

Salt-tolerant cultivars maintained higher growth rate, leaf cell membrane integrity, and osmotic adjustment capability under saline condition by enhancing antioxidant enzyme activities and accumulating osmotic regulators. Compared to salt-sensitive cultivars, salt-tolerant cultivars reduced root Na+ uptake by decreasing AsAKT1 and AsHKT2;1 expression, excluded more root Na+ by increasing AsSOS1 expression, compartmentalized more Na+ into root vacuoles by increasing AsNHX1 and AsVATP-P1 expression, and absorbed more K+ by increasing AsKUP1 expression.

The ASM is a major QTL for dehydration tolerance, contributing to 27.18% of the variation in lipid peroxidation among 170 accessions. This ASM can be used for marker-aided breeding and allele-mining for dehydration tolerance in foxtail millet.

The drought-resistant oat variety DA92-2F6 and the drought-susceptible variety Longyan 3 were investigated under 15% PEG-6000 drought stress. After 7 days of stress, antioxidant enzyme, osmotic adjustment substance, and photosynthetic efficiency were significantly higher in DA92-2F6, except for cell membrane permeability.

MeJA up-regulated the expression of key genes PuAAT, PuADH3, PuADH5, PuADH9, PuLOX1, and PuLOX3 in the LOX pathway and transcription factors PuMYB21-like, PuMYB108-like, PuWRKY61, PuWRKY72, and PuWRKY31. MeJA may prevent the decline in ester biosynthesis, aroma, and quality of cold-stored 'Nanguo' pears.
Two oat cultivars, Baiyan7 (tolerant) and Yizhangyan4 (sensitive), were characterized under saline-alkali stress. Baiyan7 showed better adaptability to saline-alkali stress than Yizhangyan4.

Cluster analysis classified 524 oat germplasm resources into five groups. 174 oat germplasm resources with saline-alkali tolerance at the germination stage were screened. A regression equation for identifying germinated saline-alkali-tolerant oat germplasm resources under the same conditions was established.

QTL mapping for main stem length detected four QTL. The system processed 178 samples from DNA extraction to genotyping in 37 days, enabling genotyping of genome-wide markers for hundreds of buckwheat plants before maturity.

This study constructed recombinant inbred lines (RILs) using cultivated species and wild buckwheat. 84,521 SNP markers were identified by Genotyping-by-Sequencing (GBS). High-resolution, high-density SNP genetic maps were developed for QTL mapping, gene cloning and comparative mapping of buckwheat.

A total of 40,901 SSR loci were identified in the TB genome, with a mean distance of 11 kb. Based on these loci, 8,089 pairs of SSR primers were designed. 101 primer pairs for polymorphic SSR loci were used to genotype 97 core germplasm resources.

Drought stress influenced all traits. The varieties IESH, GD, and MKL exhibited distinct responses. Based on grain yield under drought, IESH had superior potential, followed by GD, and then MKL. Drought reduced transpiration rates: MKL by 40%, GD by 46%, and IESH by 20%. Drought reduced stomatal conductance: MKL by 31%, GD by 39%, and IESH by 21%.

SbER10_X1 exhibits abundant transcript in a few sorghum varieties grown in mountainous areas with strong light, heat, and water supply. Its expression is significantly and positively correlated with plant biomass in 32 sorghum germplasm resources.

The QTLs for frost survival and plant emergence under cold sowing conditions do not overlap. Genome-wide association studies identified four significant associations for frost survival. These QTLs are located on chromosomes Sb02, Sb07, and Sb08.

The study identified the ZF-HD family in quinoa and a drought-responsive CqZF-HD14 gene. Transient overexpression of CqZF-HD14 promotes photosynthetic pigment accumulation under drought stress, strengthens the antioxidant system, and enhances drought tolerance.

Key genes TaMAX1, TaMOC1, and TN1 in wheat, ZmTB1, ZmD14, and ZmMOC1 in maize, and MAX1-like genes, OsMAX1, and OsHAM2 in rice are highlighted. In sugarcane, genes SoMAX2, SoMAX3, SoMAX4-1, SoMAX4-2, and SoTB1 regulate bud outgrowth and tillering.

Nine QTLs were identified for SDW_70, with the highest PVE of 18.4%. Four of these QTLs also governed SLA_70, with the highest PVE of 15.7%. Some were involved in epistatic interactions and formed multiple QTL mapping models.

Principal component analysis transformed 21 conventional traits into seven independent indexes. Regression analysis selected these indicators: second leaf from the top surface area, peroxidase activity, catalase activity, malondialdehyde content, chlorophyll (a + b), grain weight per ear, and yield.

entities.label: TRT

text: High and low temperatures decrease seed germination under alkali stress for perennial ryegrass. Recovery percentage was highest at 400 mM salinity and 25 mM alkalinity under 10-20°C. Recovery percentage was lower under 25-35°C for ryegrass.

180 drought-inducible transcription factors were identified in common buckwheat cotyledons and roots. The analysis identified drought responsive DEGs and studied molecular mechanisms regulating drought stress response.
The BBCH scale and its numerical code are adapted to describe morphological development stages in M. × giganteus, using observations of the 'Freedom' and 'Illinois' clones in Iowa, USA.
Five AFLP markers were linked to the sht1 locus. Two markers, e54m58/610 and e55m46/320, cosegregated with the sht1 locus without recombination.
Salinity did not affect the leaf and root dry weight of Baiyan2. The leaf and root dry weight of Mengnongda1 were reduced by 42% and 35%, respectively.
The candidate gene VrFRO8 was identified by comparative genomics, transcriptome and RT-qPCR analysis. Its expression was significantly up-regulated after salt treatment.

SLAF-seq with bulked segregant analysis (BSA) was used to fine map the Blp1 gene in an F2 population from the cross Hatiexi No. 1 x Zhe5819. Based on SNP screening criteria, 77,542 polymorphic SNPs were obtained for association analysis.

text: Quantitative real-time PCR analysis showed SiDREB2 was significantly up-regulated by dehydration (polyethylene glycol) and salinity (NaCl). A synonymous SNP associated with dehydration tolerance was detected at the 558th base pair (an A/G transition) in the SiDREB2 gene in a core set of 45 foxtail millet accessions.
The grains of 'Longli-4' were treated with ethyl methanesulfonate (EMS) at 0.8% for 8 h. Nine plants from M2 families were selected to investigate the EMS mutagenesis effect.

45 barley genes or orthologs were identified as candidate genes for barley grain size and weight. 12, 20, 9, and 4 genes or orthologs were for barley, rice, maize, and wheat, respectively. 20 of them were located in 14 QTL hotspot regions on chromosomes 1H, 2H, 3H, 5H, and 7H, which control barley grain size and weight.

Early maturity is an important agronomic trait. It allows multiple cropping and utilizes light and temperature resources in alpine regions. This avoids damage from low temperatures and early frost. Genes determining flowering affect flowering time, which directly affects crop maturity and yield.

Transcriptome data before and after drought stress in foxtail millet hybrid Zhangza 19 and its parents identified 5597 DEGs. WGCNA screened 607 drought-tolerant genes. Heterotic genes were screened based on expression level.
Two multi-effect QTLs, Mqtl5-2 and Mqtl2-5, underlie panicle development and yield. One QTL, Mqtl1-2, is related to plant growth and development. Candidate genes for five major QTLs were discussed.
Two novel QTL clusters controlled NUE traits at seedling and maturity stages. Genes related to NUE traits were predicted in the major QTL intervals.

Tol showed important changes in metabolic pathways during heat shock (GST). These included up-regulation of raffinose family oligosaccharides and down-regulation of the gamma-aminobutyric acid catalytic pathway. Hexose sugar concentration became depleted. Tol tolerated elevated temperatures (ABS) due to up-regulation of osmoprotectants, chaperones, and reactive oxygen species scavengers and by suppression of SnRK1 (GENE) via transcripts and metabolites.

In the germination stage, SbNAC56 overexpression transgenic lines exhibited enhanced hypersensitivities to ABA, NaCl and d-Mannitol. This indicates SbNAC56 plays roles in plant response to abiotic stresses in an ABA dependent signaling pathway.

A genome-wide association study (GWAS) and a candidate gene-based association analysis were conducted on 59 cultivated and 99 Tibetan wild barley genotypes to identify molecular markers associated with grain protein content (GPC). Tibetan wild barley had higher GPC than cultivated barley. GPC was significantly correlated with diastatic power (DP) and malt extract, confirming its importance for malt quality.
The study mapped QTLs for resistance to B. fusca and C. partellus in sorghum. It used 243 F-9:10 sorghum RILs from ICSV 745 and PB 15520-1 with 4,955 SNP markers.

Chromosomes 1, 2, 3, 4, and 6 harbored SNPs significant for Striga tolerance in sorghum. Significant SNPs were found for plant height (4), panicle height (3), leaves per plant (2), foliar fresh grain weight (8), dry grain weight (2), and chlorophyll content (3).

SNPs from auxin-related genes, including auxin efflux carrier protein (PIN3), p-glycoprotein, and nodulin MtN21/EamA-like transporter, were associated with yield and yield-related traits under drought. Four genetic regions contained SNPs associated with several different traits, indicating pleiotropic effects.

A recombinant inbred line (RIL) population of 189 individuals was derived from a cross between the sweet sorghum cultivar 'Rio' and the grain sorghum 'BTx623'. Plant height, Brix, 100-grain weight, and flowering time were evaluated over 3 years.
Six PCR-based markers were developed to identify the resistant Pc39 allele. The closely linked marker SCAR_3456624 is within 0.37 cM of Pc39.

The Sdwa5.1.1+ was confirmed in an F-2:3 population from the same cross and mapped to a 3.298-Kb region containing the gene LOC106767068, designated as VrKNAT7-1. This gene encodes the transcription factor KNOTTED ARABIDOPSIS THALIANA7 (KNAT7), a KNOX II protein. VrKNAT7 sequence alignment between ACC41 and KPS2 revealed polymorphisms in the coding, untranslated, and promoter regions.

Field selection using pathogen and artificial inoculation is time-consuming. We used 105 F-3 lines from a cross between BSR-resistant 'Syumari' and susceptible 'Buchishoryukei-1' for BSR inoculation tests. AFLP analyses with 1024 primer sets showed six fragments were polymorphic between resistant and susceptible bulked groups.

Loss-of-function mutations in CCD7, CCD8, MAX1, and DUF genes downregulated expression of associated genes in the SL biosynthetic pathway. Edited lines showed phenotypic changes compared to wild-type plants.
Abiotic stress impacts sorghum growth and yield. Phospholipase D hydrolyzes phospholipids and responds to abiotic stresses. The Phospholipase D (SbPLD) family in sorghum is rarely reported.
Qing1 and Long4 yields were 27.25% and 21.42% higher than the control. Both varieties had higher average proline, CAT and soluble sugar contents than the control.

Subsequent experiments identified 38 QTLs controlling variation in height, flowering, biomass, leaf area, greenness and stomatal density. Colocalisation of these QTLs with agronomic traits indicates they can be used for improving sorghum performance through marker assisted selection under preflowering drought stress.

Genes associated with abscisic acid, gibberellic acid, brassinosteroid, and auxin signaling are involved in seed germination. GWAS of the 24-h germination rate across 232 cultivars identified four significant SNPs and 31 candidate genes. SbPP2C33 is the top candidate based on transcriptome integration.
Sorghum is a widely grown cereal crop. It is adapted to different climates and bred for multiple purposes, resulting in phenotypic diversity.

Three QTL, QLB-czas1, QLB-czas2, and QLB-czas8, were detected on chromosomes 1, 2, and 8 of Yugu 5. QLB-czas1 explained 14-17% of phenotypic variation in 2 environments. QLB-czas2 explained 9% in 5 environments. QLB-czas8 explained 12-20% in 6 environments. Bulked segregant analysis and RNA sequencing identified common SNPs in the genomic region of QLB-czas8.

The Shinanotsubuhime allele for qDTH2 caused late heading. The Yuikogane allele for qDTH7 led to extremely late heading. Allelic differences in qDTH2 and qDTH7 determine regional adaptability in S. italica.

Groundnut (Arachis hypogaea L.) is a crop grown in semi-arid tropics where drought constrains productivity. To understand the genetic basis and identify QTL for drought tolerance, two RIL populations ICGS 76 x CSMG 84-1 (RIL-2) and ICGS 44 x ICGS 76 (RIL-3) were used.

CcCIPK14 was used as bait in a yeast two-hybrid screen. CcCBL1 was identified as an interactor. In vitro, CcCIPK14 exhibited autophosphorylation and phosphorylation of CcCBL1. Plants overexpressing CcCBL1 showed higher survival rates under drought stress. They also exhibited higher expression of flavonoid biosynthetic genes and higher flavonoid content.
Edited lines showed reduced SL production compared to wild-type plants. Edited lines with lower SL production had delayed or reduced Striga emergence rates.

Higher abundance of heat shock protein transcripts and metabolites related to heat tolerance were noted for Tol compared to Sen both before and during heat shock. This is attributed to constitutive and inducible responses to elevated temperatures. Important changes in metabolic pathways were identified for Tol during heat shock, including up-regulation of raffinose family oligosaccharides and down-regulation of the gamma-aminobutyric acid catalytic pathway, while hexose sugar concentration became depleted.

The sorghum mini core (MC) collection was evaluated for days to 50% flowering (DF), biomass (BM), plant height (PH), soluble solid content (SSC) and juice weight (JW). The sorghum reference set (RS) was evaluated for DF and PH. Association mapping used 6,094,317 SNP markers in the MC and 265,500 SNPs in the RS. In the MC panel, three QTLs for DF, one QTL for PH, one QTL for BM, and two QTLs for JW were identified. In the RS panel, a PH QTL on chromosome 6 was identified. This QTL was also associated with DF, BM, JW, and SSC in the MC panel.

Several MTAs were identified for SA-related traits across the genome. Common markers on SBI-08 and SBI-10 were associated with aphid count and plant damage. Loci for reflectance-based traits were on SBI-02, SBI-03, and SBI-05. Candidate genes encoding leucine-rich repeats (LRR), Avr proteins, lipoxygenases (LOXs), calmodulin-dependent protein kinase, WRKY transcription factors, flavonoid biosynthesis genes, and 12-oxo-phytodienoic acid reductase were identified near SNPs significantly associated with different SA traits.
A QTL model controls tillering in sorghum. Tillering in sorghum is associated with the carbon supply-demand balance or an intrinsic propensity to tiller.
SiPSY1 transcription in 15 DAP immature grains determined YPC in mature seeds. Selection of SiPSY1 during foxtail millet domestication increased YPC in mature grains.

18 QTLs were identified for plant height (4 QTLs), fresh weight (8 QTLs), dry weight (3 QTLs), stem diameter (2 QTLs), and sugar content (1 QTL). Two overlapping QTLs, qFW9.2/qDW9 and qFW10/qDW10.2, were detected for fresh weight and dry weight.

A genome-wide association study (GWAS) was performed for ten agronomic traits in 142 foxtail millet genotypes using 10 K SNPs. SNPs on chromosome 5 numbered 844 and on chromosome 8 numbered 2153, with an average frequency of 25.9 per Mb.
Autotetraploid F1 hybrids had higher seed set than autotetraploid parental inbred lines. Quadrivalents, anaphase I and II laggards, and quartet micronuclei frequency varied among autotetraploids.

Sheepgrass adapts to adverse environments including cold, salinity, alkalinity and drought. It can survive with soil moisture below 6% in dry seasons. Little is known about sheepgrass water stress tolerance at the molecular level.
Three QTLs and nine candidate genes for plant height were obtained on chromosomes I and IX. Metabolism and MAPK signaling play important roles in foxtail millet plant height formation.
Cluster analysis separated cultivated and wild genotypes. Transferability showed 97% SSR markers were transferable to eight other Vigna species.

Sixty quinoa C2H2-type zinc finger protein transcription factor family members were identified from the quinoa genome database. They are divided into five subfamilies. Members of different subfamilies have varying numbers of zinc finger structural domains and differences in gene structure and sequence features.
The ornithine delta-aminotransferase gene OsOAT confers multi-stress tolerance in rice (Oryza sativa). OsOAT is a direct target of the stress-responsive NAC transcription factor SNAC2.

Bioinformatics analysis revealed 186 DEPs related to 37 specific KEGG pathways. Physiological, transcriptome, and proteome analyses demonstrated that maize-peanut intercropping improves photosynthetic characteristics in maize leaves. This improvement is related to PS I, PS II, cytochrome b6f complex, ATP synthase, and photosynthetic CO<sub>2</sub> fixation, caused by improved CO<sub>2</sub> carboxylation efficiency.

A panel of 219 sorghum accessions from West and Central Africa was evaluated for photoperiod response index (PRI) under field conditions. The accessions were genotyped for SNPs in six genes involved in photoperiodic control of flowering time.

Proso millet (Panicum miliaceum) is a crop with nutritional qualities and tolerance to drought stress and soil infertility. Genetic diversity studies are limited due to lack of efficient genetic markers.
13,920 were up-regulated and 12,808 down-regulated in faba bean drought-stressed leaves. 2130 transcription factors in abscisic acid-dependent and independent signaling pathways were identified.

Linkage analysis identified 21 QTL for GPC and GFC across six environments, including six major stable QTL, 72 combined QTL, and 54 epistatic QTL pairs. Association analysis showed significant differences in GPC or GFC among RILs with different genotypes for the six major stable QTL.

Compared to WT, transgenic Arabidopsis overexpressing FtMYB13 had lower ABA sensitivity and improved drought/salt tolerance. This was attributed to higher proline content, greater photosynthetic efficiency, higher transcript abundance of stress-related genes, and smaller amounts of ROS and MDA in transgenic lines. FtMYB13 is involved in mediating plant responses to ABA, salt, and drought.

A QTL-seq analysis was conducted to identify genomic regions regulating days to heading (DTH). The analysis used an F2 population derived from crosses between the middle-heading cultivar Shinanotsubuhime and the early heading cultivar Yuikogane. Under field conditions, transgressive segregation of DTH toward late heading was observed in the F2 population.

text: Genome-wide association analysis detected four markers highly associated with hullessness. Three were mapped on linkage group Mrg21 between 195.7 and 212.1 cM, indicating the dominantN1 locus on Mrg21 is the major factor controlling this trait.

A major effect marker (Chr1_61095994) explained the highest variance proportion (R-2 = 27-31%) in most evaluated traits. It was in linkage disequilibrium with a hotspot of 19 putative glutathione S-transferase genes. On chromosome four, a hotspot region involved major effect markers linked with putative MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex genes.

Elevated expression of Chalk5 increases endosperm chalkiness. Two consensus nucleotide polymorphisms in the Chalk5 promoter in rice varieties might partly account for differences in Chalk5 mRNA levels that contribute to natural variation in grain chalkiness.
VfAP2-1 and VfERF-99 enhance pathogen resistance. They positively influence ROS homeostasis, accumulating H2O2 and O2- under abiotic and biotic stresses, inhibiting pathogen colonization.

The Ethiopian sorghum germplasm has extensive genetic diversity across traits. The genetic basis of seedling traits is unexplored. Understanding this is vital for developing drought-resistant sorghum cultivars.

Under drought stress, the maximal photochemical efficiency of PSII (F-v/F-m) and oxidoreductive activity (Delta I/I-0) decreased more in Roma than in M-81E. The Delta I/I0 decreased more markedly than Fv/Fm, indicating PSI was more sensitive to drought stress than PSII.

A two-year experiment under acidic conditions measured 12 yield-related traits in the F2 and F3 populations of the cross E232 x Murasakimochi and its reciprocal cross. The phenotypic coefficient of variation was high.

This study mapped QTLs for agronomic and yield traits using three connected mapping populations of different genetic backgrounds in Ethiopian sorghum germplasm. The three bi-parental populations, each with 207 F 2:3 lines, were evaluated under two moisture stress environments.

Transcriptomic analysis revealed enriched metabolic pathways in L4 seeds: flavonoid biosynthesis, TCA cycle, and terpenoid backbone biosynthesis. L1 seeds showed enriched carbon metabolism. Key genes involved include CHS, CHI, AACT, ENO1, IDH, NADP-ME, and HAO2L. Aging may adversely affect flavonoids and terpenoids, contributing to vigor loss in storage-sensitive seeds. Storage-tolerant seeds maintain carbon metabolism and energy supply.

The best location for planting strip-tilled fertilized corn varies with soil and climatic conditions and the time between fertilizer application and planting. With fewer days, planting directly on the center of the fertilized strip-till decreased plant population and grain yield.

145 VfAP2/ERFs were identified and are unevenly distributed across six chromosomes. Phylogenetic analysis classified them into five subgroups. Cis-elements analysis found VfAP2/ERF promoters contain elements related to light response, plant hormone, abiotic stress response, and plant growth and development.

SG QTL were mapped near previously reported chromosomal regions. Two heat-stress QTL were mapped to bPb-5529 on 5H, adjacent to QTL for root length and root-shoot ratio. SG QTL detection in barley under heat- and water-stressed conditions enables high throughput screening for these traits.

IP-MS and transcriptome analysis revealed that SiUBC39 is involved in growth and development regulation, drought stress response, and immune response. SiPIP2;1 and SiEhd2 were identified as interactors of SiUBC39, explaining its roles in blast resistance and flowering time control. Domestication analysis identified an A/G mutation in the SiUBC39 promoter TATA box, distinguishing domesticated and wild haplotypes and highlighting its role in domestication selection.

Phylogenetic analysis assigned eight FtNAC TFs to three abiotic stress-related subgroups. The relative expression levels of FtNAC genes were analyzed under abiotic stresses (salt, drought, cold, ultraviolet B) and exogenous phytohormones (abscisic acid, methyl jasmonate, salicylic acid).

The Sd3 allele increased enzyme thermostability and enhanced diastatic power, a malting quality trait. The BC1F1 individuals impacted the agronomic and quality traits of the final progenies, showing the importance of screening at the early backcrossing stage in MABC.

Multiple SSR markers, including SXAU8002 for 100-grain weight and SXAU8006 for stem diameter, were associated with agronomic traits. A candidate gene, FtPinG0007685500, was identified that may affect node number and stem diameter by participating in lignin synthesis.

LCR4 exhibited robust resistance against stripe rust infection at both the seedling stage and the adult stage upon inoculation with Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici (Pst) race V26 and mixed Pst races, respectively. Genetic analysis elucidated that the translocated 2RL chromosome segment is responsible for this resistance.

text: Sorghum bicolor is a tolerant cereal crop. C2 domain family proteins are involved in the response to abiotic stress such as salt and drought stress. Less information on C2 domain family members has been reported in Sorghum bicolor.

NM5 showed a delay in the transcriptional responses of the ROS scavenging system to simulated drought treatment and an easy recovery of photosynthesis-associated gene expression. Compared to JS6, NM5 exhibited different regulation strategies in the jasmonic acid (JA) signal transduction pathway.

The genes in the ornithine pathway (Orn-OAT-P5C/GSA-P5CR-Pro) were up-regulated. The genes in the glutamate pathway (Glu-P5CS-P5C/GSA-P5CR-Pro) were also up-regulated. AtOAT expression enhanced wheat abiotic tolerance. It modified proline biosynthesis by up-regulating proline biosynthesis-associated genes and down-regulating the proline catabolic gene under stress.

An F5 population from a cross between low juice yielding Xinliang52 (XL52) and high juice yielding W455 lines was used for QTL analysis of stem juice yield. A QTL controlling stem juice yield was mapped with SSR marker Xtxp97, explaining 46.7% of phenotypic variance.

The genetic diversity and population structure of hulless oat were investigated using genotyping-by-sequencing on 805 oat lines, including 186 hulless oats. Population structure analyses showed strong genetic differentiation between hulless landraces and other oat lines, including modern hulless cultivars.

An EMS-induced foxtail millet population of about 15,000 M-1 lines was established. 1353 independent M-2 lines with abnormal phenotypes in leaf color, plant morphology, and panicle shape were identified.

The segregation ratio matched the expected ratio in the F-2 population of the two crosses. Four maintainer lines/CMS line pairs (F-5/BC3) with high stalk juice and stalk juice sugar contents were developed.

The seed-setting rate was positively correlated with grain number. Grain number was correlated with flower number at the same plant position. Grains number on branches was the most dominant component of the seed-setting rate (Path coefficient = 2.19). Grains number on stem was the next component (Path coefficient = 0.60).

SSR molecular markers were developed and characterized for mung bean. They were used to analyze genetic diversity. A genomic library containing microsatellite loci was isolated, cloned, and sequenced from the mung bean variety 'MCV-1'.

SiCBL5, SiCIPK24, and SiSOS1 co-overexpression in yeast conferred salt tolerance. Under salt stress, SiCBL5 overexpressors had lower Na+ accumulation and stronger Na+ efflux than wild-type plants, while RNAi-SiCBL5 plants had higher Na+ accumulation and weaker Na+ efflux.

Gene Ontology (GO), KOG and KEGG analyses showed that DEGs were involved in processes including photosynthesis, flavonoid biosynthesis, response to stimulus and abiotic stress, reactive oxygen species scavenging, signal transduction, secondary metabolite biosynthesis and transport. Proteins such as LEA, DHNs and HSPs were identified. Their expression was upregulated in drought-stressed faba bean leaves, indicating their role in drought adaptation by protecting and stabilizing cellular processes.
AsA priming enhanced seed vigor in aged oat (Avena sativa) seeds. The seeds were aged by controlled deterioration at 45 degrees C for 5 days and primed with 1.5 mM AsA for 24 h.

Yield was positively correlated with the number of effective branches, main panicle length, and 1000-grain weight. Path analysis indicated that MDA content, proline content, CAT activity, and soluble sugar content positively affected yield.

Structural variations were associated with transposable elements, which influenced gene expression in coding or regulatory regions. We identified 139 loci associated with 31 domestication and agronomic traits, including candidate genes and superior haplotypes such as LG1 for panicle architecture.

The co-expression network of highly correlated modules was constructed. Hub genes of millet in response to drought stress were found. Expression changes in carbon fixation, sucrose and starch synthesis, lignin synthesis, gibberellin synthesis, and proline synthesis of millet were analyzed.

Transcriptomic analysis revealed 1776 differentially expressed genes in Liu Leng Zi Da Mai (waterlogging tolerant) and 839 DEGs in Naso Nijo (waterlogging sensitive). Many DEGs were associated with transcription factors, including AP2/ERF, HSF, WRKY, MYB, and MADS. Integration of GWAS and RNA-Seq data analysis pinpointed 32 candidate genes, primarily linked to plant hormones and energy metabolism regulation, such as ERF, HSF, AEC, and LEA.
Over 112,000 SNPs were identified for marker development. 5647 DEGs were identified.
SV markers captured genetic diversity not discerned by SNP markers. No heritability correlation was observed between SNP and SV markers associated with the same phenotype.

Roma had greater increases in Q(A) reduction state (1-q(p)), nonphotochemical quenching (NPQ), and minimal fluorescence yield (F-0) than M-81E. M-81E had lower H2O2 content than Roma. Photoinhibition may be related to reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation.

Grain yields were reduced by 51% for GD, 37% for MKL and 33% for IESH. GD exhibited a greater yield decline than IESH under water shortage. Sorghum responds to drought by reducing plant size, biomass, leaf area, transpiration rate and stomatal conductance.

The 2H chromosome has the most important QTL and pleiotropic effect for yield and its components, including kernel weight, days to heading, and biological yield. The cross Arta/Harmal was adapted, and mechanisms were developed to cope with drought stress, with biological yield and harvest index positively correlated with grain yield.
Potassium transporter 8 and monothiol glutaredoxin were hub genes in the dark green module. 2987 genes correlated with increasing CC and RWC.

Several WRKY, MYB, ERF, and bHLH transcription factors and transporters responded to Cd treatment. Cd stress affects cell wall function and GSH metabolism. These changes might contribute to Cd tolerance in Tartary buckwheat.
This marker interval contained 12 genes in either accession, with five genes in common. The Vada and SusPtrit haplotypes were found in 57% and 43%, respectively, of a 194 barley accessions panel.
A single recessive gene from T1121 contributes to longer ECL. Using five insertion and deletion markers, the gene was fine mapped to a ~255 kb region near LG10.

Electrophoretic mobility shift assays and yeast one-hybrids showed that the transcription factor SiARDP binds to the SiLTP promoter's dehydration-responsive element in vitro and in vivo. SiLTP expression was higher in SiARDP-OE plants than in WT.

To explore genetic diversity of drought-induced oxidative stress tolerance in foxtail millet, lipid peroxidation was used as a biochemical marker to assess membrane integrity under stress. It screened 107 cultivars and classified the genotypes as highly tolerant, tolerant, sensitive, and highly sensitive. Four cultivars with differential dehydration tolerance responses were selected to understand the physiological and biochemical basis of tolerance mechanisms.

FtIST1 is a novel SBP gene isolated from salt-responsive Tartary buckwheat. Its molecular characteristics and function in salt tolerance are unclear. Bioinformatics analysis of FtIST1 was performed using DNAMAN, MEGA7.0, and SOPMA.

The study explored the relationship between three spike traits. SL was affected by SRN and SD, but SRN and SD had almost no relationship. The effect of QTLs on grain weight per spike (GWPS) was explored. qSRN2-1 and qSL5-1 had a greater effect on GWPS, suggesting they are potential loci to increase yield.

GsNAC2 was induced by saline-alkali treatment and expressed in sorghum leaves. GsNAC2-overexpressing sorghum plants showed increased plant height, dry weight, moisture content, root activity, leaf length, chlorophyll content, stomatal conductance, relative root activity, relative chlorophyll content, relative stomatal conductance, and relative transpiration rate after saline-alkali treatment.

The genes of LRNK1 and LR2381 showed different expression patterns under salt stress. Metabolomic data revealed different salicylic acid and betaine profiles between LRNK1 and LR2381, which mediated salt tolerance in sorghum.

Indole-3-acetic acid (IAA), cytokinins (CTK), gibberellic acid (GA3), abscisic acid (ABA), soluble sugar, amino acid, and root bleeding intensity were mainly affected by non-additive genetic effects of the genes. Soluble protein was affected by additive genetic effects of the genes and had a high narrow heritability of 75.50%.
GWAS investigated RSA traits and GP accuracy in sorghum at seedling stage. NRA, NNR, NRL, FSW, DSW, and LA were measured in 160 sorghum accessions grown in rhizotrons.

The QTLs (QKW 1.5, QKW2.7b, QKW4.1, QKW6.7, QKW6.9) have positive effects on crop yield in semi-dry and dry areas. In the Arta/Harmal population, genomic co-localisation of the QTL suggests selection for drought tolerance in barley via linked markers.

Haplotype and QTL analyses showed that E2 genes, including SiUBC39, are associated with plant height, flowering time, and stress tolerance. CRISPR/Cas9 validation found that disrupting SiUBC39 caused early flowering, reduced plant height, and reduced grain yield, resembling Setaria viridis. IP-MS and transcriptome analysis revealed SiUBC39 is involved in growth and development regulation, drought stress response, and immune response.

A major QTL locus qGL1, accounting for 16.7% phenotypic variance, is located on chromosome 1H. It is a new QTL affecting GL. Two other QTLs, qGL5 and qTGW5, accounting for 20.7% and 21.1% phenotypic variance respectively, were identified in the same region. Sequencing showed they are identical to the HvDep1 gene.

Molecular markers are linked to morphological loci. The linkage map integrates data on quantitative traits with morphological variants and aids in map-based cloning of genes controlling morphological traits.
SbWRKY50 directly binds to the upstream promoter of the SOS1 gene in A. thaliana. In sweet sorghum, SbWRKY50 directly binds to the upstream promoters of SOS1 and HKT1.
The cultivation of oats has been affected by Septoria avenae f. sp. avenae, septoria leaf blotch disease.

FA increased the relative water and chlorophyll contents of oat leaves under drought stress. FA enhanced the activity of antioxidant enzymes SOD, POD, PAL, CAT and 4CL. FA inhibited the accumulation of malondialdehyde (MDA), hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) and dehydroascorbic acid (DHA). FA reduced oxidative damage in oat leaves, improved drought resistance, and promoted plant growth.
