text: Weighted gene co-expression network analysis showed that DEGs in modules correlated with quinoa variety and temperature were enriched in the oxidative phosphorylation pathway. Physicochemical analyses showed that superoxide dismutase and peroxidase activities, and soluble protein and sugar contents, were higher in XCq than in QCq.

Circadian rhythm genes CRY, GI, PRR5 coordinate ABA-dependent and ABA-independent pathways to enhance stress adaptation. Fluridone-mediated ABA suppression inhibited oxidoreductase activity and soluble sugar content.

Sorghum (Sorghum bicolor L.) is a drought tolerant staple crop and cold sensitive. Low temperature stresses such as chilling and frost affect the agronomic performance of sorghum and limit its geographical distribution.

Hydric stress reduced photosynthetic apparatus function by 63% due to PSII damage. Pre-anthesis was the most vulnerable stage to hydric stress, decreasing grains per panicle weight (85%), grains per panicle number (69%), and 100-grain weight (46%).
The Dry locus controls the pithy/juicy stem trait. The Dry gene encodes a plant-specific NAC transcription factor.

The proteomic results suggest that 155 differentially expressed proteins are drought-responsive proteins influenced by SiNRX1. These proteins are enriched in pathways including biosynthesis of secondary metabolites, brassinosteroid biosynthesis, metabolic pathways, and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.

Osmoregulatory substance content changes and antioxidant enzyme activity modulation are key drought resistance mechanisms. Therefore, Fv/Fo, Pro, Tr, Gs, and Pn are reliable parameters for assessing drought resistance in forage oat seedlings.
The crop has adaptation to drought, salinity, and high temperatures. It is susceptible to low temperatures.
GM2 and TKW were partially associated with markers at different positions. Prediction accuracy of yield was improved by 5% when using marker scores of GM2 and DH with yield in multiple regression.
Four markers were above the cutoff point. The significant marker-trait associations are useful for marker-assisted selection in anthracnose resistance breeding and for gene and QTL mapping.

Heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Arabidopsis thaliana mutants showed that overexpressing SbHKT1;4 caused hypersensitivity to Na+ stress. This hypersensitivity was alleviated by elevated K+ levels, indicating SbHKT1;4 may mediate K+ uptake under excessive Na+. Electrophysiology in Xenopus laevis oocytes demonstrated SbHKT1;4 transports Na+ and K+.

The quinoa C2H2 gene family promoter's 2000 bp region contains cis-acting elements responsive to stress and hormone factors. Transcriptome sequencing and RT-qPCR analysis showed higher gene expression in the high-resistant material compared to the sensitive material under flooding stress.

Watermelon, rich in citrulline, was used as a model. Transcriptomic and metabolomic analysis studied citrulline accumulation during drought stress and nitrogen perturbation. Experiments in semi-controlled and open field environments monitored stress treatments with physiological measurements to study drought-induced citrulline accumulation in vegetative tissues.

Overexpression of FtNAC31 lowered seed germination rate under ABA treatment and enhanced tolerance to salt and drought stress in transgenic Arabidopsis. Under various stresses, activities of SOD, POD, and CAT increased sharply in FtNAC31 overexpressed lines.

The maximum distance between markers ABAM232 and ABAM497 was 33.4 cM. QTL analysis using composite interval mapping identified QTLs for disease resistance from phenotypic and genotypic data of an F-2 population.

Molecular diversity of 40 Tibetan wild barley accessions, 10 Syrian, 72 North American, 36 European, 9 South American, and 8 Australian varieties was characterized using multiple microsatellite loci. The 42 SSR primers amplified 278 alleles across the 175 barley accessions.

Pearl millet is a crop with resilience to abiotic stress. We collected 1,455,924 SNP and 124,532 SV markers from a pearl millet inbred germplasm association panel of 242 accessions, including 120 observed phenotypes related to yield.

text: SiPYL3 transcript levels were upregulated under abiotic stress. SiPYL3 weakens drought resistance and positively modulates salt tolerance at the seedling stage.
Seedling reaction to crown rust was assessed as infection type with 10 isolates. Adult plant reaction was assessed in 10 location-years as percentage severity and infection reaction.

An F2 population of 300 individuals was generated by crossing the elite foxtail millet variety Jingu28 with the backbone line Ai88. A genetic linkage map was constructed using 213 published SSR markers and two InDel markers. QTL mapping identified 46 QTL for 12 agronomic traits, including 13 major effect QTL.
A non-specific LTP gene (SiLTP) was isolated from a foxtail millet suppression subtractive hybridization library. SiLTP was expressed in all foxtail millet tissues.

Path analysis indicated that MDA content, proline content, CAT activity and soluble sugar content positively affected yield. Proline content contributed the most. Qing1 and Long4 are suitable for large-scale planting in alpine regions. B-16 is suitable for functional food research and development. Gong8 is suitable for large-scale planting and functional food development.

qRT-PCR results indicated that 30 genes responded to Usi infection. 17 genes showed strong association with rust resistance. Three resistance-associated genes demonstrated transactivation activity and nuclear localization, indicating regulatory function in defense responses.
Thirty-nine favorable QTL alleles inherit from Yugu1. Three stable QTLs were detected in multi-environments. Nine QTL clusters were identified on Chromosome 3, 6, 7 and 9.
A QTL on chromosome 13A is associated with a malate transporter locus (LOC_Os06g15779). Malate exudation increased in the Al-tolerant parent UFRGS17 but not in the sensitive parent.
10 SNPs and 17 QTLs were identified for ESC. 40 SNPs were identified for LSF. The diversity panel showed variation in traits associated with ESC and LSF tolerance.
The qSL2-5 overlaps with the zeo1 gene. zeo1 is the SL regulatory gene in qSL2-5. The qSRN2-1 overlaps with vrs1. vrs1 affects SRN.

NPH3 and SnRK1 regulate seed germinability under cold stress. Candidates can be exploited to develop low temperature tolerant cultivars for marker-assisted breeding, genomic selection, and genetic engineering.
Weedy and wild Avena species harbour genes providing resistance to septoria leaf blotch. One breakthrough involves disrupting avenacinase activity.
Three and two QTNs were detected by three and two multi-locus methods, respectively. One QTN on chromosome 5 is associated with CP, NDF, and ADF.
A major seed dormancy QTL in sorghum, qDor7, was fine mapped. The qDor7 locus was delimited to a 96-kb region containing 16 predicted gene models.

DETs related to hormone signaling (auxins, ethylene, gibberellin, and abscisic acid), kinases, protein modifications, and degradation were identified, indicating roles of hormones and kinases in enhancing salt tolerance. Transcription factors, such as ERF, bHLH, HBP, MADS, MYB, and WRKY, were predicted to regulate transcripts involved in salt stress responses.

NaCl treatments inhibited growth and photosynthesis more severely in Lvjuren than in Fengtian. Fengtian restricted overaccumulation of Na+ and Cl- in leaf blades more efficiently than Lvjuren. This was attributed to upregulated expressions of SbNHX2, SbHKT1;4, SbHKT1;5, SbCLCc, and SbCLCg or downregulated expression of SbNPF6.4.
SiPLATZ12 overexpression increases Na+ accumulation, decreases K+ accumulation, and lowers vacuolar pH. SiPLATZ12 overexpression represses transcription of most NHX, SOS, and CBL genes.

The salt-tolerant oat cultivar Vao-9 and the salt-sensitive oat cultivar Bai5 were treated with 150 mmol L-1 salt stress for 6 days. Label-Free technology was used to analyze differentially expressed proteins in leaves.

'Dingku-1' had improved plant height, stem diameter, and biomass accumulation after rewatering from a brief drought. 'Xiqiao-2' showed greater tolerance to long-term post-anthesis drought. 'Dingku-1' was less affected by short-term post-anthesis drought.

The stabilized hybrid Summer (♀) x Kanlow (♂) (SxK) can be damaged by aphids. Hormone, metabolite, and RNA-Seq analyses delineated defense networks induced by aphid feeding in SxK switchgrass and pinpointed transcription factors (TFs) such as WRKYs that regulate these responses.

Functional PACE-PCR markers were developed for Wx, wx(a), and wx(b) alleles. The markers are suitable for marker-assisted selection and multiplexed, low-to-mid-density genomic selection. They were validated in segregating populations of three public and private breeding programs.
Four stable QTL were identified. Eight candidate genes were predicted using RNA-seq and WGCNA.

Seven PsRBOH genes were identified in the pea (Pisum sativum L.) genome. They were phylogenetically clustered into five groups. Collinearity analyses identified four pairs of orthologs between pea and soybean RBOHs.

SiDREBs were identified as 166 family members and classified into six subfamilies. They are unevenly distributed on nine chromosomes and designated as SiDREB1-166 based on their chromosomal positions.
The sorghum aphid Melanaphis sacchari is an important insect in the late growth phase of sorghum (Sorghum bicolor). The mechanisms of sorghum response to aphid infestation are unclear.

Ten QTL for bruchid resistance were identified on chromosomes Vu02, Vu03, Vu04, Vu06, Vu08, Vu09, and Vu11. Two major QTLs were mapped: MDP6.1(MW) on Vu06 for median development period (R-2 = 25.9%), and a co-localized QTL region on Vu09 containing NEI9.1(MW) for adult bruchid emergence number (R-2 = 28.6%), NE9.1(MW) for egg number per 10 seeds (R-2 = 18.8%), ANH9.1(MW) for average exit holes on seeds (R-2 = 19.6%), and seed weight (R-2 = 15.6%-16.3%). Markers linked to these QTLs (MDP6.1(MW), NEI9.1(MW), ANH9.1(MW)) can be validated for marker-assisted selection in cowpea breeding.

An F-2 population was derived from a cross between the breeding line 'Tokei1121' (T1121) and the cultivar 'Erimo167'. It was phenotyped for epicotyl length (ECL) and genotyped using SSR and SNP markers. A molecular linkage map was generated. Fifty-two segregating markers, including 27 SSRs and 25 SNPs, were located on seven linkage groups at a LOD threshold of 3.0.

The ASM was validated in 170 foxtail millet accessions using single-marker regression analysis. Regression of lipid peroxidation (LP) on the ASM showed that the SiDREB2-associated trait explained 27.18% of LP variation, indicating it is a major QTL for dehydration tolerance.
SiTPSs and SiTPPs contribute to low-temperature stress responses. These genes are genetic resources for low-temperature breeding in foxtail millet.
Broomcorn millet (Panicum miliaceum L.) is a crop domesticated in northern China. It has been improved for reproductive and vegetative traits.

Salinity tolerance is an important trait in brewing sorghum. A transcriptome and metabolome analysis was conducted on two sweet sorghum genotypes: the salt-tolerant line NY1298 and the salt-sensitive line MY1176, after salt treatment.
Two QTL clusters contained overlapping QTLs for PH, FW and DW. Orthologs of three SD candidate genes from maize were found within two SD QTLs.
42 common unigenes expressed differentially in all four cultivars after 100 mM NaCl for 48 h. These DEGs may be involved in salinity response. Eight of the 42 DEGs encode transcription factors.

In sorghum, stay-green is a trait for improving post-flowering drought tolerance. Genes in the introgressed QTL regions are predicted and profiled to identify candidate genes and prove the genetic basis.

A linkage map was developed and QTLs for pyrenophora leaf spot resistance were identified in oats (Avena sativa L.). The study used 96 F-2 plants derived from the cross EC/0007662 (resistant) and EC/0131291 (susceptible).

text: This study examined dehydration responses in four drought-distinguished millet genotypes (two tolerant and two sensitive). The genotypes were subjected to dehydration stress using 20% PEG-6000 at time points 0, 2, 6, and 12 hours.
19 QTLs were detected for five traits: one QTL for CP, five QTLs for NDF, three QTLs for ADF, two QTLs for ADL, and eight QTLs for HC. Four overlapping QTLs were detected for NDF, ADF, ADL, and HC.
Localization of these QTL on the consensus map identified 16 genomic regions containing 125 QTL. Key regions were selected based on identified QTL, and their role in drought adaptation is discussed.

Bulked segregant analysis and molecular marker mapping identified a major locus, HvSS1, in a recombination cold spot spanning 173.44-396.33 Mb on chromosome 6H. Candidate gene mining was conducted by analyzing sequence differences, expression patterns, and variant distributions of genes in the interval using barley genomic resources.
RNA-seq analysis and qRT-PCR of nine VrBBX genes showed they may respond to environmental stress. VrBBX5, VrBBX10 and VrBBX12 are important candidate genes for plant stress response.

The stay-green trait is a component of tolerance to terminal drought stress in sorghum. To map QTLs for stay-green, two recombinant inbred populations of 226 F-3:5 lines each were developed from crosses IS9830 x E36-1 and N13 x E36-1.
One QTL is on chromosome 3H and three QTLs are on chromosome 5H. All PHS QTLs coincide with dormancy QTLs, but some QTLs are more important for PHS than for dormancy.

Lines 1180, 1373, 1318, and 1 had the highest average grain yield, outperforming the recurrent parent Teshale. Lines 1199, 1263, 1101, and 1204 had the shortest average days to flowering, making them suitable to escape moisture stress periods.
A panel of 243 oat accessions was used to explore coleoptile length. Coleoptile length is crucial for determining sowing depth in low-precipitation regions and is significant for oat breeding.

A field cold emergence trial and two semi-controlled frost survival experiments were conducted using a diversity set (n = 285) to investigate frost survival traits. Results show high phenotypic diversity for frost survival. Superior genotypes combine high frost tolerance and cold emergence levels via quantitative trait inheritance. Tolerance to temperatures above and below 0°C was not correlated, suggesting separate genetic control.

Transcriptome sequencing was performed on two quinoa cultivars with different storage performance: Longli No.4 (L4) and Longli No.1 (L1). Multispectral imaging and germination phenotypes showed significantly less seed vigor loss in L1 than in L4, indicating L1 seeds have stronger aging resistance and storability.
Sorghum dwarfing genes Dw1, Dw2, and Dw3 are crucial for plant architecture and yield. Their allelic distribution was analyzed in 241 Chinese landrace accessions.
Rust causes yield losses in lentil. It is controlled by identifying resistant sources, understanding inheritance, and breeding for host resistance.
Sorghum drought tolerance and heat stress tolerance must be genetically improved. Drought tolerance mechanism, stay green trait, and grain yield under stress have been studied.

Six sorghum restorer lines, six sorghum sterile lines, and 36 hybrid combinations were created using the NCII incomplete double-row hybridisation method. F1 generation hybrids were evaluated for leaf photosynthesis parameters, carbon metabolism enzymes, and their correlation with yield traits during the flowering stage.

Two CC-NBS-LRR alleles, PmTR1 and PmTR3, were isolated and validated. They are located on rye chromosome 6RS. PmTR1 confers age-related resistance starting from the three-leaf stage. PmTR3 confers typical all-stage resistance.

SbWRKY51 ectopic expression in Arabidopsis showed similar phenotypes. Mutation of the homologous gene AtWRKY11 increased salt sensitivity in Arabidopsis. Under salt stress, SbWRKY51-overexpressed transgenic sorghum altered expression of oxidative stress and lignin biosynthesis genes and increased lignin content.

The three-line method was used to produce waxy sorghums, including the male sterile line (A-line), male sterile maintenance line (B-line), and fertility-restoring line (R-line). Using backcross breeding, molecular-assisted selection, and traditional field selection, high-quality three-line materials (A-, B-, R-lines, named CNA1, CNB1, CNR1) and the F1 hybrid CNH1 with favorable agronomic traits and yield quality were obtained.

A 6-day heat shock treatment reaching 45°C was imposed on two Sorghum bicolor genotypes: one heat-sensitive (Sen) and one heat-tolerant (Tol). Sen reduced photosynthetic functioning during heat shock. Tol increased its photosynthetic rate.

Gene expression was compared between stay-green sorghum variety B35 and senescent variety R16. Differentially expressed transcripts showed enrichment for genes in the 'response to osmotic stress' GO category. P5CS2 gene was highly expressed in the stay-green line B35 compared to the senescent line R16. This high expression correlated with higher proline levels.

The population was phenotyped for stem greenness maintenance (stg) and recovery dry weight (rdw) after drought stress in greenhouse experiments. In field experiments, visual ratings and dry weights were used to phenotype drought stress-induced premature senescence in the RIL population.

text: The study introduced Populus euphratica PeNAC1 into hexaploid cultivated oat, producing six transgenic lines. Under salt stress, PeNAC1 expression improved seed germination rate, seedling survival rate, and leaf chlorophyll content in transgenic plants compared to the non-transgenic control. These changes increased spikelet number and grain number per spike in transgenic oat under salinity and reduced yield loss per plant.
The map included 1610 markers. QTL mapping identified 19 QTLs: two for PH, three for SD, four for TN, four for FW, and five for DW. Two QTL clusters contained overlapping QTLs for PH, FW, and DW.

qFLW5-2 was a major QTL detected across 3 environments and explained a large proportion of FLW variation. The QTL was validated with 9 InDel markers across different planting densities. RNA-seq revealed 2,293 and 2,338 DEGs between biparents at heading stage and grain filling stage.

text: Gong8 had high yield. B-16 had lower starch content than corn, lower fat content than wheat, higher protein content than rice, higher dietary fibre content than rice, and higher essential amino acid content than rice.

A region on SBI-01a from SQR was associated with seedling emergence and seedling vigor scores in early and late plantings. A QTL for early and late emergence was identified by CIM on SBI-02, favoring the SRN39 allele.

The Agrobacterium-mediated transformation protocol was optimised for F. tataricum callus, achieving a transformation rate of approximately 20%. The protocol was confirmed by successful GUS staining, GFP expression, and albino plant generation via FtPDS gene inactivation.

We phenotyped these accessions for ten agro-morphological traits under water limited environments at Kobo and Mieso. Significant differences among genotypes and genotype by environment interactions were found for all traits. Significant correlations were found between agro-morphological traits.

SiCBL5 overexpression in foxtail millet increased salt tolerance. SiCBL5 knockdown increased salt hypersensitivity. SiCBL5 physically interacts with SiCIPK24 in vitro and in vivo, shown by yeast two-hybrid and firefly luciferase complementation imaging assays.

An important QTL was found which may affect multiple traits. The gene SORBI_3008G116500 is the most likely candidate gene. It is a homolog of the Arabidopsis thaliana gene VIP5, found by analyzing the gene annotation in the LD block.

Twelve QTLs for five foliar diseases were detected on sorghum linkage groups SBI-03, SBI-04, and SBI-06. They accounted for 6.9-44.9% phenotypic variance. The morphological marker Plant color (Plcor) was associated with most QTLs across years and locations.

Inclusive Composite Interval Mapping (ICIM) mapped QTLs for five forage traits. 18 QTLs were identified for plant height (PH, 4 QTLs), fresh weight (FW, 8 QTLs), dry weight (DW, 3 QTLs), stem diameter (SD, 2 QTLs), and sugar content (SC, 1 QTL).
Genes regulating chlorophyll content (CC) and relative water content (RWC) under abiotic stress were screened using WGCNA. Gene-trait correlations were defined as modules, each denoted by a color.
The locus explained 15% of phenotypic variance in GWAS and 64% in QTL mapping. Pm13 confers APR, confirmed by comparing field data with laboratory leaf segment tests.

Blp1 was delimited within a 1.66 Mb region on chromosome 1H by the upstream marker HZSNP63 and the downstream marker HZSNP59. The SLAF-seq-BSA approach identified the candidate region and discovered polymorphic markers.

Isolation and analysis confirmed the markers' homology with drought-induced ESTs from cowpea. Two candidates showed homology to a multidrug resistance protein 3 and a photosystem I assembly protein ycf3.

Barley yellow dwarf viruses (BYDVs) cause barley yellow dwarf (BYD) disease and affect oat (Avena sativa L.). Oat lacked molecular marker technology for marker-trait association studies to determine genetic tolerance mechanisms.
We highlighted the genetic basis of drought tolerance in sorghum. We also discussed interactions of drought with other abiotic stresses and biotic factors.
The down-regulation of ribosomal protein-related genes causes stunted growth in salt-sensitive accessions. The salt-tolerant accession alleviates salt stress by increasing energy production.

A single cytoplasmic source of foxtail millet male sterile lines limits heterosis utilization. To explore cytoplasmic genetic diversity, 23 polymorphic mitochondrial DNA primer pairs were identified from 14 diverse foxtail millet accessions after screening 34 consensus mtDNA primers derived from various plant species.

The salt tolerance of 188 accessions was evaluated using shoot length, root length, shoot fresh weight, root fresh weight, shoot dry weight, and root dry weight. The evaluation used the salt tolerant index, Spearman correlation analysis, membership function analysis, principal component analysis, and cluster analysis. The results classified the accessions as highly salt tolerant, salt-tolerant, moderate, salt-sensitive, or highly salt-sensitive.

Transcriptome analysis revealed that up-regulated genes in transgenic rice were involved in phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, fatty acid elongation, phenylalanine metabolism, and flavonoid biosynthesis pathways. Quantitative real-time PCR showed that genes encoding major enzymes in lignin and suberin biosynthesis pathways had higher expression in SiMYB16 transgenic plants. Flavonoid and lignin content, and fatty acid synthase activity increased in SiMYB16 transgenic rice compared to wild-type under salt stress.
Zinc and iron are essential mineral elements for crop growth and human health. Foxtail millet is a food crop rich in mineral elements and an important dietary supplement.

Two QTL on chromosomes 5H and 6H, designated Qcl.caf-5H and Qcl.caf-6H, are novel. Qcl.caf-5H explained up to 30.9% of phenotypic variance with a LOD of 15.1. Five near isogenic line pairs were developed to validate this QTL. The NILs showed an average 21.0% difference in CL.

SbMADS1 influences days to flowering. SbFT acts as a mobile signal integrating photoperiod cues. These genes are involved in starch and sucrose metabolism pathways. The pathways support improved growth and yield in sorghum. Functional genome annotation revealed that candidate genes are involved in starch and sucrose metabolism, secondary metabolism, and hormonal signaling.

A novel bHLH gene FtbHLH3 was isolated. In tartary buckwheat, FtbHLH3 expression was induced by polyethylene glycol 6000 (PEG6000) and abscisic acid (ABA). Subcellular localization and a yeast one-hybrid assay showed FtbHLH3 has transcriptional activation activity.

Forage sorghum cultivars in India are susceptible to foliar diseases including anthracnose, rust, zonate leaf spot, drechslera leaf blight and target leaf spot. QTLs confer resistance to these foliar diseases.
QTL regions contain genes encoding chilling stress and hormonal response genes. These QTL can aid molecular breeding of sorghum for improved low-temperature germinability.

The Stg introgression lines Meko/B35-selection 120, Teshale/B35-selection 2 and Teshale/E36-1 showed better drought stress tolerance than other genotypes. These lines also showed better grain yield than their recurrent parents under post-flowering drought stress.

A barley mapping population was developed using morphological marker stocks as parents. Ninety-four doubled-haploid lines were derived from an F-1 using the Hordeum bulbosum system for genetic mapping.
Chinese chestnut is affected by red spider attack. Tannins are secondary metabolites associated with plant defense against insects.

Weighted gene co-expression network analysis revealed three modules linked to salt stress in BY953. Key genes AsGF14B, AsPRMS, and AsUPL1 play central roles. BY953 activates key genes involved in proline metabolism, peroxisome and ion transport during salt stress. This enables BY953 to maintain osmotic pressure, ensure cell membrane integrity, scavenge ROS, and enhance root tissue tolerance and shoot tissue ability to excrete Na+ and retain K+.

NPBTs have been applied to address pathogen threats in citrus. Citrus could use NPBTs due to its complex biology, including seedlessness, apomixis, high heterozygosity, long juvenility, and aptitude for in vitro manipulation.

The genes underlying two cloned QTLs, Dw3 for plant height on chromosome 7 and Ma1 for flowering time on chromosome 6, were localized to the correct genomic regions. Two genomic regions on chromosomes 6 and 7 are closely associated with multiple traits, suggesting pleiotropy or tight linkage.

Genome editing in citrus via transgenesis has induced resistance to Citrus bacterial canker in sweet orange and grapefruit using the resistance gene CsLOB1. NPBTs will be used to improve fruit traits.

A resistance locus on chromosome 2 was effective against pathotypes from Texas and Puerto Rico. A 41.6 kb genomic region at the tip of chromosome 8 was associated with resistance response in Georgia, Texas, and Puerto Rico.

entities.text: pathotypes from Texas and Puerto Rico
entities.label: BIS

text: The dw1dw3 genotype shows strong height reduction. This advances understanding of sorghum plant height genetic architecture and provides a foundation for molecular breeding strategies.

Using 1571 polymorphic SNP markers from the adzuki bean genome, 26 agronomic QTLs and five qualitative trait genes related to pigmentation were mapped via restriction-site-associated DNA sequencing of 150 members of an F2 population from a cross between cultivated and wild adzuki beans. On chromosomes 4, 7, and 10, 11 QTLs for flowering time and pod maturity were mapped. Six QTLs for 100-seed weight were detected.
The QTL cluster qTN6.2/qFW6/qDW6.1 overlaps with qTN6.1 and qDW6.2. The sorghum ortholog of the heterotic biomass gene ARGOS, Sobic.006G092500, is located within the five QTLs on chromosome 6.

SiCDPK7 transgenic plant lines had higher catalase (CAT) activity and lower malonaldehyde (MDA) content than WT plants. SiCDPK7 transgenic Arabidopsis and foxtail millet showed increased transcription levels of heat and cold stress-responsive genes under stress conditions.

To isolate the gene, a high-resolution mapping population with 10,204 meiotic events was constructed. F-2 plants were screened using co-dominant flanking markers, and segmental recombinant inbred lines (RILs) were tested for BaMMV resistance.

The QTL improved leaf relative water content by 5.0% in 'Baronesse' and 9.1% in 'Aydanhanim' under controlled environments. It accelerated heading and maturity by 2.5 days in 'Baronesse' and by 5-6 days in 'Aydanhanim'.

Population genomics analyses on 445 worldwide sorghum accessions, covering wild sorghum and four end-use subpopulations. Frequent genetic exchanges among subpopulations. Strong selective sweeps affected 14.68% (107.5 Mb) of the sorghum genome, including 3649, 4287, and 3888 genes during sorghum domestication, grain sorghum improvement, and sweet sorghum improvement.
The sbgpgl1 mutant and wild-type BTx623 were compared at the heading stage. The sbgpgl1 mutant had less height, and smaller leaf and panicle length and width.

A genome-wide association study on drought tolerance in Tartary buckwheat germplasm identified a gene encoding pectin methylesterase inhibitors protein (FtPMEI13). This gene is associated with drought tolerance and is induced during drought stress and abscisic acid (ABA) treatment.

Using marker-assisted selection, the resistance QTL QTL-Sb.RKN.3.1 from 'Honey Drip' sorghum was crossed into five sorghum backgrounds including forage, sweet, and grain sorghum until the BC1F6 generation. Greenhouse experiments showed the recurrent parent genotypes were susceptible to M. incognita and statistically similar.

Sorghum cultivars SCS (Sanchisan) and JL (Jinliang 5) have contrasting drought resistance. Under drought stress, soluble sugar content in SCS leaves increased by 112.2%. Betaine and POD levels in JL leaves also increased. Metabolome analysis identified 374 differentially accumulated metabolites.

Agronomic traits were evaluated in 1250 foxtail millet accessions. A crucial gene SiTGW6 governing grain yield was identified. Elite haplotypes and dCAPS markers were developed for SiTGW6 to facilitate molecular breeding.

A core collection of 888 foxtail millet accessions was inoculated with blast strain HN-1. GWAS identified regulators for blast disease resistance. Phenotypic variation showed: <1.60% highly resistant, 35.25% moderately resistant, 57.09% moderately susceptible, 6.08% highly susceptible.

Covariance analysis showed SiDREBs are more closely related to monocots sorghum, maize, and rice than to dicots Arabidopsis thaliana, tomato, and soybean. Promoter analysis revealed SiDREBs contain stress-related cis-acting elements.

The sorghum transcription factor SbNAC2 is strongly induced by abiotic stresses: PEG-simulated dehydration, salt, and chilling at 4°C. Virus-induced gene silencing of SbNAC2 in sorghum seedlings weakened drought tolerance.

Analysis revealed that quinoa inhibits photosynthesis at high temperatures. Strategies for high temperature stress management include regulation of heat stress transcription factors (HSFs) for heat tolerance and regulation of purine metabolism to enhance stress signals. The tolerant genotype may have an enhanced response through lower purine levels.

AFLP markers clustered on centromeric regions and were more abundant on chromosome 1 (7H). RAPD markers showed 54% segregation distortion, compared to 26% for AFLP markers, 27% for SSR markers, and 18% for RFLP markers.

Diversity Arrays Technology Sequencing (DArTseq) based single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNP) markers and anthracnose resistance genes were associated genome-wide in diverse sorghum populations for resistance breeding. Three hundred sixty-six sorghum populations were assessed for anthracnose resistance in three seasons in western Ethiopia using artificial inoculation.
Overexpression of the OsOAT gene in rice enhanced drought and osmotic stress tolerance. It increased delta-OAT activity and Pro accumulation under normal conditions.

Two materials with significant grain quality differences were selected for transcriptome analysis. In barley grain, 4636 and 3684 differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified at 21 days post anthesis and 28 days post anthesis, respectively. These DEGs mainly participate in carbon fixation in photosynthetic organisms and starch and sucrose metabolism.

Rice-Tartary is an easily-shelled local variety. The F-2 segregating population was constructed by crossing Yunqiao No.1 (hard-shelled) and Rice-Tartary (easily-shelled). BSA-seq was used for initial mapping.
The SiARDP gene was cloned from foxtail millet. Its transcript level increased after drought, high salt, low temperature, and ABA treatment in foxtail millet seedlings.

Under drought stress, water use efficiency increased by 18.4% in An and by 63.2% in YG. Root exudation decreased by 66.7% in YG and by 89.0% in An. Photosynthesis rate decreased by 34.3% in YG and by 61.8% in An. Grain yield decreased by 40.6% in YG and by 62.6% in An.
The drought resistant hybrid M79 and its parental lines E1 and H1 were exposed to drought stress. M79 showed higher photosynthetic energy conversion efficiency and drought tolerance than E1 and H1.
The 4H and 7H QTLs had negative additive effects of -0.02 and -0.033, and originated from the male parent Schooner.
Sorghum bicolor is an important cereal crop. Stay-green sorghum plants retain green leaf area longer under drought conditions and have higher yields than senescent plants.

We integrated multiple data sets to combine recent QTL and GWAS analyses for yield and drought resistance. This uncovered consistent genomic regions related to these traits. We determined the physical position of significant markers in the reference faba bean genome and assessed the co-localization of significant QTNs with stable QTLs, pinpointing likely candidate genes. One hundred fifty-two annotated genes in 10 overlapping genomic regions were predicted as faba bean candidate genes for drought and yield related traits. Many were closely related to genes previously identified in other crops.
The MTSI selection index selected genotypes based on multiple agro-morphological traits, excluding leaf-to-stem ratio. Genotypes G81, G90, G80, and G89 were identified as desirable by MTSI.
We analyzed 33 SiNAC genes from the foxtail millet genome. Phylogenetic analysis classified these genes into 11 subgroups. Chromosomal mapping localized them to nine chromosomes.

FtbHLH3 overexpression in Arabidopsis increased drought tolerance and oxidative tolerance. Transgenic lines had lower MDA, IL, and ROS, and higher Pro content, antioxidant enzyme activities, and photosynthetic efficiency compared to WT. Under drought stress, transgenic lines had higher expression of stress-responsive genes than WT.

QTL for stem sugar-related and other agronomic traits were identified in a sorghum population from the cross R9188 x R9403463-2-1. QTL analyses used phenotypic data for 11 traits from two field experiments and a genetic map with 228 SSR and AFLP markers. The markers formed 16 linkage groups, with 11 groups assigned to sorghum chromosomes SBI-01 to SBI-10.

We analyzed the E2 gene family in foxtail millet (Setaria italica). 52 E2 genes were identified and classified into four subfamilies (UBC, UEV, SCE, and RCE). Foxtail millet exhibited significant gene expansion.
QTLs detected per trait ranged from 1 to 5, averaging 2.3. There were five QTLs for tuber width and three QTLs for tuber weight.
210 MYB genes were identified in sorghum. The genes, named SbMYB1-SbMYB210, are distributed across ten chromosomes. They were divided into six subfamilies.

Foliar resistance to Ascochyta lentis is controlled by a dominant gene (AbR(1)) in lentil accession ILL5588 ('Northfield'). Flanking RAPD markers linked to the resistance locus were identified by bulked segregant analysis.
The dendrogram from SSR molecular marker data grouped the genotypes into three clusters. The SSR marker data showed a narrow genetic base in mungbean genotypes.
Citric acid treatment up-regulated CHS, ANR, MYB and bHLH genes and promoted flavonoid accumulation. It delayed sorghum seed discoloration and enhanced antioxidant activity and storage life.
High-affinity K+ transporters (HKT) are essential for plant response to abiotic stresses. They maintain Na+ and K+ balance and prevent sodium toxicity in root and leaf.

Transcriptomic and metabolomic analyses were performed on the roots of a Cd-tolerant Tartary buckwheat cultivar after 0 h (CK), 6 h (T1), and 48 h (T2) of Cd treatment. Fresh weight and root length were not significantly inhibited under T1 treatment but were inhibited under T2 treatment.

High-yielding varieties had decreased plant height and increased leaf area per plant at maturity compared to low- and middle-yielding varieties. The decline in leaf area per plant and root traits from flowering to maturity was less pronounced in high-yielding varieties.

Multiple-effect QTLs were identified. The major QTL on chromosome SBI-06 was significantly associated with flowering date, plant height, Brix, sucrose, and sugar content. Four of ten traits showed significant QTL-by-environment interaction.

The QTL qFBW6.1 is newly reported. Eight SNPs were converted into CAPS markers. These QTLs and markers can be used for marker-assisted selection in sorghum to develop lines with bioenergy-related traits.

The profiling of 200 Tartary buckwheat accessions shows 540 metabolites were modified by human selection. A metabolic genome-wide association study shows 384 mGWAS signals for 336 metabolites are under selection.

DNA marker GSR345 is a reliable diagnostic tool for greenbug resistance in twenty sorghum accessions. It facilitates marker-assisted breeding for aphid resistance and positional-cloning of SgR1 in sorghum.
The QTL-hotspot genomic region controls chickpea growth under drought. A non-synonymous substitution in the transcription factor CaTIFY4b regulates chickpea seed weight and organ size.

The S. italica genome was sequenced in 2012, but lacked high-density genetic maps linked to agronomic and yield traits. We resequenced a foxtail millet population of 439 recombinant inbred lines (RILs) and developed a high-resolution bin map with high-density SNP markers for gene identification.

Ninety-two doubled haploid lines were generated from a cross between Franklin and TX9425. They were grown in three environments to identify QTLs controlling heading date, plant height and spike characteristics.

A pot experiment investigated the effect of foliar application of gibberellic acid, kinetin, and salicylic acid under salt stress on two sweet sorghum varieties, Yajin 13 and Yajin 71. Parameters included Na+, K+, Ca2+, P, and Mg2+ content, photosynthetic rate, transpiration rate, stomatal conductance, and membrane permeability.

Analysis of cis-acting elements showed that SbPUB genes are involved in response to salt stress. Proteomic and transcriptomic data show that several SbPUB genes have diverse expressions under different salt treatments.
Cold storage delays ripening and senescence, but reduces fruit aroma after long-term storage. The lipoxygenase (LOX) pathway synthesizes esters responsible for 'Nanguo' pear aroma.
A F(2:3) mapping population was derived from a cross between cowpea line TVu2185 and yard-long bean line TVu6642. Using DArT markers, 30 QTLs for pod and seed traits were detected.

SiPLC1 is predominantly expressed in roots during early stem elongation. SiPLC1 is upregulated under salt stress. Overexpression of SiPLC1 in Arabidopsis mitigated salt-induced damage. SiPLC1 has a role in salt-stress signal transduction in foxtail millet.
Drought tolerant material M059 and drought sensitive material M024 were selected from 70 quinoa germplasms. 7042 differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified by transcriptomic analyses.

The two RIPS were evaluated during post-rainy seasons at Patancheru, India, in 1999/2000 (RIP 2) and 2000/2001 (RIP 1). The stay-green traits measured were green leaf area percentages at 15, 30 and 45 days after flowering (% GL15, % GL30, % GL45).

text: 305 significant marker-trait associations were found for 11 traits. 21 were major effect for pod yield per plant (PYPP), specific dry weight at 70 days after sowing (SDW_70), and specific leaf area at at 70 DAS (SLA_70). Three major main effect QTLs were identified for PYPP with the highest phenotypic variance explained (PVE) of 10.5%.

text: The individual maps covered 629.7-883.5 cM, comprised 672-981 DArT markers and 15-19 linkage groups, with an average marker distance of 0.9-1.2 cM. The consensus map had a total length of 795.3 cM, comprised 1539 DArT markers and resolved 11 linkage groups, with an average inter-marker distance of 0.65 cM.
FtMYB30 is an R2R3-MYB transcription factor from the SG22 family. It localizes in the nucleus and has transcriptional activation activity. FtMYB30 responds to abiotic stress and abscisic acid (ABA).
Haplotype analysis revealed multiple haplotypes on these genes. Extreme varieties were analyzed for seed starch content and starch granule characteristics.

Four novel QTL were identified. Qstna.sau-5H and Qnhi.sau-5H are N-related QTL unaffected by N treatment. Qtgw.sau-2H is for thousand-grain weight. Qph.sau-3H is for plant height. Qsl.sau-7H is for spike length. Qal.sau-7H is for awn length. These four QTL are stable expression QTL.
Fungal resistance genes are identified for breeding. Quantitative trait loci mapping studies have reported genomic regions linked to fungal disease resistance.
Sorghum is a genetically diversified grain crop grown in semiarid regions. Drought stress severely restricts its production and reduces its productivity and nutritional quality.

The BIC model selection criteria was used to choose the best model and ascertain the working traits. The working traits were combined to detect QTL affecting these traits, which can boost QTL signals and increase statistical power. The method was applied to a Barley dataset containing eight traits.
Sorghum bicolor yields are affected by saline-alkali conditions. NAC transcription factors function in plant stress response.

Ectopic overexpression of SiNCED1 enhances drought stress resistance by elevating endogenous ABA levels and promoting stomatal closure. SiNCED1 modulates ABA-related stress responsive gene expression.

A natural population of 334 barley varieties or lines and a double haploid population from the cross between Tam407227 and Franklin were used to search for new QTL for BYMD resistance. Two major QTL on chromosomes 3H and 7H were detected by genome wide association study and validated in the DH population.

1398 Chinese barley accessions were characterized with molecular markers for vernalization genes HvVRN1 and HvVRN2. Different haplotype combinations at these genes were present at different frequencies in wild barley, landraces and modern cultivars.

The analysis of daily growth traits revealed early transient QTL predictive of final phenotypes. It identified known and novel candidate genes associated with growth-related traits and temporal responses to cold stress.

Major QTLs for heading date, plant height, node number, stem diameter, panicle neck length, and flag leaf width were consistently detected under both photoperiods. Close associations of genomic regions on chromosomes 6 and 7 with multiple traits suggest pleiotropy or tight linkage.

Whole-genome duplication analysis showed 14 duplication pairs in the MAPK cascade family in foxtail millet, expanded by segmental replication. qRT-PCR results indicated that expression levels of most SiMAPKs and SiMAPKKs changed under exogenous hormone and abiotic stress treatments. SiMAPK3 and SiMAPKK4-2 were induced under almost all treatments, while SiMAPKK5 expression was repressed.

Another 40 markers showed associations with resistance. These markers are distributed across all seven barley chromosomes. Most markers were grouped into eleven QTL regions. These QTL regions coincide with the locations of most reported resistance genes.

Tolerant accession Q68 showed superior salt tolerance associated with faster root-specific H2O2 accumulation and reactive oxygen species-induced K+ and Ca2+ fluxes in the root apex within 30 min after NaCl application. This was associated with constitutive up-regulation of membrane-localized receptor kinase regulatory protein FERONIA.

text: qRT-PCR analysis indicated that multiple stress-responsive genes expression in transgenic lines was significantly higher than in WT under drought stress. The expression of AtNCED gene in ABA biosynthesis was increased significantly under normal and stress conditions. An ABA-response-element (ABRE) was found in promoter regions.
This QTL is likely Rmy16Hb. The three QTL have an additive effect on BYMD resistance, reducing disease scores from 2.45 (all sensitive alleles) to 0.62 (all tolerant alleles).

Beta-1,3-glucanase (XnhsbmSFC4/SBI-10) is involved in aphid and brown planthopper resistance. It was associated with deadhearts% and leaf glossiness. Comparative QTL analysis revealed common QTL for shoot fly, greenbug, head bug, and midge.

Marker-assisted selection was used to transfer the resistance QTL, QTL-Sb.RKN.3.1, from 'Honey Drip' sorghum into five different sorghum backgrounds, including forage, sweet, and grain sorghum, until the BC1F6 generation.

A cowpea genetic map was constructed using the F-2 population from the cross IC-1 x BRS Marataoa, based on SNP markers. The cpi gene was mapped with reference to the species consensus map to identify markers for assisted selection for BNF. Parents and 89 F-2 plants were genotyped with 51,128 SNP markers, and 910 polymorphic markers were used to construct the map.
Integrative analyses of transcriptomic data across tissues and developmental stages in sorghum are limited. This limits the discovery of the genetic basis underlying complex agricultural traits.

Genome-wide screening developed SSR markers associated with six major agronomic traits and rutin contents in 97 core germplasm resources. 40,901 SSR loci were identified; they were uniformly distributed throughout the TB genome, with a mean distance of 11 kb between loci.
Four allelic variants SAI-1a, SAI-1b, SAI-1c and SAI-1d were identified at the SAI-1 locus in 165 sweet sorghum cultivars. A co-dominant marker SBX1 was developed to discriminate these variants.
Quantitative trait locus mapping identified 26 QTLs associated with plant height. 13 QTLs were detected under multiple environments, including six novel QTLs.

Fifteen QTLs were related to NUE and its components. Most QTLs were detected at lower nitrogen rates, none at the highest rate. These QTLs were found on Chromosomes 3(3H) and 7(5H) in 2005 and on Chromosome 2(2H) in 2008.
BSA-Seq anchored the barley pale-green (pg) gene on chromosome 3HL. Marker-assisted validation mapped pg to a 3.9 Mb physical interval.
16 QTL were identified in the C-1 population. 39 QTL were identified in the C-2 population. Two major QTL were in the C-1 population. Three major QTL were in the C-2 population.

Major and minor QTLs and QTL interactions confirm drought tolerance complexity in groundnut. Modern approaches like marker-assisted recurrent selection or genomic selection are suggested for breeding drought tolerance.
Four PsRBOHs had a high probability of localization in the plasma membrane. PsRBOH6 was localized to the plasma membrane and endoplasmic reticulum.
The SQR allele of Xtxp51 had a large positive effect on vigor and emergence in the Tx2794 population. Xtxp51 showed slight genotype by environment interaction in the Wheatland population.
Drought stress threatens crop yield. Basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) transcription factors function in Arabidopsis and rice under drought stress, but are little known in sorghum.

Differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified. Among them, three metabolic pathways were related to β-glucan biosynthesis: xyloglucan:xyloglucosyl transferase activity, starch and sucrose metabolism, and photosynthesis.

Under salt stress, saccharides and amino acids levels elevated in Baiyan 2 (BY2) and Baiyan 5 (BY5). In BY2 exposed to 150 mM NaCl, metabolite concentrations increased significantly: sucrose increased by 38.34-fold, Sophorose increased by 314.15-fold, Isomaltose 2 increased by 25.76-fold.

Sorghum landraces from China exhibit excellent emergence and seedling vigor under cool conditions. They are used as sources of genes for improving seedling cold tolerance in other cultivars. Marker-assisted selection (MAS) could expedite the introgression of genes from landraces into elite lines. However, few studies have demonstrated the efficacy of MAS for quantitatively inherited agronomic traits.

The study maps genomic regions for terminal heat- and drought-stress tolerance in barley. A hundred lines from a ND24260 x Flagship doubled haploid population were evaluated for stay-green expression.

The research evaluated the purity and relationships of the original oats (Avena sativa L.) variety AV-25 and two multi-line sub-populations AV25-T and AV25-S. Both sub-populations are useful as forages in the central highland region of Colombia, which includes the departments of Boyaca and Cundinamarca. They were derived from the original composite mixture Avena 25, used in the mountainsides of the southern department of Narino.
Triploid Miscanthus × giganteus is a sterile perennial grass used for biomass production. A scale standardizing description of M. × giganteus morphological stages has not been developed.

Recent advancements in enhancing crop yield involve research on tiller development using QTL mapping, association studies, and transcriptome analysis across crops. QTL mapping has identified genomic regions associated with tiller development. Genome-wide association studies (GWAS) have provided insights into natural genetic variations.

The expression of AtOAT enhanced drought and salt stress tolerances in wheat by increasing proline content and peroxidase activity. AtOAT expression also provided partial heat stress tolerance in transgenic wheat plants.

Overexpression of SiMYBS3 in Arabidopsis thaliana conferred drought tolerance, ABA insensitivity, and earlier flowering. SiMYBS3 is a drought-related heterotic gene for enhancing drought resistance in crop breeding.
Huairen and Shuozhou are ideal testing environments. YS13, JS15, PS3, and PM4 have excellent yield-trait combinations.

We re-sequenced 206 forage sorghum germplasms and identified 14,570,430 SNPs and 1,967,033 indels. Using SNP markers, we analyzed population genetic structure and identified gene loci related to acid detergent fiber content by genome-wide association analysis (GWAS).
Five major QTLs with PVE >10% were detected: qPH5-2 for PH, qPD5-2 for PD, qPW5-1 for PW, qPC7-1 for PC, and qPC7-2 for PC. The QTL qPH5-2 for PH had a PVE of 30.52%.

Salt exposure increased reactive oxygen species and osmolyte accumulation. Transcriptional reprogramming intensified with higher salinity. Network analysis identified eight regulatory layers corresponding to distinct salt stress stages. It revealed coordinated activation of ethylene and jasmonic acid signaling pathways. Lipid transfer proteins (VrLTPs) were induced under high-salt conditions. VrLTPs co-expressed with transcription factors WRKY, MYB, and NAC. VrLTPs likely function as regulators of late-stage stress adaptation.
Chenopodium quinoa is a halophytic crop with remarkable resistance to harsh growth conditions. However, most commercial cultivars are susceptible to preharvest sprouting.

text: A linkage map was constructed using 12 morphological markers, 87 RFLP, five RAPD, one STS, one IFLP, 33 SSR, and 586 AFLP markers. The map spanned 1,387 cM with an average density of one marker per 1.9 cM.
GWAS identified 77 SNPs associated with waterlogging tolerance during germination. RNA-Seq revealed 1776 DEGs in Liu Leng Zi Da Mai (tolerant) and 839 DEGs in Naso Nijo (sensitive).
Genomic structural mutations, especially deletions, are a source of variation. Structural variations occur in or near genes related to stress response and disease resistance.
Four QTL influenced winter injury scores at Pullman in 1999. Winter hardiness is influenced by several genes and the cumulative effects of winter stress.
Under high temperature stress, FtPYL16 expression dropped to 0.12 and FtPYL17 fell to 0.22. At grain development stages, FtPY15 expression is high at 19 D.

QTLs for kernel morphology and groat percentage were compared to QTLs for chemical grain composition and agronomic traits. Several oat genome regions contained QTL clusters influencing two or more traits.
Long-dormancy-associated alleles at both loci were over-represented in East Asian accessions. Both Qsd1 and Qsd2 had significant genetic effects on seed dormancy in cultivated barley.

Drought stress significantly decreased the unsaturation indices (UI) of digalactosyl-diacylglycerol (DGDG), monogalactosyl-diacylglycerol (MGDG), phosphatidylglycerol (PG) and phosphatidylcholine (PC) in both cultivars, except for DGDG in cv. Kangsi.
The CAMTA family in quinoa has structures and functions. CAMTAs regulate organ development, growth, and drought stress responses.
Jingu 21 was sown in the field in 2022. 31 herbicides were applied. 21 traits, including morphological, physiological, and yield-component traits, were measured to assess millet resistance.

GWAS detected 49, 5, and 25 significant SNP loci with GLM, MLM, and FarmCPU, respectively. Many loci were detected by two or more models. Two candidate genes for drought tolerance were annotated: Sb08g019720.1, homologous to the early flowering MYB protein transcription factor gene in Arabidopsis thaliana; and Sb01g037050.1, homologous to the basic leucine zipper transcription factor gene in maize.
QTL affecting these traits were detected. The two oat populations have different genetic mechanisms for heading date.
Carotenoid content defines foxtail millet varieties. Varieties with different carotenoid levels have distinct genetic features and nutritional profiles.

Metabolite changes were examined in DK19 and K33 during drought stress periods day 7 and day 11. 15 significantly altered metabolites were identified in the materials within amino acid biosynthesis pathways.
Residual atrazine in soil is a major abiotic stress to millet. An atrazine-resistant variety (Gongai2, GA2) and an atrazine-sensitive variety (Longgu31, LG31) were obtained from field experiments.

Three QTLs associated with A: E were identified. Two QTLs are on SBI-09 and one QTL is on SBI-10. They accounted for 17-21% of the phenotypic variation. Subsequent experiments identified 38 QTLs controlling height, flowering, biomass, leaf area, greenness and stomatal density.

Arabidopsis plants overexpressing FtbHLH2 had higher root length and photosynthetic efficiency, and lower malondialdehyde and reactive oxygen species after cold treatment compared to wild type plants. The expression levels of some stress-related genes in transgenic plants were higher than in wild type under normal and stress conditions.
Sorghum is a staple crop in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia. Drought and high temperature can cause significant yield losses in these sorghum production regions.
FtbZIP83, an AREB/ABF gene, was isolated from Tartary buckwheat. FtbZIP83 expression was induced by ABA, NaCl and PEG.

A QTL for wheat grain TKW was detected on chromosome 7AS, flanked by SSR marker Wmc17, using GWAS. BSR-seq analysis of an F2 population with extreme TKW traits identified a candidate trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase gene at 135.0 Mb (CS V1.0), designated TaTPP-7A.

text: Ectopic expression of transcription factors GmTT2A, GmTT2B, GmMYB5A, or R in soybean hairy roots induced accumulation of PAs or anthocyanins. It upregulated 1775, 856, 1411, and 1766 genes, respectively. Several of these genes encode enzymes for PA biosynthesis. Genes regulated by GmTT2A and GmTT2B partially overlapped, suggesting conserved but divergent roles for these TFs in regulating PA accumulation.

DROOPY LEAF1 (DPY1) is a leucine-rich repeat receptor-like kinase. DPY1 regulates plant architecture in foxtail millet (Setaria italica). DPY1 is essential for drought stress acclimation in foxtail millet.
A genome-wide association study (GWAS) for drought tolerance was conducted in cowpea. 331 cowpea genotypes were evaluated for drought tolerance.
SbPLD beta 1 is the target gene of sbi-miR159. SbPLD delta 2 is the target gene of sbi-miR167.

We evaluated 284 sorghum lines under greenhouse conditions and performed SNP genotyping to identify genetic loci associated with drought adaptive traits: shoot transpiration efficiency, shoot biomass, chlorophyll content, and leaf characters. All traits showed significant variation and high heritability.

MSG26 homozygous barley plants progressed normally through Meiosis II to the tetrad stage, but were defective in late uninucleate microspores and developed pollen-less anthers. MSG26 was mapped using seven barley F-2 populations to a 0.02-cM region anchored to a 506-kb low-quality assembly between CAPS markers SP1M14 and SP1M49.

Leaf width was negatively associated with plant transpiration efficiency in lysimetry experiments. Narrow leaves may reduce plant transpiration without biomass accumulation trade-offs. The sorghum diversity panel showed a wide range in largest leaf width, with consistent ranking among sorghum races. Environmental adaptation may modify leaf width.
The Sobic.001G283700 gene encodes glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferases (GPATs) and is closely associated with salt stress tolerance.

Quinoa (Chenopodium quinoa Willd.) is a cash crop. The molecular mechanism of its response to cold stress at the seed germination stage is unknown. A comparative transcriptomic analysis was performed between cold-tolerant cultivar XCq and cold-sensitive cultivar QCq under cold stress.

Four major QTLs for partial rust resistance, Prq1a, Prq1b, Prq2, and Prq7, and three minor QTLs, Prq3, Prq5, and Prq6, were detected. Two major QTLs for flowering time, Ftq1 and Ftq7, and five weaker QTLs, Ftq2, Ftq3, Ftq4, Ftq5, and Ftq6, were revealed.
Pm 2-2 colocalized with panicle compactness. Candidate genes were identified from both loci.

A recombinant inbred line population of 121 lines was derived from the cross between cultivated barley Baudin and wild barley CN4027. 15 NUE traits were measured under hydroponic culture with normal N and low N treatments. Field trials with N sufficiency and N deficiency treatments were conducted in two growing seasons.

text: SiLEA14 overexpression improved Escherichia coli growth under salt stress compared to the control. Transgenic Arabidopsis and foxtail millet plants overexpressing SiLEA14 were obtained to assess its function.
Soil salinity is an abiotic stress limiting crop production. Oat (Avena sativa) is a cereal with salt tolerance.
The genomic heritability of family means was highest for DM, HD and RUST, with estimates of 0.45, 0.74 and 0.73, respectively. Predictive ability increased with the number of SNPs.

Most wax QTLs were associated with pre-anthesis drought tolerant Tx7000. One QTL for WL overlapped with the staygreen QTL Stg2 and was represented by a single marker near the centromeric region on the short arm of SBI-01.
Genome-wide association analysis identified 181 QTNs (LOD >= 4) for nodal root angle (NRA), shoot traits, and RSA across six GWAS models. 73 QTNs were detected by at least two ML-GWAS methods.
F-3 lines were phenotyped for bioenergy-related traits. QTL analysis identified three major plant height QTLs: qPH1.1, qPH7.1, and qPH9.1 on chromosomes 1, 7, and 9.

A population of 220 double haploid lines was developed from a cross of Vlamingh and WABAR2147. Vlamingh showed adult plant resistance (APR) to scald. WABAR2147 showed seedling resistance to a group of isolates. The population was tested for APR to scald under natural infection in two seasons and in a seedling screen with three isolates.
Under drought stress, the test cultivar showed decreased key physiological parameters. These included increased Fv/Fm, Fv/Fo, SPAD value, Pn, Tr, and Gs.

107 of the 300 genotypes produced flowers and fruit. Their yields ranged from 538 to 5132 kg ha(-1). Their 1000-grain weights were between 1.19 g and 2.37 g. These 107 quinoa genotypes were categorized into four groups based on grain yield: below 1500, 1500-2250, 2250-3000, and above 3000 kg ha(-1).

Mutagenesis studies showed that the HMA domain is indispensable for Cd binding. Deleting the HMA domain drastically reduced Cd accumulation and impaired yeast growth. Truncating other domains, such as the isoprenylation motif, had no significant effect. SbHIPP40 is a pivotal Cd accumulator via its HMA domain. This advances understanding of sorghum's Cd detoxification mechanisms and offers a genetic resource for enhancing phytoremediation strategies.

A genome-wide association study (GWAS) used 132 mungbean genotypes for traits including flowering time, YMD resistance, SPAD value, trichome density, and leaf area. Genotyping by sequencing (GBS) identified 31,953 high-quality SNPs across all 11 mungbean chromosomes for the GWAS.

A cDNA (SiDREB2) encoding a putative dehydration-responsive element-binding protein 2 was isolated from foxtail millet. An allele-specific marker (ASM) for dehydration tolerance was developed. The cDNA clone (GenBank accession no. GT090998) codes for a putative DREB2 protein. It was isolated as a differentially expressed gene from a 6 h dehydration stress SSH library.
The heat shock protein 70 (Hsp70) gene from Sorghum bicolor MN1618 is designated SbHsp70-1. Its full-length cDNA is 2524 bp with a 1950 bp open reading frame encoding a 649 amino acid protein.
15 oat varieties were tested for alkali tolerance under four Na2CO3 treatment levels (0, 10, 20, 40 mM). The alkali tolerance of oat seedlings was evaluated.

Sorghum genetic resource collection, selection strategies, and breeding improvement have progressed. Functional genetic studies identified key genes regulating yield-related traits, quality attributes, and tolerance to abiotic and biotic stresses.
SiPRR37 expression changes resulted from a Tc1-Mariner transposon insertion in its first intron. SiPRR37 contributed to adaptation in northeastern ecoregions.

Stay-green QTLs were mapped to physical locations on chromosomes in the sorghum genome. Their intervals ranged from 1.8 to 2.5 Mbp. Stable QTLs were detected at two locations across three consecutive seasons in two mapping populations.
Annual ryegrass (Lolium multiflorum Lam.) and perennial ryegrass (Lolium perenne L.) are two species of ryegrass.
A genetic linkage map for mungbean was constructed using 430 SSR and EST-SSR markers. All markers were mapped onto 11 linkage groups spanning 727.6 cM.

Similar studies in sugarcane identified genes SoMAX2, SoMAX3, SoMAX4-1, SoMAX4-2, and SoTB1. These genes regulate bud outgrowth and tillering. They involve hormonal control through auxins, gibberellins, and cytokinins to coordinate plant responses. These discoveries are essential for breeding and genetic engineering strategies to develop crop varieties with favorable tillering traits.

Co-regulation analysis suggested that DEGs in M79 contributed to a regulatory network involving photosynthesis, signal transduction, transcriptional regulation, redox regulation, hormonal signaling, and osmotic regulation. RNA-seq analysis showed that some photosynthesis-related DEGs were highly expressed in M79 compared to its parental lines under drought stress.

We used 325 PAVs polymorphic between sorghum inbred lines Ji2731 and E-Tian, together with 49 SSR markers, and constructed a genetic map. The map consisted of 10 linkage groups corresponding to the 10 chromosomes of sorghum and spanned 1,430.3 cM covering 97% of the physical genome.

DREB genes code for transcription factors involved in abiotic stress response. Characterizing DREB genes and developing functional markers for effective alleles is important for marker-assisted selection in foxtail millet.
Three predicted genes were annotated for QLB-czas2 and 19 for QLB-czas8, associated with disease resistance. These QTL are new loci conferring resistance to leaf blast.

A genetic linkage map of hexaploid cultivated oat was developed. Homoeologous relationships of linkage groups were identified. Homologous linkage groups were compared between this map and the previously published hexaploid oat map from the cross 'Kanota/Ogle' (KO). 510 markers were assessed on a recombinant inbred population of 152 F-5:6 lines derived from the cross 'Ogle/MAM17-5' (OM). The markers included 172 RFLP, 324 AFLP, and 14 SSR.

Quantitative trait loci (QTLs) for grain quality, yield components and other traits were investigated in two Sorghum caudatum x guinea recombinant inbred line (RIL) populations. Sixteen traits were evaluated and related to two genetic maps using simple (SIM) and composite (CIM) interval mapping.

text: The fungus Magnaporthe grisea causes blast disease, resulting in significant yield losses in cereal crops. Few regulatory genes for blast resistance in grass species have been identified, and the genetic basis of blast resistance in cereals remains elusive.

The study assessed predicting grain sorghum hybrid performance using parental genomic information. One hundred and two public sorghum inbred parents were genotyped. Ninty-nine inbreds were crossed to three tester female parents, generating 204 hybrids for evaluation.

text: The study identified genomic regions controlling heading date and plant height in two hulled by naked oat populations. Recombinant inbred lines and parents were genotyped with a 6K BeadChip Illumina Infinium array and assessed for heading date and plant height.

Integrated analyses showed that Zhonggu2 improves drought resistance by preventing reactive oxygen species accumulation through promoting glycine-serine and flavanone metabolism. Zhonggu2 may also delay phospholipid decomposition to maintain plasma membrane stability under drought stress.

Quantitative real-time PCR and transcriptional activity assays demonstrated that the gene SiMYB56 activates expression of lignin biosynthesis genes under drought conditions. Overexpression of SiMYB56 leads to ABA accumulation in seeds of transgenic rice plants.

We assembled a 4.84-Gb genome sequence of qingke cultivar Zangqing320 using PacBio sequencing and anchored 4.59-Gb to seven chromosomes. Among 46,787 annotated genes, 31,564 were validated by RNA-sequencing data from 39 barley genotypes.

The anthracnose resistance in sorghum line SC112-14 (Cs-SC112) was studied using F2 progenies and recombinant inbred lines against pathotypes from Puerto Rico, Texas, Arkansas, and Georgia. The results show that a series of nearest single loci at the distal region of chromosome 5 control the resistance against pathotypes from Puerto Rico, Texas, and Arkansas.

text: A total of 100 SSR markers among 193 primer pairs detected polymorphism between the two mapping parents of an F-2 population: cultivated S. italica B100 and wild S. viridis A10. Excluding 14 markers with unclear amplifications and five unlinked markers, a foxtail millet SSR linkage map was constructed by integrating 81 new SSR markers with 20 anchored RFLP markers. The 81 SSR markers covered nine foxtail millet chromosomes.
We cloned the WP1 gene using map-based cloning. A white panicle mutant wp1.a had reduced chlorophyll and carotenoid contents in leaf and panicle.
Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) has high tolerance to drought stress. The SNARE gene is related to drought stress in foxtail millet.
Foxtail millet (Setaria italica L.) is a C4 crop with drought resistance. Studying its stress response genes can help understand the molecular mechanisms of this tolerance.

Breeding initiatives target specific traits to produce drought-tolerant lines. Drought tolerance can be improved by integrating desirable traits into superior breeding lines, screening germplasm for resistant lines, and understanding tolerance mechanisms. Molecular techniques like genome editing, sequencing, and transcriptomics can boost drought tolerance in sorghum.

A set of 103 recombinant inbred line (RIL) populations from the Badia and Kavir cross was evaluated under normal, salinity, and drought conditions in 2019. Linkage maps were prepared using 152 SSR markers, 72 ISSR, 7 IRAP, 29 CAAT, 27 SCoT, and 15 iPBS alleles.

Traits 1000-grain weight (TGW), grain weight per spike (GWPS), and dry matter content (DMC) exhibited high-parent heterosis, with TGW reaching 82.54%. P-n correlated positively with T-r, C-i, G(s), RuBP carboxylase, PEP carboxylase, GWPS, TGW, and DMC, suggesting T-r, C-i, and G(s) could aid in identifying high-photosynthesis sorghum varieties.

A morphogene-assisted transformation (MAT) method accelerated transformation times compared to classical callus-based approaches. It also expanded the number of amenable genotypes, including several previously untransformed or recalcitrant ones. Another altruistic transformation advance involved introducing a gene of interest using a separate Agrobacterium strain from the one containing morphogenes, resulting in plants with the gene of interest but without morphogenes.

We constructed 36 transcriptome libraries from four sorghum materials with contrasting germination abilities at three developmental stages. KEGG analysis showed enrichment in metabolic pathways, biosynthesis of secondary metabolites, starch and sucrose metabolism, and plant hormone signal transduction.

Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) has a high-quality reference genome and large mutant libraries based on the Yugu1 variety. However, genetic and mutation mapping tools for forward genetics research on S. italica are lacking.

The copper, zinc, and manganese content was lower in sbgpgl1 leaves than in BTx623. RNA-seq and UPLC-MS/MS identified 4469 differentially expressed genes and 775 differentially accumulated metabolites.

A recombinant inbred line (RIL) population from the cross Baudin x CN4079 was used to evaluate twenty barley yield, agronomy, and nitrogen-related traits under low nitrogen (LN) and normal nitrogen (NN) treatments. Seventeen QTL were identified, including eight QTL expressed under both LN and NN, eight LN-specific QTL, and one NN-specific QTL.
Black barley is caused by phytomelanin synthesized in lemma or pericarp. The trait is controlled by the dominant gene Blp1. The gene Blp1 is mapped on chromosome 1H by molecular markers.

5,020 highly repetitive microsatellite motifs were isolated from the released genome of the genotype 'Yugu1'. 788 SSR primer pairs were designed based on sequence comparison between S. italica and S. viridis.

Establishing gene function is essential for improving sorghum. Identifying genes associated with abiotic stress tolerances in sorghum requires molecular understanding. Over 40% of the sorghum transcriptome was not annotated.

One hundred-thirty QTL formed 22 QTL clusters. Six recombinant inbred lines, RIL35, RIL48, RIL77, RIL80, RIL115 and RIL125, were screened for transgressive inheritance and favorable alleles in plant height, tiller, panicle morphology and yield traits.

The study investigated seed germination traits under control and salt stress conditions in 350 diverse barley accessions. A genome-wide association study using 24,000 genetic markers detected marker-trait associations and candidate genes for salinity tolerance during germination.

SbMYC2 is a bHLH transcription factor in sorghum. SbMYC2 responded significantly to PEG-simulated drought stress and JA treatments. Overexpression of SbMYC2 enhanced drought tolerance in Arabidopsis, rice and sorghum.

Salt-treated Baiyan2 had higher leaf osmotic potential than treated Mengnongda1. Salt-treated Baiyan2 also had higher xylem proline concentration, sheath proline concentration, and proline contribution to sheath osmotic potential than treated Mengnongda1.

text: A total of 29 resistant and 20 susceptible recombinant inbred lines (RILs) were derived from the HC 136 x G 73 cross. These RILs were used for bulked segregant analysis to identify a RAPD marker linked to a gene for anthracnose resistance. The polymorphism between parents HC 136 and G 73 was evaluated using 84 random sequence decamer primers.

The grain number determination in sorghum was studied based on crop growth rate around flowering, reproductive biomass partitioning to the panicle, and grain-set efficiency per unit of panicle biomass. P-R and E-G are genotype-specific traits.

text: Phvul.001G244500 encodes a Basic Helix-Loop-Helix transcription factor. The expression of defense-related genes PR1a, PR1b, and PR2 was modulated in a time-course experiment.
WP1 encodes phytoene synthase. Sequencing and cross tests verified that the white panicle mutant wp1.b is an allelic mutant of wp1.a.

SiC2H2-ZFPs were identified in foxtail millet (Setaria italica v2.0). 67 members were unevenly distributed across all nine chromosomes. Most SiC2H2 proteins were alkaline, stable, and nuclear-localized. SiC2H2-11 and SiC2H2-66 were chloroplast-targeted.
AMMI analysis showed significant genotype and environment effects on yield. G x E PCA identified JS8, PS3, PS6, and PM4 as dominant genotypes.
The drought-tolerant genotype RTx430 shows a genotype-specific drought response in organelle protein profiles. Its organelle-enriched proteins changed abundance upon drought stress.

Target leaf spot (TLS) is a foliar disease of sorghum caused by Bipolaris cookei. It affects grain yield by causing premature leaf drying and defoliation. Two sorghum recombinant inbred line populations, BTx623/BTx642 and BTx623/SC155-14E, were assessed for TLS resistance.

Gene PMA1G03779.1 was identified through GWAS on the Milletdb platform. It modulates yield and responds to environmental stresses. The stress-related PLATZs transcription factors family expands in 87.5% of millet accessions. It contributes to vegetative growth and abiotic stress responses.

Expression of genes in transcriptional regulation, signal transduction and secondary metabolism changed significantly between the two varieties under salt stress. Hundreds of genes involved in the salt stress response were differentially expressed, especially genes encoding receptors like kinases and transcription factors. qRT-PCR analysis of selected DEGs was consistent with RNA-seq results.
Linkage mapping placed the two resistant loci in a 7.7 to 8 cM region on LG7. QTL analysis using field reaction data revealed a major QTL, QPc.FD-AS-AA4, for field resistance.

Senescence-delaying haplotypes of 45 key candidate genes were enriched in lines with prolonged senescence duration. Senescence-promoting haplotypes were enriched in lines with accelerated senescence. Haplotype combinations of these genes explain the segregation of the senescence trait in a recombinant inbred population.

The flavonoid biosynthetic pathway was analyzed at the single-cell level in Tartary buckwheat. The core regulatory factor FtHD2B in Tartary buckwheat roots promotes root stress response by positively regulating flavonoid synthesis.
Twenty-six SNP markers were significantly associated with starch content. These SNP markers were located on chromosomes 1H and 4H, and were loci of qSC1-1 and qSC4-1, respectively.
Avena sativa has greater salt stress tolerance than Avena nuda. The cultivars' salt tolerance ranking is: 'Qinghai 444' > 'Mengyan No. 1' > 'Baiyan No. 18' > 'Qingyin No. 3'.

A blended kinship matrix using pedigree and genomic information was used to optimize multi-trait predictions. Multi-trait genomic analysis using PH information on the training population increased predictive ability for GY by up to 16%.

Sorghum is a model crop for abiotic stress research due to its small genome and tolerance to drought, salinity-alkalinity, and heat. Advances include gapless reference genomes, pan-genome analyses of structural variation, and population resequencing revealing domestication signatures and stress adaptation loci.

There were 11 and 9 DEGs within the location range of qFLW5-2. Combining QTL mapping and RNA-seq, we identified Seita.5g134600 (encoding Aux/IAA) and Seita.5G123900 (encoding cytochrome P450) as candidate genes for qFLW5-2.

GsNAC2-overexpressing sorghum had lower H2O2 and O-2(-) levels, plasma membrane relative permeability, and malondialdehyde (MDA) content. Transcriptome analysis showed a high proportion of differentially-expressed genes (DEGs) participated in defence mechanisms. 18 DEGs were related to synthetic glutathione.
Foxtail millet is stress-tolerant. HKT genes have potential functions in salt tolerance.

The Gllc 527 marker can be used for marker-assisted selection for rust resistance. Additional markers closer to the rust resistance locus are required. The markers linked to the rust resistance gene serve as starting points for map-based cloning of the rust resistance gene.

Expression profiling showed tissue-specific patterns: preferential expression in roots (12 genes), stems (6 genes), leaves (5 genes), and spikes (7 genes). Three genes showed dual peak expression in stems and leaves. Under salt and drought stress, AsEPF/EPFLs are induced, with different genes showing varying expression patterns.

This major QTL overlaps with agronomic traits. The W89001002003 alleles at this locus favor lower leaf Delta C-13, increased plant height, reduced leaf area index, grain yield, harvest index, and days to maturity. This QTL and its associated marker may be useful in breeding programs for improving water use efficiency and yield stability in barley.
One dwarfing gene was from Franklin, which is the same as the denso gene. The other dwarfing gene was from a Chinese landrace variety.

200 genic SSR markers and 100 genomic SSR markers were developed from cowpea unigene and genome sequences. Among them, 27 genic and 27 genomic SSR markers were polymorphic and were used for genetic diversity and population structure assessment in 105 selected cowpea accessions.

Reduced leaf senescence (stay-green) improves tolerance of post-flowering moisture stress in grain sorghum. Quantitative trait loci (QTLs) associated with stay-green have been identified in sorghum to facilitate transfer of this trait into adapted genetic backgrounds.

The study mapped quantitative trait loci (QTL) for drought-resistance traits at seedling stage in Israeli wild barley (Hordeum spontaneum). Mapping used an F-4 population from a cross between genotype WQ23-38 from a xeric environment and MA10-30 from a mesic environment.

Candidate genes include FPA for flowering time (VRADI10G01470; chr. 10), TIR-NBS-LRR for mungbean yellow mosaic India virus resistance (VRADI09G06940; chr. 9), and E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RIE1 for SPAD value (VRADI07G28100; chr. 7).
