text: The barley lines' starch content was evaluated. The average starch content across three growing seasons was 51.5% at the low end and 55.0% at the high end. Population structure affected starch content. Barley in group 2 had low starch content, while barley in group 4 had high starch content.

The study identified the SbMYBHv33 transcription factor, which is downregulated by salt, drought, and ABA in the salt-tolerant sorghum inbred line M-81E. Overexpression of SbMYBHv33 in sorghum significantly reduced biomass accumulation and salinity tolerance at the seedling stage.

STRUCTURE, phylogenetic, and PCoA analyses grouped most Ethiopian and Turkish broomcorn accessions together. Most East Asian accessions from China and South Korea formed another group. Two Sudanese broomcorn individuals were in a distinct group.

Comparative analysis confirmed that the region associated with these three loci is not associated with the anthracnose resistance locus Cg1. Cg1 was previously mapped in the distal region of the same chromosome. The dominant mode of action against pathotypes from Puerto Rico suggests that these resistance sources can be beneficial for sorghum hybrid production.
Sheepgrass seedlings survived when soil water content was reduced to 14.09% in a drought stress experiment.

Four QTLs for flag leaf thickness (FLT) were detected. They are located on chromosomes 2H, 3H, 5H, and 6H. The QTL on 6H had the largest effect, with a maximum LOD of 9.8 explaining up to 20.9% of phenotypic variance.

Increasing NaCl concentrations progressively inhibited plant height in JXD6 seedlings. The unifoliate leaves exhibited elevated leaf surface temperature, increased chlorophyll a, total chlorophyll and carotenoids contents, enhanced O2- accumulation, and heightened superoxide dismutase, peroxidase, and catalase activities.

The resulting frame shift causes a Gly436Gln substitution and forms a new termination site at the 70th triplet of the shifted reading frame. The variant protein is called CYP704B:p.G436Qfs*70. The barley HORVU4Hr1G074840 gene was specifically expressed in anthers.
Sorghum (Sorghum bicolor) has the stay-green trait. A panel of 333 sorghum lines was studied for leaf senescence onset and progression.

SCAR markers SCA 12-1 and SCA 12-2 were designed from the cloned RAPD product sequence. They specifically amplified the RAPD fragment in resistant parent G 73, resistant bulk, and respective RILs. SCAR marker SCA 12 is at the same locus as RAPD marker OPA 12(383) and is linked to the gene for resistance to anthracnose.

Foxtail millet [Setaria italica] is a multigrain crop. Drought is the main abiotic stress limiting foxtail millet yield. Screening for drought-tolerant varieties and identifying drought resistance regulatory genes is significant in foxtail millet breeding.
B-lines in the F-2, BC1F2 and BC2F2 generations were identified by LW7 marker. The segregation ratio of 3:1 for male fertility and male sterility in the progenies supported the one-gene model.

The barley Sdw3 locus (Hvu_sdw3) has extensive synteny with orthologous regions (Osa_sdw3, Sbi_sdw3, Bsy_sdw3) in Oryza sativa, Sorghum bicolor, and Brachypodium sylvaticum. This allowed efficient synteny-based marker saturation in the target interval. Comparative sequence analysis showed colinearity for 23 of the 38, 35, and 29 genes identified in Brachypodium, rice, and Sorghum, respectively.

A core set of 96 diverse common bean genotypes was characterized using 91 genome-wide genomic and genic SSR markers. The study identified 691 alleles ranging from 2 to 21 with an average of 7.59 alleles per locus. This effort aimed to identify genes and QTLs for yield and yield-contributing traits.
Broomcorn millet (Panicum miliaceum L.) is adapted to drought, heat, and salinity. Genes involved in these processes can improve the crop.

Sorghum bicolor faces challenges from drought and salinity. This study evaluates sorghum genotype responses to separate and combined drought and salinity stresses. The experiment measured physiological parameters including stomatal conductance and chlorophyll content.

We revisited data from 895 barley varieties grown in Australia. We used statistical models to examine gene-phenotype associations and two-way epistasis analyses to find novel genes controlling flowering time in barley.

Major QTL for plant height (29.51% PVE) and total biomass yield (16.46% PVE) were detected on chromosome 7. A QTL for stem diameter (9.43% PVE) was detected on chromosome 1. Several QTL for brix were associated with sugar transporter genes.

Pan-genomic variants are important in foxtail millet domestication and improvement. The yield gene SiGW3 has a 366-bp promoter presence/absence variant with gene expression variation. A graph-based genome was developed. Large-scale genetic studies for 68 traits across 13 environments identified potential genes for millet improvement at different geographic sites.

The new method was applied to a Barley dataset. A QTL can affect multiple traits. The new method increased QTL detection power and found 4 more QTLs than the single-trait method. The -logP values were higher and correlated with single-trait analysis.
We identified 45 SbHIPP genes in sorghum. SbHIPP40 was expressed in leaves and roots and upregulated under cadmium exposure.

A high-density linkage map was constructed using 1694 bin markers. A QTL locus controlling hull color (TRT) was identified and named Sihc1 (QTL). Sihc1 is located between markers M33926512 (MRK) and M34281352 (MRK) on chromosome 6 (CHR). It explained 80.26% of the phenotypic variation.
Substitution mapping narrowed qECL7.1 to a 418 kb region flanked by markers TC99_10,211,134 bp and TC102_10,628,880 bp. 35 genes were predicted within the qECL7.1 region.

Collinearity analysis revealed three gene duplication pairs: SiTPS2 and SiTPS9, SiTPS3 and SiTPS8, SiTPS6 and SiTPS7 in foxtail millet. Expression analysis showed SiTPSs and SiTPPs have spatiotemporal specificity and are induced by low temperature.

An F-6:8 RIL population from the cross between WAOAT2132 (Dw6) and Caracas, and the two parents, were used to evaluate the genetic effects of the Dw6 dwarfing gene on plant height and other agronomic traits in oat (Avena sativa L.) across three environments, and to develop closely linked markers for MAS for Dw6. The parents differed in all agronomic traits except whorl number.
Ten barley HvASRs were identified, unevenly distributed on three chromosomes. Barley, wheat, Brachypodium distachyon, rice, maize, foxtail millet, and tomato ASRs were classified into two clusters.

Many MQTL were located on chromosomes 2H and 5H. Chromosome 2H was mainly for water-logging and drought. Chromosome 5H was mainly for salinity and low temperature. Chromosomes 2H and 5H are important for barley abiotic tolerance. Genes associated with abiotic stresses are concentrated on these chromosomes.

Three QTL for winter survival were detected at Haymana in 1997-1998. One QTL for winter survival was detected at Pullman in 1998-1999. Three QTL for winter survival were identified at Haymana in 1999-2000. Only one QTL was common to all environments.

Overexpression of SiASR1 in tobacco increased tolerance to drought and oxidative stresses. SiASR1 expression modulated transcript levels of oxidation-related genes NtSOD, NtAPX, NtCAT, NtRbohA and NtRbohB under drought and oxidative stress conditions.
Under drought stress, FtPYL14 and FtPYL15 expression increased fourfold. Under high temperature stress, FtPYL16 expression dropped to 0.12 and FtPYL17 fell to 0.22.

Genotypes Amarilla Sacaca (A. Sacaca) and QQ57 exhibited high salt tolerance thresholds, low salt sensitivity index (SI), and high Na+ sequestration capacity into vacuoles at the first development stage.

text: Sorghum is sensitive to cold stress in early growth phases. Cold adaptability is crucial for cultivating bioenergy and grain sorghum. Identifying genes that enhance biomass accumulation under early cold stress can improve sorghum varieties.

The study identified morphological and physiological traits associated with drought tolerance in three sorghum varieties: Makueni (MKL) landrace, Gadam (GD) open-pollinated variety, and IESH 22012 hybrid. A randomized split-plot design was used with three sorghum varieties and two moisture levels (control and drought), replicated four times in 2021 and 2022. Rainout shelters imposed drought conditions.
Genotypes of brewing sorghum show specific responses under salt stress. There are variations in gene and metabolite expression among genotypes under high-salt stress.

The QTL on 3H (qRYM-3H/qTFRYM-3H) was confirmed as the BYMD resistance gene eIF4E by haplotype analysis. The QTL on 7H (qRYM-7H/qTFRYM-7H) is a novel QTL. Another QTL on 2H was identified from the DH population.

Genes facilitating starch/sucrose conversion to glucose, fructose, and maltose were upregulated in low-dormancy genotypes. ABA signaling, mediated by SbPP2C33, coordinates carbohydrate mobilization during seed germination in sorghum.

A linkage map with 700 markers was used to identify QTLs. BLUPs were estimated for drought tolerance traits in rainy and post-rainy seasons at Dharwad and post-rainy seasons at Tirupati, and used for single marker analysis, composite interval mapping, and multiple QTL mapping.

Six genes from five subfamilies were characterized for expression responses to abiotic stresses. Overexpression of SiABI5.12 confers osmotic stress tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis thaliana, demonstrating SiABI5 function in abiotic stress response.
Candidate genes can improve salt tolerance in sorghum. Transcriptional profiles explain the salt-tolerance mechanism.

AP2/ERF genes in Arabidopsis, wheat, rice and barley are extensively studied. In quinoa, AP2/ERF genes lacked systematic investigation. This study identified and characterized AP2/ERF genes in quinoa genome-wide. 105 CqAP2/ERF genes were identified. Phylogenetic analysis classified them into 5 subfamilies.
Waxy sorghum seeds have reduced amylose content in starch. A nonfunctional waxy (wx) allele causes waxy endosperms by the absence of granule-bound starch synthase enzyme in sorghum.

Pc39 cosegregated with SNP marker GMI_ES01_c12570_390 in the AC Assiniboia/MN841801 RIL population. It was flanked by SNP markers avgbs_126086.1.41 and GMI_ES15_c276_702, with genetic distances of 1.7 and 0.3 cM, respectively. In the AC Medallion/MN841801 RIL population, similar results were obtained. The flanking markers had genetic distances of 0.4 and 0.4 cM, respectively.
We identified an elite haplotype Hap2 of SiNLP2, which is utilized in breeding. Our study provided gene resources for variety improvement and marker-assisted selection.

A F-2 segregating population was constructed by crossing Yunqiao No.1 (hard-shelled) and Rice-Tartary (easily-shelled). Bulked segregant analysis sequencing (BSA-seq) was used for initial mapping. The gene locus controlling the easily-shelled trait was preliminarily located in a 4.07 Mb region of chromosome 1. Kompetitive allele-specific PCR primers based on SNPs in the initial region were designed and tested in 335 individual plants of the F-2 population.

SNP loci linked to the crown rust resistance gene Pc98 were identified. KASP assays were developed for marker-assisted selection. Crown rust is a damaging disease of oat caused by Puccinia coronata var. avenae f. sp. avenae.

entities.text: Puccinia coronata var. avenae f. sp. avenae
entities.label: BIS

text: New sweet sorghum germplasm selection requires resistance to anthracnose, a disease caused by Colletotrichum sublineolum. Identifying novel anthracnose-resistance sources in sweet sorghum germplasm enables faster development of new resistant germplasm.

SbMYC2 directly activates the expression of the GLUTATHIONE-DISULFIDE REDUCTASE gene SbGR1. SbGR1 silencing weakened drought tolerance in sorghum, and increased ROS accumulation and decreased chlorophyll content in sorghum leaves.

Genomic regions for BYD tolerance were mapped in an oat population of 152 recombinant inbred lines from the cross 'Ogle' (tolerant)/MAM17-5 (sensitive). Tolerance was evaluated in field trials under artificial inoculation with viruliferous aphids harboring BYD virus isolate PAV-IL.

entities.text: viruliferous aphids harboring BYD virus isolate PAV-IL
entities.label: BIS

relations.head: viruliferous aphids harboring BYD virus isolate PAV-IL
relations.label: CON

text: Nucleoredoxin (NRX) is a member of the thioredoxin (TRX) superfamily. It regulates plant growth, development, and stress responses. Its role in drought resistance in foxtail millet is unclear.

Optimal germination occurred at higher temperatures under stress conditions: 20-30°C for annual ryegrass, and 20-30°C and 25-35°C for perennial ryegrass. Germination percentage and germination rate were inhibited by increasing salinity or alkalinity, especially higher alkalinities.

Defense-related genes Sobic.002G013800 (Meta-QTL 2.1), Sobic.005G053800 (Meta-QTL 5.1), Sobic.005G062600 (Meta-QTL 5.1), Sobic.005G075100 (Meta-QTL 5.1), and Sobic.007G027100 (Meta-QTL 7.3) were significantly upregulated in several tissues during different stress conditions in sorghum.

Chromosomal segmental duplication expanded VrBBX genes, with most duplicated pairs undergoing purifying selection. VrBBX genes in the same group share similar structures. Most VrBBX promoters contain stress and hormone response elements.

Segregation was observed for traits including time to wilt, leaf relative elongation rate, recovery rate, shoot regrowth, osmolarity, relative water content, leaf length, and root length. Eighteen putative QTL effects were identified by single-trait analyses and confirmed by multi-trait analysis and multiple interval mapping.

qPH9 was detected in multi-environments using two new F-2 populations from the same F-1 plant. It was narrowed to a 281 kb interval using 1024 recessive F-2 individuals from the same F-1 plant. Marker MRI1016 was significantly correlated with plant height. Seita.9G088900 and Seita.9G089700 are candidate genes for qPH9.
SiPYL3 overexpression increases grain number and grain weight in rice. Haplotype analysis showed SiPYL3HAP4 has higher thousand grain weight than other SiPYL3 haplotypes.

A genetic linkage map was constructed with 118 SSR and 8 INDEL markers. Fourteen QTLs were detected on chromosomes 1, 3, 6, 7, and 9. These included six QTLs for plant height, four QTLs for Brix, and two QTLs each for 100-grain weight and flowering time.

NPBTs aim to overcome traditional breeding limits to obtain new varieties with improved organoleptic traits and resistance to biotic and abiotic stress. Knowledge of the genes controlling a specific trait is essential for NPBTs such as genome editing and cisgenesis.

entities.text: resistance to biotic and abiotic stress
entities.label: TRT

text: The male-sterile allele of HORVU4Hr1G074840 in GSHO745 contains a 4-bp deletion in the last exon. The frame shift causes a Gly436Gln substitution and a new termination site.

The foxtail millet (Setaria italica) ASR gene SiASR4 was cloned. SiASR4 localizes to the cell nucleus, cytoplasm and cytomembrane. The SiASR4 protein contains 102 amino acids and an ABA/WDS (abscisic acid/water-deficit stress) domain, with a molecular mass of 11.5 kDa.

Sixty-six genotypes were resistant to at least one of six races. Twenty-two genotypes were resistant to all six races. These 22 genotypes were resistant to bulk inoculum at the seeding stage and to natural populations of Pca at the adult plant stage.

SNPs recombination analysis indicated markers 2_12850 and 2_00188 flank the cpi gene. These markers are located in linkage group 11 at distances of 6.7 cM and 5.64 cM respectively. Introgression of linkage group 11 with the cowpea reference map revealed short distances from zero to 0.6 cM for these markers, indicating strong association with the cpi gene.

Foxtail millet (Setaria italica L.) has exceptional drought resistance. Its gene SiMBR2 (Seita.7G226600), a member of the MBR family linked to protein ubiquitination, is associated with drought stress.
A multi-environment genome-wide association study was conducted on 440 Southern Oat Association Panel (SOAP) lines. Adult plant resistance was measured as severity (SV) and infection response (IR).
40 QTL controlling traits formed 13 co-located QTL clusters. The candidate gene Seita.9G020100 for qHD9-1 and GA20 oxidase gene Seita.5G404900 for qPH5-1 were identified.
105 M-QTLs with 3.48-33.36% PVE were identified using QTL Cartographer. 65 M-QTLs with 1.3-15.01% PVE were identified using QTLNetwork. 53 M-QTLs were identified using both programmes.

High water uptake varieties have loosely structured starch granules. Low water uptake varieties have compact starch granules. The compactness of starch granules may be associated with seed imbibition.

Oat varieties from European breeding programs showed less diversity than those from North and South America. Associations between AFLP markers and agronomic traits (grain yield, groat yield, panicle emergence, plant height, lodging) and kernel quality traits (kernel weight, test weight, screening percent, groat percent) were investigated.

SbP5CS2 gene is ubiquitously expressed. SbP5CS1 gene is mainly expressed in mature vegetative and reproductive organs. Proline concentration increases after stress application and correlates with SbP5CS expression.

HN16 and its mutated line asm1 were studied. H2O2 was examined for its role in sorghum against aphid. Constitutive H2O2 was the main factor for increased aphid tolerance. Aphid feeding also induced increased H2O2 concentration. Studying ROS scavenging enzymes found APX and GPX were closely related. SOD, POX and CAT were not involved in RMES1-mediated resistance.

A population of 541 lines was used to identify QTL for FHB resistance in a GWAS, and their effect was verified in independent breeding material. The material was tested in six environments over three years at two locations in spawn inoculated and mist irrigated disease trials.
A major QTL for RKN resistance is on sorghum chromosome 3. Multiple resistance genes should be pyramided in a cultivar.

Protein phosphatase 2Cs (PP2Cs) are signaling regulators in plants and play central roles in abscisic acid (ABA)-mediated stress signaling. The PP2C gene family has not been systematically identified and characterized in pea (Pisum sativum). We identified 89 PsPP2C genes.

A weighted gene co-expression network analysis (WGCNA) constructed 34 modules for all expressed genes. Seven modules were closely related to drought treatment. The drought-related module contained 1,343 hub genes, including RAB18, LEA14, and RD22. Cell cycle and DNA replication-related transcriptional pathways were identified as vital regulators of drought tolerance in foxtail millet.

Genetic modeling revealed carotenoid content is controlled by three major-gene pairs plus polygenes (MX3-AI-A model). Major-gene heritability is 96.65% and polygene heritability is 3.35%. Three elite RILs with higher carotenoid and superior agronomic performance were identified for marker-assisted backcrossing.
The grain filling rate (GFR) determines grain yield in foxtail millet. Its regulatory and molecular mechanisms are unclear.

Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) with probes pAs120, AF226603, Ast-R171 and Ast-T116 was used to analyze genome structural diversity and relationships in tetraploid, hexaploid and octoploid Avena species and amphiploids. In A. agadiriana (2n=4x=28), the probes hybridized to all chromosomes, indicating an autotetraploid origin or origin from closely related ancestors with the same genomes.

The alkali tolerance threshold for oats is 10 mM Na2CO3. Strongly alkali-tolerant varieties are Hammer (V1), Monica (V4) and Tyke (V6). Weakly alkali-tolerant varieties are Baiyan 2 (V5) and Apollo (V12).

Ectopic expression of the AsSnRK2D gene in tobacco improved primary root length and plant fresh weight under dehydration and salt stress. The AsSnRK2D-overexpressing tobacco showed improved tolerance to dehydration and salinity stress.
AtRCAR1/2/3 and AtMPK6 directly interact with the FtMYB30 transcription factor. FtMYB30 mediates plant tolerance to salt and drought through an ABA-dependent signaling pathway.
SbPLD alpha 3 expression was high in most tissues. SbPLD alpha 4 expression was mainly in roots.

2683 genes and 160 metabolites had significant differential abundances between control and Cd-treated groups. Multi-omics analysis showed the flavonoid biosynthesis pathway regulates Cd stress responses in sorghum.

Significant QTLs for NS, NDP, SW and PW traits were mapped on LG4 with nearest markers 4181663 and 4175954, explaining 21.9%, 21.8%, 23.5% and 19.9% PVE respectively. Seven consensus QTLs for yield and morphological traits were mapped on LG2, LG3, LG4, LG7A and LG10.
Several mutations of the sorghum GRANULE-BOUND STARCH SYNTHASE (GBSS) gene result in a low amylose:amylopectin starch ratio. Recessive waxy alleles improve starch digestibility.
Linkage maps were constructed with SNPs for CT19 X TX430 and ICSV700 X M81 E populations. QTL mapping identified QTL conferring chilling tolerance at seedling stage.

Different natural growth environments resulted in adaptation, with significant differences in salt tolerance between northern and southern Chinese Tartary buckwheat populations. Using selective sweep scanning and a genome-wide association study, we identified genes responsible for Tartary buckwheat domestication and differentiation, which were experimentally validated.

Dehdrine (DHN) can screen drought tolerance in durra and caudatum races. NADP-Malic Enzyme, Carbonic Anhydrase (CA) and Plasma membrane Intrinsic Protein (PIP2-5) are up-regulated by drought stress only in durra race.

The study used sorghum accessions PI302232 (small grain, Sg) and PI563512 (large grain, Lg). The 1000-grain weight, grain length, and grain width of Lg were higher than Sg. An F2 population was derived from the cross between Sg and Lg parents.

text: Chromosomes 1, 4, 5, and 7 are important for salinity stress. They involve genes controlling stomata length (LR), leaf number (LN), leaf weight (LW), and genetic score (SCR).
AREB/ABFs in bZIP transcription factors are involved in plant drought and salt stress responses. Their properties and functions in Fagopyrum tataricum are poorly understood.

Association studies identified markers significantly associated with lateral root angle (LRA) on chromosomes 2, 6, 7, and 11, length distribution (LED) on chromosome 8, and total root length-growth rate (TRL_GR), volume (VOL), and total dry weight (TDW) on chromosomes 3 and 5. Genes from these regions are potential candidates.
12 QTLs were identified at germination stage. 29 QTLs were identified at seedling stage. Only qGP7-1 for germination percentage was consistent under control and 2.0% NaCl stress.

Two major effect SNPs, S01_72265728 and S05_58213541, explained 35% and 32% of Fe and Zn phenotypic variance, respectively. SNP S01_72265728 is in a cytochrome P450 gene and has a positive effect on kernel Fe accumulation. SNP S05_58213541 is intergenic near gene Sobic.005G134800, a zinc-binding ribosomal protein, and has a negative effect on Zn.

One gene was mapped to chromosome 6H based on seedling tests. Two QTLs, QSc.VlWa.4H and QSc.VlWa.6H, were mapped to chromosomes 4H and 6H based on APR. Epistatic interaction occurred between the two QTLs. Environment/QTL interaction was observed only for QSc.VlWa.6H, which co-segregated with the seedling resistance gene and contributed to basal scald resistance across all growth stages.

To detect molecular markers linked to the sht1 locus, AFLP analysis was used with bulked segregant analysis of progeny from a cross between a non-brittle common buckwheat and a brittle self-compatible buckwheat line. 312 primer combinations were screened and a linkage map around the sht1 locus was constructed using 102 F-2 plants.

We identified 70 B3 proteins in pearl millet and categorized them into ARF, RAV, LAV, and REM subfamilies. We mapped their chromosomal locations and analyzed their gene structures, conserved motifs, and gene duplication events.

SbERF027-overexpressing lines had higher cold tolerance than wild-type plants. SbERF027-silenced lines via VIGS had lower cold tolerance than wild-type plants. SNP-911 of the promoter enhanced cold tolerance by mediating SbERF027 expression.

The major QTL qSiMS6.1 was delimited to a 186-kb interval between markers SiM20 and SiM9. Millet_GLEAN_10020454 in this region is the candidate gene for qSiMS6.1, encoding a male-sterile 5 like protein.

DArT markers detected 30 QTLs for pod and seed traits, explaining 1.8-13.0% phenotypic variation. Major QTLs were identified: qPeN2.2 for peduncle number per plant on chromosome 2, qPoL3 for pod length on chromosome 3, qSB4 for seed breadth on chromosome 4, qSL7.2 for seed length on chromosome 7, and qST9 for seed thickness on chromosome 9.

Cis-acting elements ABRE, MYB, ARE, and STRE were identified in the promoters of HvASRs. The expression of HvASRs was influenced by salt, osmotic, and ABA treatments in barley seedling roots and leaves.

Understanding the genetic function of forage quality-related traits, including crude protein (CP), neutral detergent fiber (NDF), acid detergent fiber (ADF), hemicellulose (HC), and cellulose (CL) contents, is essential for identifying forage quality genes and selecting effective molecular markers in sorghum. In this study, 245 sorghum accessions were genotyped using 85,585 single-nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs), and phenotypic data were obtained from four environments.

The map length was 1010.18 cM across 11 linkage groups. 58 QTLs were detected for plant height, maximum leaf area, biomass, relative water content, days to first flowering, and seed yield, and 5 QTLs for drought tolerance index, across irrigated and drought environments at two locations. 38 QTLs were consistently detected two or more times.

QTLs qPH5A and qMLA2A were consistently identified in intervals from GMES5773 to MUS128 on LG05 and from Mchr11-34 to HAAS_VR_1812 on LG02 across four environments. They contributed 6.40-20.06% and 6.97-7.94% of phenotypic variation, respectively. These QTLs did not share loci with previously identified drought-related loci.

The salt tolerance-related genes are well studied in Arabidopsis and soybean, but limited in mungbean. We mined salt tolerance related genes using the survival rate trait and 160,1405 SNPs in 112 mungbean accessions. VrFRO8 significantly associated with salt-stress were identified in the GWAS analysis.

SiPLC1 is a GENE. It is homologous to AtPLC1 and AtPLC3 of Arabidopsis thaliana and to OsPLC4 of Oryza sativa. SiPLC1 is predominantly expressed in roots during the early stem elongation GST. Its expression is upregulated under salt stress ABS.
We identified 7 SiHKT genes in foxtail millet. Phylogenetic analysis, conserved motif prediction, and gene structure analysis classified these genes into two subfamilies.

The alleles per locus ranged from one for CEDG006, CEDG010, CEDG050, CEDG088, CEDG092 and CEDG232 to three for CEDG214. The average was 1.5 alleles per primer. The expected heterozygosity index was 0.29 ranging from 0.15 to 0.49.
CqGST genes are distributed on all chromosomes except chromosome 09. Chromosome 07 contains 20 CqGST genes. CqGSTL1 and CqGSTU1 cannot be mapped to specific chromosomes.

F-2 and F-3 populations from a cross between Sorghum virgatum and domesticated sorghum were used. Human selection reshaped sorghum through QTLs with large genetic effects in harvest, plant architecture and grain taste traits, including reduction of shattering, few branches, short stature, and removal of polyphenols from seed. Seed width expansion was selected through small-effect QTLs to improve yield.

Plants with drought tolerance QTLs and higher recurrent parent genome were selfed to produce BC4F3. Under non-irrigated conditions, BC4F3 lines with introgressed QTLs had higher yield than both parents.

VrFRO8 reduces SOD contents by influencing the Fe2+/Fe3+ ratio under salt stress. This study mined a key gene associated with salt tolerance and constructed a VrFRO8-related PPI network for salt tolerance.
Two barley genotypes, XZ20 and XZ25, with different total phenolics content were used. Drought affected 118 phenolic metabolites during the grain developing stage.

A comparative transcriptome analysis was performed on wild types, landraces, and improved cultivars of broomcorn millet at seeding and filling stages. The analysis identified genes selected during domestication and improvement. Variations in gene expression patterns between wild types and landraces, and between landraces and improved cultivars, were evaluated to explore molecular mechanisms of domestication and improvement.

The malondialdehyde (MDA) content of the sinrx1 mutant was significantly higher than that of the WT. The drought resistance of the sinrx1 mutant decreased significantly compared to the WT during germination and seedling stages.

Crown rust is caused by Puccinia coronata f. sp. avenae Eriks. It is a serious problem for oat production. Pyramiding multiple resistance genes into new cultivars is a key breeding objective. Many race-specific resistance genes have been mapped. Markers closely linked to these genes have been identified.

entities.text: Puccinia coronata f. sp. avenae Eriks
entities.label: BIS

text: However, this gene is temperature sensitive and linked to short spikes, awns, and high grain density, resembling a brh gene. To use it in breeding, the linkage between the dwarfing gene and the unfavorable spike traits must be broken.
SiWRKY89-overexpressing plants had higher expression levels of abiotic stress-responsive genes than controls. SiWRKY89 binds to the W-box elements of AtNCED3.
Foxtail millet has a small diploid genome and better adaptability to arid lands. We identified 29 putative HAK/KUP/KT proteins from the Setaria italica genome database.

The TCP gene family members were identified and analyzed in three oat varieties: "SFS", "Sang", and "OT3098v2". Their responses to drought and salt stresses were investigated. "SFS" has 83 TCP genes, "Sang" has 65, and "OT3098v2" has 30. "SFS" has 22 specific TCP genes. The oat TCP genes are classified into the PCF, CIN, and CYC/TB1 subfamilies.

Six environmentally stable QTLs for GPC were detected on chromosomes 2H, 4H, 6H, and 7H, with increasing alleles from ZGMLEL. Three QTLs on chromosome 7H (QGpc.ZiSc-7H.1, QGpc.ZiSc-7H.2, QGpc.ZiSc-7H.3) linked in coupling phase were identified.
The dwarfing gene from Franklin was more effective in early sowing trials. The dwarfing gene from TX9425 was more effective in later sown trials.

Holo-omics study interactions among fungi in sorghum. The most abundant fungal endophyte genome was assembled from rhizospheric metagenomic sequences. Its metatranscriptome was compared with the host plant metabolome and transcriptome under drought.

Quantitative Trait Loci (QTL) analysis used 252 co-dominant and dominant markers on 132 lentil recombinant inbred lines under progressive drought-stressed conditions. Eighteen QTLs controlling 14 root and shoot traits were identified.

Two accessions, ZQ25 and GK5, with significantly different grain size, were selected for transcriptome analysis. Differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified at 21 days post anthesis (DPA) and 28 DPA. There were 3,733 DEGs at 21 DPA and 4,396 DEGs at 28 DPA. These DEGs were enriched in photosynthesis-antenna proteins, phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, and flavonoid biosynthesis.
Under drought conditions, root length increased significantly for both varieties ('Zhonggu2' > 'An04'). K+, Ca2+ and NO3- content increased significantly in Zhonggu2.

A total of 221 QTL for 17 morpho-agronomic and yield-related traits were identified. Of these, 109 QTL were detected in multiple environments, including the stable qLMS6.1. qLMS6.1 harbors the candidate gene Seita.6G250500. 70 QTL were repeatedly identified in different trials at the same location.

Sorghum plant color is leaf sheath and leaf color. It was evaluated in the sorghum mini core collection by scoring leaf sheath and leaf color at maturity as tan, red, or purple across three environments. Genome-wide association mapping was performed with 6,094,317 SNP markers.

entities.text: leaf sheath and leaf color at maturity
entities.label: TRT

relations.head: leaf sheath and leaf color at maturity
relations.label: OCI

text: Cluster analysis classified genotypes into four groups based on grain yield association. Acc. #28546 and Acc. #216739 were highlighted as highly drought tolerant across environments. The study identified 32 QTNs for drought indices and grain yield under stress conditions, and 22 QTNs under non-stress conditions, at two locations. Five common QTNs were linked to multiple drought indices.

Genetics of domestication in zombi pea were investigated by QTL analysis using an F-2 population of 139 plants from a cross between cultivated V. vexillata (JP235863) and wild V. vexillata (AusTRCF66514). A linkage map with 11 linkage groups was constructed from the F-2 population using 145 SSR, 117 RAD-seq and 2 morphological markers.
105 QTLs and genes for 38 traits were identified. Many linkage groups were important in mungbean domestication.

SbHKT1;4 is a HKT gene family member from Sorghum bicolor. Under Na+ stress, SbHKT1;4 expression was more strongly upregulated in a salt-tolerant sorghum accession. This upregulation correlated with a better balanced Na+/K+ ratio and enhanced plant growth.

Comparative analysis of the early (S2) to late (S4) grain-filling stage revealed 108 co-regulated metabolites, 78 JG21-exclusive metabolites, and 72 NMB-specific metabolites. These differential metabolites exhibited distinct accumulation patterns. They were enriched in flavonoid and phenylpropanoid pathways between cultivars, suggesting roles in modulating color, nutritional quality, and grain texture.

Three genomic regions contained QTLs for seed weight and days to flowering, linking the traits. Comparing these QTLs with those in azuki bean [Vigna angularis] and rice bean [Vigna umbellata] showed conservation across the three Vigna species.

Overexpression of FtPMEI13 improves drought tolerance by altering antioxidant enzyme activities and osmotically active metabolite levels. FtPMEI13 interacts with pectin methylesterase (PME) and inhibits PME activity under drought stress.
BC2F4 lines were evaluated under irrigated and drought conditions and three rainfed environments. Under drought, the grain yield of C-MAB-introgression lines was higher than recurrent parents.

GWAS using SNP markers identified two genomic loci on chromosomes 2 and 9 associated with blast disease resistance in foxtail millet. Eight putative genes were identified using rice blast transcriptomic data.
Seed width expansion was selected to improve yield by accumulating small-effect QTLs. Two major QTLs for plant height (QTI-phl and dw1) were narrowed down to 24.5 kb and 13.9 kb.
SbMYC2 interacts with SbJAZs, suppresses JA signaling, and activates SbGR1 to regulate sorghum's drought tolerance. bHLH TF SbMYC2 plays a role in sorghum's response to drought stress.
In silico analysis showed SiSNAREs were expressed in specific tissues or development stages. Foxtail millet cultivars Jingu21 and Longgu16 showed different phenotypes under drought stress.

An F-1 segregating population of 217 individuals was established from a cross between Ukraine daliqiao (UD) and Youqiao 2 (YQ2), which differ in grain size. InDel and SSR primers were developed based on parent transcriptome sequencing.
A meta-analysis of QTL for abiotic stress tolerance in barley was performed using 35 experiments. MetaQTL software projected QTL positions on a reference map.

The map length was 1,654 cM with an average marker interval of 16.4 cM. The 81 SSR markers were unevenly distributed across nine chromosomes. Chromosome 8 had the fewest markers (3) and chromosome 9 had the most (18). To verify their usefulness, 37 SSR markers were randomly selected to analyze genetic diversity in 40 foxtail millet accessions.

Sorghum has lower water requirements and compact growth in accessions like TX430 and BTx623, making it practical for genome editing studies. Maize shares close genetic similarity with Sorghum and belongs to the Panicoideae subfamily, so it could benefit from Sorghum-based insights.

Transcriptome profiling and qPCR validation in the YuGu 56 cultivar identified stress-responsive candidates SiC2H2-35 and SiC2H2-58 for salt tolerance, and SiC2H2-23 with 5.19-fold induction under salt stress and SiC2H2-32 with 5.47-fold induction under drought. This study provides insights into the C2H2-ZFP family in foxtail millet and highlights potential genetic markers for improving stress resilience through molecular breeding.
Sorghum is susceptible to fungal diseases. Understanding the genetic control of disease resistance is essential for developing resistant sorghum cultivars.

Perenniality was introduced from S. halepense into S. bicolor. QTL affecting eight seed yield-related traits and their linkage relationships were identified. Interval mapping in the BC1F2 population from S. bicolor x S. halepense revealed 80 QTL with LOD > 2.5 for the eight traits, ranging from 1 to 13 QTL per trait.

text: Abiotic stress affects quinoa growth and yield. Novel abiotic stress resistant genes are needed for quinoa breeding.
Two significant markers were adjacent to a beta-glucan candidate gene in the rice genome. GWAS can be used for QTL detection and marker-assisted selection to improve beta-glucan concentration in oat.

Transcriptomic data analysis identified seven differentially expressed AsaPP2C genes: Asa_chr6Dg00217, Asa_chr6Ag01950, Asa_chr3Ag01998, Asa_chr5Ag00079, Asa_chr4Cg03270, Asa_chr6Cg02197, and Asa_chr7Dg02992. These genes exhibited dynamic expression patterns under varying stress conditions, with time-dependent and stress-dependent transcriptional responses.

RFLP genotyping of the Dw6 NILs showed that 13 of the 14 lines were homogeneously maternal or paternal near Dw6/dw6. The cDNA clone aco245 codes for a vacuolar proton ATPase subunit H, a candidate gene for Dw6.

This review focuses on advances in plant protection using CRISPR/Cas9 technology in crops against viral, fungal, and bacterial diseases. For viral disease resistance, strategies in model species like Arabidopsis and Nicotiana benthamiana are discussed. These include integrating CRISPR sequences that target the viral genome and inducing CRISPR-mediated mutations in the host plant genome.

Sixteen metabolites were enriched in the phenylpropanoid biosynthesis pathway. Seventeen metabolites were enriched in the flavonoids biosynthesis pathway. Six genes related to amino acid metabolism and 12 key genes associated with secondary metabolism showed differential expression.
Two QTL were identified for yield and 'staygreen' under post-flowering drought. These QTL were also associated with yield under fully irrigated conditions.
Linkage analysis located Pg2 on linkage group Mrg20 of the oat consensus genetic map. Predictive markers were converted to KASP assays for oat breeding.
Two pyramided backcross lines H3 and H4 were homozygous at all five marker loci in BC4F4 and BC4F5 generations. Their recurrent parent genome recovery ranged from 96.4% to 100.0%.
Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) is a C4 crop. Its genome was sequenced in 2012, but lacked high-density genetic maps for agronomic and yield traits.

Sixty-two QTLs were identified for 39 traits across 10 LGs. Major QTLs were identified for late flowering inherited from ACC 1 (6 QTLs, PVE 11.2-29.9%) and perenniality inherited from ACC 87 (3 QTLs, PVE 17.4-22.6%) in separate population analyses.
Two distinct genetic sub-populations were identified. GWAS with 2,760,264 SNPs was conducted in four environments to investigate the genetic basis of natural variation in sorghum grain color.

MicroRNAs are endogenous small RNAs with regulatory roles in plant development and stress response. We used Illumina sequencing to investigate drought-responsive miRNAs from S. italica inbred An04-4783 seedlings under control and drought conditions.

Three segregation distortion regions were located on chromosomes 2H, 3H, and 5H using RFLP and morphological markers. This distortion indicates preferential gametic selection during doubled-haploid line development.

Genotypes with the one-kbp insertion of the HvAACT1 gene had high mean performance for all measured traits. Lines without the insertion had the lowest mean value. Marker-assisted selection accelerates selection of acid-tolerant barley lines.
GWAS identified 24 SNPs associated with traits: HT (3), TN (4), PL (4), Brix (3), RS (10). Seven SNPs were novel: HT (1), TN (1), Brix (1), RS (4).

Ten Shaker K+ channel genes were identified in foxtail millet. They were divided into five groups by phylogenetic analysis. Gene structures, chromosome locations, cis-acting regulatory elements in promoters, and post-translation modification sites of the Shaker K+ channels were analyzed.

Genome-wide analysis and expression profiling identified CqNAC79 and CqHIPP34 regulated by CqZF-HD14. Bimolecular fluorescence complementation determined their interactions. Physiological and biochemical analyses and transient overexpression revealed that CqNAC79 and CqHIPP34 resist drought by promoting photosynthetic pigment accumulation and maintaining antioxidant capacity under drought stress.

Under salt-stress conditions, SbWRKY50 over-expression plants showed decreased germination rate, root length, biomass, and potassium ion content, and increased hydrogen peroxide, superoxide anion, and sodium ion content. Real-time PCR showed decreased expression of AtSOS1, AtHKT1, and osmotic and oxidative stress-related genes in the over-expression strains under salt stress.
Drought stress challenges global agriculture. Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) is a C4 crop native to China, a coarse grain crop in arid regions and a model for studying drought adaptation.

We identified eight MAPKK and 49 MAPKKK genes in sorghum. We characterized 16 sorghum MAPK genes. RNA-seq analysis revealed that 10 MAPK cascade genes were involved in drought stress response in sorghum.

A genome-wide association study identified SNP site Ft4:1777524 as associated with rutin levels. In the 400 kb candidate region, 27 key genes were identified. Transcriptome analysis revealed that FtS1Fa1 is linked to RC.

'Dingku-1' and 'Xiqiao-2' accumulated soluble sugars and proline, respectively, to maintain osmotic balance under drought stress. 'Xiqiao-2' exhibited elevated flavonoids and polyphenols in leaves, mitigating oxidative damage from drought.

QTL analysis detected two major and two minor QTLs for PHS tolerance in a segregating population from the cross between PHS-tolerant 'Kyukei 29' and susceptible 'Kyukei SC7'. Different major and minor QTLs were found in other segregating populations from PHS-tolerant lines 'Kyukei 28' and 'NARO-FE-1'. Candidate markers linked to PHS were located near these QTL regions.

text: Exogenous ABA application under low temperature-drought stress activated the PYR/PYL-PP2C-SnRK2 cascade, enhanced antioxidant defenses (SOD, POD, CAT), and increased the ratio of soluble sugars to starch. The ABA-responsive antioxidant module (MEwhite, 110 genes), identified via WGCNA, coordinates redox homeostasis and carbohydrate metabolism.

155 alleles were identified, averaging 2.9 alleles per marker, with a PIC average of 0.3615. Genomic SSRs had a higher PIC (0.3996) than genic SSRs (0.3235). Most polymorphic genomic SSRs were di- and trinucleotide repeats (51.9% and 37.0% respectively).

Five stable QTLs were identified in at least two environments using MLM-GWAS. One candidate gene, SETIT_006045mg (Chr4: 744,701-746,852), enhances grain weight per panicle. It was identified by homologous gene comparison and gene expression analysis, and validated by haplotype analysis of 330 accessions.
Eight NAC-like genes, FtNAC2 through FtNAC9, were isolated from Tartary buckwheat. All FtNAC proteins contain the typical NAM domain of the NAC TFs family in the N terminus.

The CqHAKs family members were analyzed for evolutionary relationships, physicochemical properties, conserved domains, motifs, gene structure, and cis-regulatory elements. Transcription data analysis showed that CqHAKs have diverse expression patterns in different tissues and in response to drought, heat, low phosphorus, and salt stresses.
SiZIPs show spatiotemporal expression in foxtail millet. Multiple SiZIPs respond to Fe, Cd, Na, and K metal ions, drought, and cold stresses.

SiHAK1 is expressed in various tissues and up-regulated in shoots under low K condition. SiHAK1 is localized in the cell membrane and mediates high-affinity K+ uptake in Cy162 yeast cells and Arabidopsis athak5 mutants under low K.
SiASR4 expression increased in SiARDP-overexpressing plants. SiASR4-transgenic Arabidopsis and SiASR4-overexpressing foxtail millet showed enhanced tolerance to drought and salt stress.

SNP marker assays were tested in 125 A. strigosa accessions rated for crown rust resistance. SNP marker GMI_ES17_c6425_188 is a candidate for marker-assisted selection, in addition to marker GMI_ES02_c37788_255.

Differentially expressed proteins unique to JK3 were enriched in metabolic pathways. Integrated proteomic and transcriptomic analysis revealed 17 co-upregulated differentially expressed genes exclusive to JK3.

Heat and drought adaptive QTL were identified in a spring bread wheat population from the Seri/Babax cross. The population was grown in environments with natural heat and drought stress in Mexico, West Asia, North Africa, and South Asia.
Foxtail millet (Setaria italica L.) has efficient nutrient utilization and robust abiotic stress responses. Its molecular mechanisms for biotic stress tolerance are poorly understood.
Sorghum is a drought-tolerant grain crop. This study explores the drought tolerance mechanisms of sorghum.

Leaf width correlated with plant-level transpiration efficiency and associated with 19 QTL in sorghum. Reducing transpiration without compromising photosynthesis and yield enhances plant transpiration efficiency, a desirable selection target in crop improvement.
Qianku 3 had a higher seed-setting rate than Liuku 3 in 2017 and 2018. Qianku 3 also had a higher filled grain number and seed-setting rate on branch than Liuku 3.

Natural variation analysis identified SiYTH1(AGTG) as the dominant allele for drought tolerance in foxtail millet. This study offers insights into m(6) A reader-mediated drought tolerance and provides a genetic resource for improving drought tolerance in foxtail millet breeding.
Tartary buckwheat (Fagopyrum tataricum) has adaptation to low nitrogen condition. The plasticity of roots drives this adaptation.

Expression analysis showed that FeRDEB1 gene transcript levels increased rapidly after low-/high-temperature treatment, drought stress, and exogenous ABA treatment. Over-expression of FeDREB1 gene enhanced drought and freezing tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis but caused growth retardation.
The C2 cluster contained QTL for yield, agronomic, and N-related traits. Among novel QTL, Qstna.sau-5H and Qnhi.sau-5H expression was unaffected by N treatment.

Breeding for BYD tolerance controls the disease. Characterizing major tolerance sources and identifying marker-trait associations benefits BYD tolerance breeding. Genomic regions for BYD tolerance were mapped in an oat population of 152 recombinant inbred lines from the cross 'Ogle' (tolerant)/MAM17-5 (sensitive).

Droughts negatively affect sorghum's productivity and nutritional quality. However, resilient genotypes exist across its diversity centers. These genotypes function differently under drought stress at molecular and physiological levels.

GWAS identified seven SNP markers. SNP2 and SNP3 positively correlated with beta-glucan. SNP1, SNP4, SNP5, SNP6, and SNP7 negatively correlated with beta-glucan. Candidate genes HORVU7Hr1G000320 and HORVU7Hr1G000040 are linked to SNP7 and belong to the nucleotide triphosphate hydrolase superfamily, which may affect beta-glucan synthase activity.
SiLTP ectopic expression in tobacco increased salt and drought tolerance compared to the wild type (WT). SiLTP overexpression (OE) and RNA interference (RNAi) transgenic foxtail millet were obtained.
ML and GWAS were integrated to assess GP efficiency for seven key agronomic traits in 195 oat cultivars. GWAS identified 94 trait-associated SNPs.
Waxy sorghum (wx) results from mutation of granule-bound starch synthase I (GBSS I). Several mutant types exist, carrying wxa, wxb, and wxc waxy alleles.

Four loci for flag leaf thickness (FLT) were detected in barley. The loci are located on chromosome arms 2H, 3H, 5H and 6H. They were identified by assessing 201 recombinant inbred lines in field and glasshouse trials across seasons.
Ninety-five forage sorghum lines were evaluated under five climatic conditions over two years. The study identified stable genotypes with high forage yields and key yield traits for sorghum breeding.
Transgenic rice SbHIPP40 overexpressing accumulated higher Cd in stems, leaves, and grains compared to wild-type plants. SbHIPP40 modulates key pathways in signal transduction and stress responses.

The highest number of AsTCP collinear relationships is in "SFS" with 89 pairs. Under drought and salt stress, gene expression differed significantly among oat cultivars and treatment periods. Genes AsTCP021, AsTCP033, AsTCP044, AsTCP053, and AsTCP058 showed significant expression changes under both treatments and may play important roles in oat's response to abiotic stresses.

The average alleles per locus was 2.66, indicating low polymorphism. The polymorphic information content (PIC) of gSSRs was higher than eSSRs. Most polymorphic gSSRs were di- and tri-nucleotide repeats (52.4% and 38.1% of loci, respectively). Cluster analysis differentiated genotypes into nine subgroups, separating wild mung bean accessions from cultivated accessions.

The predominant race was TJD. TJD was virulent against Pg1, Pg2, Pg3, Pg4, Pg8, Pg9, and Pg15. TJD accounted for 35.8% in 2018 and 37.8% in 2019. The sub-predominant races were TJN and TKN. TJN accounted for 30.2% in 2018 and 28.3% in 2019. TKN accounted for 20.8% in 2018 and 12.3% in 2019. All isolates were virulent to Pg1, Pg2, Pg3, and Pg4. All isolates were avirulent to Pg6 and Pg16.

35,216 simple sequence repeats (SSRs) were identified in 27,055 unigene sequences, with trinucleotides as the most abundant repeat unit at 66.72%. 292 differentially expressed genes were identified between the two genotypes, enriched in 88 GO terms and 12 KEGG pathways.

SbMYBHV33 overexpression promoted ABA pathway genes in sorghum and inhibited growth. Under salt stress, SbMYBHV33 expression decreased in overexpressed lines, and the promotion of ABA pathway genes was attenuated.

Quantitative real-time PCR analysis showed that genes encoding major enzymes in lignin and suberin biosynthesis pathways had higher expression in SiMYB16 transgenic plants. The content of flavonoid and lignin, and the activity of fatty acid synthase increased in SiMYB16 transgenic rice compared with wild-type plants under salt stress. SiMYB16 gene enhances plant salt tolerance by regulating lignin and suberin biosynthesis.

GmTT2A and GmTT2B upregulated anthocyanidin reductase. GmMYB5A upregulated leucoanthocyanidin reductase. Overexpression of GmTT2B in transgenic soybean increased proanthocyanidin and anthocyanin accumulation. Overexpression of MtLAP1 in transgenic soybean also increased proanthocyanidin and anthocyanin accumulation. This was associated with transcriptional reprogramming.

CRISPR/Cas9 genome editing disrupted the symbiotic nitrogen fixation (SNF) gene in Vigna unguiculata. Guide RNAs targeting symbiosis receptor-like kinase (SYMRK) achieved about 67% mutagenic efficiency in hairy-root-transformed plants, and nodule formation was blocked in mutants.
The VrPR gene was cloned and overexpressed in Arabidopsis thaliana for functional validation. The ORF of VrPR contains 1200 bp and encodes 399 amino acids.

Using a RIL population from a foxtail millet backbone breeding line and an elite cultivar, a high-density bin map was constructed. Six novel multi-environment effect QTLs and seven candidate genes for dwarf phenotype were identified. Plant height is an important trait for yield potential.

Genome-wide association studies identify genetic loci linked to grain micronutrient traits, including high iron content. These resources support molecular breeding and marker-assisted selection to improve little millet.

Previous reports related yield promoting effects to the region harboring the flowering locus eps2 (barley HvCEN gene). Sequencing of a 1025 bp fragment encompassing the HvCEN coding region revealed no Single Nucleotide Polymorphism (SNP) variation in our parents and near-isogenic lines, ruling out direct involvement of eps2. These findings pointed to another flowering locus in the QTL region.

Sorghum is used for food, feed, and biofuel. Grain color is a conventional proxy for selecting antioxidants polyphenols in sorghum grain. This use was hampered by inconsistency between grain color and antioxidants concentration.

ABA treatment increased fresh weight, root vigor, leaf SOD, POD, CAT activity and reduced leaf plasma membrane permeability in Tartary buckwheat under salt stress. Optimal ABA concentration for Chuanqiao No. 1 was 40 mu M and for Chuanqiao No. 2 was 10 mu M. ABA treatment promoted expression of FtNHX1, FtSOS1, potassium channel and four ABA receptor-related genes in stem and root of Tartary buckwheat.
Milling yield is an important quality trait of cultivated oat (Avena sativa L.). Kernel morphology and groat percentage influence milling yield.
Some QTLs for BYD tolerance are linked to QTLs for plant height and days to heading. Problems with QTL mapping for BYD tolerance are discussed.

GWAS detected 46 SNPs associated with traits. An SNP on chromosome 6 is linked to lysine with a phenotypic contribution of 49.62%. Candidate gene analysis identified 138 genes within 100 kb of significant QTLs. These genes are involved in metabolic and biosynthetic pathways including amino acid and carbohydrate metabolism.

122 doubled haploid lines from a cross between cultivars Huaai 11 and Huadamai 6 were genotyped by 9680 SNPs and phenotyped for 14 agronomic traits. 913 quantitative trait nucleotides (QTNs) were identified by five multi-locus GWAS methods to be associated with the 14 traits.

Kompetitive Allele-Specific PCR assays were designed for Pc39-linked SNP loci. Two SNP loci defined a haplotype that predicted Pc39 status in oat germplasm and will be useful for marker-assisted selection.

The study dissected resistance into QTL using phenotypic data from four environments and a 162 microsatellite marker linkage map from 168 RILs of cross 296B x IS18551. For five component traits across four environments, 29 QTL were detected by multiple QTL mapping: four for leaf glossiness, four for seedling vigor, seven for oviposition, six for deadhearts, two for adaxial trichome density, and six for abaxial trichome density.

Orthologous and pathway-driven analyses identified 14 candidate genes for GPC and five candidate genes for GFC within the QTL regions. The five most promising candidates are Seita.5G398800, Seita.5G401000, Seita.9G026300, Seita.5G429700, and Seita.3G015600. Haplotype analysis of 577 foxtail millet accessions revealed strong correlations between two genes (Seita.3G015600 and Seita.5G429700) and GFC.

The study describes SSR frequency, type, and distribution in mungbean's genic region and reports the development of a large set of SSR markers. These genic SSR markers are a genomic resource for qualitative and quantitative traits mapping, marker-assisted selection, and genetic diversity studies in mungbean and related Vigna species.

Post-anthesis drought adversely impacted the growth, morphology, and biomass accumulation of Tartary buckwheat. Drought stress enhanced the maximum photosynthetic capacity (Fv/Fm) and light protection ability (NPQ) of the 'Xiqiao-2' genotype.

69 C2 domain genes belonging to 5 groups were identified in the sorghum genome. The C2 family genes were localized on 10 chromosomes. 3 tandem repeat genes and 1 pair of repeat gene fragments were detected.

Association analysis identified 38 significant marker-trait associations under GLM and 31 under MLM. Five markers were associated with crude protein content, three with beta-glucan content, and seven with yield traits.
Twenty-four QTL-by-environment interactions and three epistatic interactions were detected. The locus N1 controlling the covered/hulless character affected most traits.

Sorghum production is affected by anthracnose disease caused by Colletotrichum sublineolum. New sources of anthracnose resistance should be identified to introgress novel genes into susceptible varieties.
Genotype significantly affected A, E, and A:E. In Experiment 1, A:E values ranged from 1.2-4.4 mmol CO2 mol(-1) H2O kPa(-1) under 40% FC to 1.6-3.1 mmol CO2 mol(-1) H2O kPa(-1) under 80% FC.
Foxtail millet cultivar H138 was used for RNA-seq analysis. The drought-induced transcriptomic data showed that CDPKs were up- or down-regulated by drought.

The provisional name Qph-7H is suggested for this QTL. Qph-7H from ZAU 7 reduces plant height to about 3/4 of normal. Qph-7H is considered a semi-dwarfing gene. Other QTLs for plant height were found, but their expression was variable across eight environments.

98.9% of naked barley accessions were from East Asia. A deletion in the barley ERF gene explains compromised adhesive caryopsis formation in this collection. This trait can be selected with a new co-dominant diagnostic marker.

Seventy-three QTL were associated with exocarp color, mesocarp color, testa color, and tannin content on all chromosomes except chromosome 5. Forty-seven of these QTL may be novel. Some important QTL colocalized with orthologous genes in the flavonoid biosynthetic pathway from other plants. These include orthologs of Arabidopsis thaliana genes TT2, TT7, TT12, TT16, and AT5G41220 (GST), and orthologs of rice genes MYB61 and OsbHLH025.

Seed dormancy is a key domestication trait for major crops. It is acquired in long-term development processes and enables plant survival in adverse conditions. Seed dormancy is a complex trait under polygenic control and is affected by endogenous and environmental factors.

The salt-tolerant cultivar Vao-9 enhances carbohydrate and energy metabolism through the pentose and glucuronate interconversions and carbon fixation pathways in prokaryotes, reducing salt stress damage. Down-regulation of ribosomes and up-regulated expression of HSPs and CRT occur through the regulation of protein synthesis in response to salt stress.

The three-line method is a breeding strategy to obtain hybrid F1 offspring. The study used sorghum variety Liangnuo No.1 (male sterile), glutinous sorghum variety SB6 from Taiwan (carrying the wxc waxy allele), and sorghum reference genome variety BTx623. The goal was to produce waxy sorghums, including the male sterile line (A-line), male sterile maintenance line (B-line), and fertility-restoring line (R-line).

Four mungbean F-5 recombinant inbred lines (RILs) from crosses of two wild types (ACC 1, ACC 87) and two cultivars (Berken, Kiloga) were used with DArT markers to construct linkage maps and identify QTLs associated with 54 traits. Polymorphic DArT markers varied from 1062 to 2013 among the four RIL populations.

Phylogenetic analysis with Arabidopsis thaliana and Oryza sativa homologs classified these genes into seven subfamilies. Evolutionary studies revealed 14 segmental duplication events and syntenic conservation with Triticum aestivum.

A hybrid of sweet sorghum M81 and sudangrass Sa was used to construct 174 F11 populations. Genotyping by RAD-seq produced SNPs. These SNPs were used to construct a high-density genetic map of 790 SNPs spanning 2101 cM. The map covered all ten chromosomes with over 80% coverage.

Methods: 288 oat lines were evaluated using 2,213 SNP markers from an oat iSelect 6K-beadchip array to study genetic diversity, population structure, linkage disequilibrium, and genotype-phenotype association for hullessness and lemma color. Results: The average gene diversity and PIC were 0.324 and 0.262.
The sorghum gene SbWRKY51 encodes a group II WRKY transcription factor. Its expression is up-regulated by salt stress, drought, and abscisic acid (ABA).

VRADIO7G29860, VRADIO7G29450, and VRADIO7G28520 encode s-adenosyl-L-methionine, FTSHI1 protein, and CRS2-associated factor, respectively, and are candidate genes for chlorophyll biosynthesis. They may be used to develop genotypes with high SPAD value and trichome density for yield and MYMIV resistance in mungbean.
ASR gene transcript expression patterns indicate ASRs may play roles in abiotic stress responses in foxtail millet tissues. Subcellular localization assays showed SiASR1 localizes in the nucleus.

100 oat cultivars were clustered into three salt tolerance levels. Salt-tolerant cultivars maintained higher growth rate, leaf cell membrane integrity, and osmotic adjustment capability under saline condition.

Two major-effect QTLs SCot8-B-CAAT5-D and HVM54-Bmag0571 on chromosomes 1 and 2 have positive allele effects for developing barley varieties for drought conditions. The new alleles qLWS-4a, qSLS-4, qLNS-7b, qSCS-7, and qLNS-7a are useful for pyramiding elite alleles in molecular breeding and marker assisted selection to improve salinity tolerance in barley.

It is tolerant to drought, salinity, and high temperature. To understand salinity tolerance mechanisms, physiological and transcriptome analyses were conducted between salinity tolerant line ICMB 01222 and salinity susceptible line ICMB 081 under control and salinity conditions.
FtMYB12 was induced by low temperature. FtMYB12 encodes a nuclear transcription activator.

We identified 217 foxtail millet seed resources for drought resistance at the maturity stage. Two cultivars with extremely strong drought resistance, JKH4 (Chi 5422) and JKH6 (Chigu 26), and two with extremely weak drought resistance, JRK3 (17M1309) and JRK6 (Canggu 9), were selected for physiological comparison and transcriptome sequencing before and after drought treatment.

A novel wx loss-of-function allele, wxe, was identified in the sorghum mutant population of BTx623. Compared to the wild-type, wxe exhibited reduced amylose content, increased kernel hardness, elevated protein content, reduced endosperm-to-germ ratio, and decreased kernel weight.
SbWRKY55 binds to the promoter of SbBGLU22 and inhibits its expression. SbWRKY55 interacts with SbFYVE1, blocking the ABA signaling pathway.

The naked trait is controlled by a 17-kb deletion harboring the entire ethylene response factor (ERF) gene on the nud locus on barley chromosome 7HL. We developed a new PCR-based diagnostic marker to rapidly select hulled and naked barley accessions, which produces a smaller product with improved resolution.
Broomcorn millet (Panicum miliaceum L.) is a crop. A worldwide collection of 516 broomcorn millet genomes shows genetic variations, population structure and diversity.

Cowpea is tolerant to drought and salt stresses. WRKY transcription factors are related to abiotic stress tolerance. The expression of the cowpea WRKY gene family in response to water deficit is not well known.
SbCASP-LP1C1 improves salt tolerance in sweet sorghum [Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench]. It enhances the root apoplastic barrier and blocks sodium ion transport to the shoot.

entities.text: blocks sodium ion transport to the shoot
entities.label: TRT

text: Overexpression of SbMPK14 in Arabidopsis and maize caused hypersensitivity to drought by promoting water loss. SbMPK14 functions as a negative regulator of drought response. Transcriptome analysis and qRT-PCR verification of maize SbMPK14 overexpression lines showed that SbMPK14 increases drought sensitivity by suppressing the activity of specific ERF and WRKY transcription factors.
A genome-wide analysis identified 33 AsEPF/EPFL genes in oats (Avena sativa). Among these, 28 genes contain the conserved EPF domain and 5 genes harbor the stomagen domain.
Lutein, zeaxanthin, and beta-carotene were the major carotenoids. Several QTL were detected for each compound, color, and total carotenoids.
QTL for yield and yield components were mapped and tagged using ICi-Mapping software v. 41.

entities.text: QTL for yield and yield components
entities.label: QTL

text: Three primers were designed from the identified SNP to develop an ASM for dehydration tolerance. The ASM produced a 261 bp fragment in all tolerant accessions and no amplification in sensitive accessions.

Nine QTL were identified for PCERGOT. Five QTL were identified using the overall data set. Four QTL were specific to the group data sets defined by temperature and humidity. QTL on SBI-02 and SBI-06 were validated in additional populations.
A GWAS involved 446 elite oat (Avena sativa L.) breeding lines from North America. They were genotyped with 1005 DArT markers and had phenotypic data from historical and balanced 2-year data.

This review integrates findings on the responses of S. viridis and S. italica to drought, heat, light, saline soils, nutrient deficiencies and heavy metal contamination. Comparative analyses highlight conserved and divergent stress-response pathways between S. italica and S. viridis.

One hundred and fourteen oat (Avena sativa L.) varieties were evaluated for genetic diversity using 77 molecular polymorphisms from eight AFLP primer combinations. Genetic similarity was calculated with the DICE coefficient for cluster analysis and principal component analysis.

qRT-PCR analysis showed that AtOAT transformation up-regulated the expression of proline biosynthesis genes TaOAT, TaP5CS, and TaP5CR, and down-regulated the proline catabolism gene TaP5CDH in transgenic plants under stress. Genes in the ornithine pathway (Orn-OAT-P5C/GSA-P5CR-Pro) were up-regulated, along with genes in the glutamate pathway (Glu-P5CS-P5C/GSA-P5CR-Pro).
AsSnRK2D, a member of the SnRK2 family, was isolated from oat (Avena sativa). The AsSnRK2D protein contains SnRK2 family-specific functional domains.

SNP markers for PcKM were tested on these populations and linkage maps were generated. Seventeen newly developed SNP markers from GBS data were mapped in these two populations and three other populations segregating for Pc45 or PcKM.
The genotypic drought response ranking is XiNong 9943 > XiNong 9940 > QianKu-5 > QianKu-4. XiNong 9943 shows drought tolerance and is useful for buckwheat breeding.

Combined stress triggers ABA accumulation by up-regulating NCED genes and suppressing CYP707A genes. Exogenous ABA application under low temperature-drought stress activated the PYR/PYL-PP2C-SnRK2 cascade, enhancing antioxidant defenses and increasing soluble sugars to starch ratio.
ABA treatment depressed most FtRCARs transcripts. ABA upregulated CHS, CHI, F3'H, F3H and FLS transcripts, while 4CL and PAL transcripts were down-regulated.

The proline content decreased in seeds with 4% and 16% moisture content, and increased in seeds with 28% moisture content. Proline played the main role in adaptation to oxidative stress in seeds with 28% moisture content. Antioxidant enzymes played the main role in seeds with 4% and 16% moisture content.

Expression analysis in Tartary buckwheat indicated that FtbZIP83 was induced by ABA, NaCl, and PEG. Overexpression of FtbZIP83 in Arabidopsis increased drought and salt tolerance. This was attributed to higher proline contents and antioxidant enzyme activity, and lower ROS accumulation and MDA content in transgenic lines compared to controls.

Nineteen QTNs and two genes (sdw1/denso and Vrs1) were previously reported. The Vrs1 gene controls the number of rows in the spike. It is associated with spikelet number of main spike, spikelet number per plant, grain number per plant, grain number per spike, and 1,000 grain weight in multiple environments and by multi-locus GWAS methods. Eight candidate genes need further validation.

entities.text: number of rows in the spike
entities.label: TRT

relations.head: Vrs1 gene
