relations.label: AFF

text: Six QTLs, qTB6, qSFW9, qJW9, qBrix2, qBrix10 and qSTI-Brix9, were main effect QTLs controlling salt tolerance with PVE > 10%. qSFW9 and qJW9 colocalized in marker interval SB5069-UGSM18 with PVEs of 17.70% and 14.20%, respectively, and positive effects from L-Tian. QTL clusters controlling PH, TB, STI-TB, SFW and JW were mapped in marker interval Xcup19-SB4177 on chromosome 7.

Gene co-expression networks constructed by WGCNA show distinct expression patterns of SbPLC1, SbPLC3a, and SbPLC4 under abiotic stress. qRT-PCR reveals expression level variations among most SbPLCs under drought, NaCl, NaHCO3, ABA treatments, and developmental stages, indicating specific and overlapping patterns. This analysis provides insights into SbPLCs roles in sorghum, their expression patterns, regulatory elements, and protein interactions under environmental stimuli and developmental stages.

Functional genomics techniques have enabled the identification of key genes implicated in defense responses against fungal pathogens in sorghum. Genome editing of identified resistance genes holds promise for developing high-performing varieties.

Lines or germplasm with the Seita.5G1346005UTR277+ allele exhibited significantly wider leaves than those with the Seita.5G1346005UTR277- allele, both within the RIL population and natural populations.

It reduced reactive oxygen species accumulation and increased chlorophyll content in sorghum leaves. Silencing SbMYC2 by virus-induced gene silencing compromised drought tolerance in sorghum seedlings.

Mutations in the Phytochrome C (SiPHYC) gene, including transposon insertions and a frame shift mutation, cause the extremely early heading phenotype. RNA-Seq analysis identified 19 differentially expressed genes associated with this phenotype. Genome-wide InDels and SNPs were identified, providing resources for marker-assisted breeding.

Heritability for P-R and E-G was larger than for CGR, grain number per square meter, or yield. A multitrait, multienvironment analysis identified 12 QTL (LOD >= 2.5) for CGR, P-R, and E-G, explaining 21 to 36% of trait variability.
The lamina joint bending assay investigated if mutation of the rice homolog of SiCST1 affected brassinolide signaling. oscst1 exhibited insensitivity to exogenous BR treatment.

Among these QTNs and adjacent genes, 39 QTNs were repeatedly detected. 10 candidate genes were identified. 19 QTNs and two genes (sdw1/denso and Vrs1) were previously reported. 8 candidate genes need further validation.
The high-density linkage map was created using GBS. The map contained 1082 SNP markers on 10 chromosomes, with an average marker distance of 1.83 cm.

Landraces possessed greater genetic diversity and contained most HvVRN1 alleles compared to modern commercial barley, especially in winter barley regions. HvVRN1 allele number diversity reduced significantly with higher growing latitudes, but increased in accessions carrying the winter HvVRN2 allele.

Drought affects sorghum growth and development. It impedes germination and embryonic structures through osmotic potential, reduces photosynthetic rates, and causes source-sink imbalance affecting seed filling. This reduces grain yield and quality. Sorghum responds to drought with morphological, physiological, and molecular alterations.

The AC cultivar was inoculated with race 73 of C. lindemuthianum. The relative expression of Co-AC candidate genes, other putative genes near this locus, and known resistance genes was assessed. Phvul.001G244500 showed significantly higher expression.

Comparative transcriptome profiling revealed differentially expressed transcripts associated with salt stress in pearl millet at 1, 3, and 7 hours of salt treatment. Salt stress increased proline, malondialdehyde, and hydrogen peroxide content in pearl millet at these time points. Pearl millet regulated superoxide dismutase, catalase, and peroxidase activities to reduce salinity impact.

Genotypes LER 1 and LER 2 are tolerant to hydric stress due to lower PSII damage. LER 1 and LEB 2 have superior RWC. LER 1 is thermo tolerant due to lower DCM at 45°C. LER 1 has potential for hydric and heat stress tolerance.

An analysis of candidate genes associated with six significant markers revealed immune receptors and putative disease resistance proteins. This genomic region on chromosome 4A is associated with crown rust resistance in oats.
TaTPP-7A is expressed in developing grains and influences grain filling and size. Overexpression of TaTPP-7A in wheat enhances grain TKW and yield.

Representative single plant selections (SPS) from the two sub-populations were grown with SPS from off-type plants and plants from the original AV25 composite mixture. Plants were genotyped by DNA extraction of 96 samples. SNPs were detected using an Illumina Infinium 6K Chip assay.

Seventy-six QTLs were mapped using recombinant inbred lines (RIL) population Arta x Harmal-2//Esp/1808-4L, evaluated in dry and semi-dry areas over 3 years. Traits included grain yield, biological yield, harvest index, kernel weight, seed per head, days to heading, kernel filling duration, growth vigour, growth habit, lodging and plant height.

The study built global and seed-preferential co-expression networks of starch synthesis pathways. These networks indicated that photosynthesis and microtubule-based movement play important roles in starch synthesis. The study generated a global transcriptome atlas of sorghum. This atlas provides a functional genomics resource for trait discovery and insight into starch synthesis regulation in sorghum.

Molecular breeding achievements include efficient transformation systems, CRISPR/Cas9-mediated genome editing enhanced by morphogenic regulators, and mutagenized populations for gene function validation. Challenges remain in functional dissection of complex quantitative traits, integration of multi-omics datasets, and genotype-dependent transformation efficiency.

Single marker analysis associated 29 markers with plant response to greenbug feeding damage. Interval mapping, composite interval mapping and multiple interval mapping identified four major QTLs for greenbug resistance on chromosome 9.

This study investigated three foxtail millet cultivars with different drought resistance. Exploring candidate genes for foxtail millet drought resistance and revealing its molecular regulatory mechanism and metabolic pathway is significant.

Marker S9_57480917, linked to a GATA transcription factor, showed pleiotropic effects on transpiration efficiency and shoot dry weight. Shoot biomass-related markers co-localized with genes for energy metabolism (Phosphofructokinase) and stress signaling (SnRK2 kinase).

A high-density genetic map spanning 2233.5 cM was constructed with 1069 markers. Using 138 reported QTLs, six Meta-QTL regions for heat tolerance were identified. Their confidence interval was reduced by 2.7-folds compared to the reported QTLs.
The transcriptome of broomcorn millet was sequenced and assembled using Illumina technology. For genotypes Yumi No. 2 and Yumi No. 3, 45,406,730 and 51,160,820 clean paired-end reads were obtained.
SbPUB genes are unevenly distributed on the 10 chromosomes of sorghum. Most SbPUB genes (16) are on chromosome 4. No SbPUB genes are on chromosome 5.

163 polymorphic loci were found in genome-wide screening. SNP-index values were 0.75 for the salt-tolerant offspring pool and 0.25 for the non-salt-tolerant offspring pool. 20 candidate genes were screened based on ANNOVAR annotation.

Foxtail millet (Setaria italica) is a crop with salt and drought tolerance. 16 PLATZ genes are identified in foxtail millet. These genes are mainly expressed in roots and panicles and are induced by abiotic stresses.

Tan1 and Tan2 play a major role in seed dormancy and induce resistance against soil-borne pathogens under cold and wet soil conditions. Seed tannin content may be used as an indirect selection marker for identifying cold tolerant germplasm for breeding.

Pm3 is the only powdery mildew resistance gene previously mapped on Mrg18. However, the pm3 differential line Mostyn was susceptible to the powdery mildew race used in this study, indicating that Pm3 and QPm.18 are different genes.

Single-marker analysis and composite interval mapping identified a QTL on chromosome 5 associated with egg number and egg number per gram of root from the resistant sweet sorghum line PI 144134. This QTL on chromosome 5 and a prior QTL on chromosome 3 can be moved from PI 144134 and Honey Drip, respectively, into elite sorghum germplasm via marker-assisted selection.

A genome-wide association study identified 36 significant QTNs associated with three Striga resistance indices. In sorghum, 33 potential candidate genes are located near 17 strongly associated and reliable QTNs.

The rutin content (RC) of 101 natural TB materials ranged from 14.08 to 24.50 mg/g, with an average of 19.46 mg/g. This revealed substantial natural variation in RC. Furthermore, 314,166 high-quality SNPs were identified through whole-genome resequencing.
Oat cultivars were subjected to drought stress from plant growth to the two-leaf stage using a pot-based water withholding method. Stress durations were 0 d, 7 d, and 14 d.

Marker-assisted backcrossing (MABC) pyramided a CLS resistance gene and two PM resistance genes from donor parent D2 into susceptible variety KING. Five markers linked to the resistance genes were used for foreground selection. Two marker sets [Set A with 15 polymorphic SSR and EST-SSR markers, Set B with 34 polymorphic ISSR loci] were used for background selection.

A global collection of Fagopyrum esculentum and Fagopyrum tataricum (190 and 51 accessions) was characterized using 37 agro-morphological traits and 24 SSR markers. Variation was observed for most traits.

Many transcript changes were common to heat or drought alone. However, 2043 specific transcripts, representing 7% of all gene probes, were changed only after the combined stress treatment. Ontological analysis of these unique transcripts identified a potential role for specific transcription factors including MYB78 and ATAF1, chaperones including unique heat shock proteins (HSPs), and metabolic pathways including polyamine biosynthesis in the Sorghum combined stress response.

The study investigated five major Shanxi Province cultivars of foxtail millet (Setaria italica) released from the 1970s to 2020s. It assessed yield, agronomic traits, photosynthesis, photosynthetic products, and canopy structure under field conditions.

Genes related to physiological traits are involved in starch and sucrose metabolism, phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, glutathione metabolism, and plant hormone signal transduction. The active oxygen metabolism system and soluble protein of proso millet root are regulated by protein serine/threonine phosphatase activity.

The autopolyploid showed few gene expression changes over long periods and under drought stress. However, more DEGs and up-regulated DEGs were observed between autotetraploid and diploid than in the CK. Genome duplication also resulted in gain or loss of drought-related genes, which may help autopolyploid survive better under drought.

Cool temperatures limit sorghum growth in temperate areas. Some Chinese landraces show higher emergence and greater seedling vigor under cool conditions than most breeding lines, but lack desirable agronomic characteristics.

The gene belongs to the S1Fa-like family, is localized in chloroplasts, and lacks transcriptional activation. FtS1Fa1 promotes rutin accumulation in Tartary buckwheat by upregulating the rutin biosynthesis gene.

Using extreme recombinants from a fine-mapping F-3 population, qGW1 was delimited to a 101-kb region on chromosome 1. This region contains 13 predicted gene models. One of these genes was under purifying selection during domestication. None of the grain size candidate genes shared sequence similarity with previously cloned grain weight-related genes from rice.

The study aimed to identify genomic regions and candidate genes for drought tolerance traits to guide marker-assisted breeding. Ninety-nine groundnut genotypes were phenotyped under drought-stressed and non-stressed field conditions in 2018/19 and 2019/20, and using the LeasyScan platform under non-stressed conditions in 2019/20 at ICRISAT/India.

The barley spontaneous mutant Sheathed Spike 1 (SS1) shows significantly shortened upper internode and sheathed spike. The upper internode parenchyma cell extension in SS1 is significantly suppressed.

Crown rust resistance is described in oat diploid species A. strigosa accession PI 258731. Resistance from PI 258731 is introgressed into hexaploid A. sativa germplasm. This study maps QTL for resistance in PI 258731. It evaluates the number of QTL. It understands the relationship between the genomic location in A. strigosa and the introgression location in A. sativa. It identifies molecular markers linked with PI 258731 resistance loci. These markers could be used for marker assisted selection and detection of this resistance in diverse A. strigosa accessions.

The mechanisms of aphid resistance in sorghum varieties were studied. The study involved epidermal cell structure, transcriptome, and metabolome analysis of aphid-resistant and aphid-susceptible varieties. Epidermal cell results showed that sorghum resistance to aphids was positively correlated with epidermal cell regularity and negatively correlated with intercellular space and leaf thickness.

34 main-effect QTLs were identified for traits: heading date, plant height, lodging, visual score, grain yield, kernel weight, milling yield, test weight, thin and plump kernels, groat beta-glucan concentration, oil concentration, and protein. Several QTLs correspond to those in homologous or homoeologous regions reported in other oat studies.

Allelic variation in 'QTL-hotspot' improves pre-anthesis water use, transpiration efficiency, root architecture and canopy development, enabling high-yield performance under terminal drought. CaTIFY4b may regulate organ size under water deficit by modulating GIF1 expression.

The proso millet cultivar 'Yumi 2' was analyzed under stimulated drought stress using 20% PEG-6000. SOD activity, POD activity, soluble protein content, MDA, and O-2(-) content increased with drought stress time but decreased after rehydration.
EBR application up-regulated CqBIN2 transcript levels and proline biosynthesis genes. Silencing CqBIN2 by VIGS reduced drought tolerance, survival rate, and proline content in quinoa seedlings.
Expression profiling of bZIP genes was performed using qRT-PCR in response to drought, salt and ABA. Several bZIP genes involved in drought and salt responses were identified.

Genomic regions contributing to genetic differentiation suggest loci related to growth habit and stress resistance were under intense selection, not hulless-related regions. Genome-wide association analysis detected four markers highly associated with hullessness.

We identified candidate genes for salt tolerance in quinoa. Some DEGs were exclusively expressed in the ST genotype. DEGs common to both genotypes under salt stress may be key genes for quinoa salinity adaptation.

Genic SSR markers were developed from mungbean transcriptome sequences and transferred to related Vigna species. EST sequences from mungbean genotype 'Jangan' were searched for SSRs. 2,299 SSR motifs from 1,848 SSR-containing unique ESTs were identified.
Foxtail millet grain is rich in nutrition. Foxtail millet is tolerant to abiotic stresses including drought.

Expression profiling of NILs and qRT-PCR assays showed changes in predicted genes at 30 and 45 DAF in leaf tissues of qSTG introgressed lines. There was a significant difference in transcript levels of genes between different DAF. QTL pyramiding has potential for improving plant performance under adverse environmental conditions.

The sterile lines 521A and 170A, and the restorer lines Ji318R and 0-30 were promising parents with high general combining ability. All endogenous substances of root bleeding sap were positively correlated with the sum of parental general combining ability at highly significant levels. IAA, CTK, GA3, ABA, soluble sugar, amino acid, and root bleeding intensity were positively correlated with male general combining ability at significant or highly significant levels.

An in silico B.Tx623 wx(a) genome assembly was constructed from the B.Tx623 reference genome (v3.1.1) to include the insertion, a similar to 5-kb-long terminal repeat (LTR) copia retrotransposon. This in silico wxa assembly improved read mapping at Sobic.010G022600 in wx(a) individuals, identified 78 new uniquely mapped reads, and enabled distinguishing different Waxy genotypes using short-read sequencing data.

Transcriptome profiling from 1 to 25 dpa reveals dynamic gene expression pathways. These pathways shift from cellular growth and embryo development (1–5 dpa) to cell division, fatty acid biosynthesis (5–25 dpa), and seed storage compounds synthesis in the endosperm (5–25 dpa). Network analysis identifies 361 hub genes linked to starch synthesis in the endosperm and 207 hub genes linked to protein synthesis in the endosperm. This will help breeders enhance sorghum grain quality.

4552 and 4845 differentially expressed genes (DEGs) were identified in XCq and QCq under cold stress, respectively. KEGG pathway analysis showed the MAPK signaling pathway was only among up-regulated DEGs in XCq. The expression of DEGs encoding transcription factors AP2/ERF, bHLH, bZIP, MYB, ICEs, and CORs related to cold response was higher in XCq than in QCq under cold stress.

The barley genome has three HvGA20ox genes. Loss of function in HvGA20ox2 can be compensated by enhanced expression of HvGA20ox1 and HvGA20ox3. A diagnostic molecular marker distinguishes wild-type, sdw1.d and sdw1.a alleles. Another molecular marker differentiates sdw1.c and sdw1.a.

Transcriptome analysis identified 588 differentially expressed genes (DEGs), with 383 up-regulated and 205 down-regulated. The DEGs were associated with ribosomes, plant hormone signal transduction, and photosynthesis. Additionally, 549 differentially expressed proteins (DEPs) were identified in maize leaves under both monocropping and maize-peanut intercropping systems.

Diverse sources of tolerance and contrasting lines can be used for sorghum improvement programs through crossing to obtain extreme phenotypes. High yielding and drought tolerant lines were abundant among accessions from lowland areas, implying targeted collection from these areas is important for improving drought adaptation of sorghum.

Promoter analysis revealed stress-responsive cis-regulatory elements (MYB, MYC, ARE, MBS), implicating AsaPP2Cs in biotic stress adaptation. Transcriptomic data identified seven differentially expressed AsaPP2C genes: Asa_chr6Dg00217, Asa_chr6Ag01950, Asa_chr3Ag01998, Asa_chr5Ag00079, Asa_chr4Cg03270, Asa_chr6Cg02197, and Asa_chr7Dg02992, validated via qRT-PCR.

DPY1 is phosphorylated and activated in response to osmotic stress. DPY1 is required for osmotic stress-induced phosphorylation events, including phosphorylation of SnRK2s. DPY1 acts upstream of SAPK6 and is required for full SAPK6 activation, regulating downstream genes in response to drought stress.
Pearl millet is tolerant to heat and drought. It can be used to identify heat-resistant genes.

Stacking resistant alleles from zero to five significantly decreased DON values and increased germination ability. The haplotype information of historical and modern Nordic varieties was analyzed and can be used to select parents for crossings.

LN improved the expression of flavonoid biosynthetic genes. The transcriptional regulation mediated by MYB and bHLH was analyzed. 78 transcription factors, 124 small secreted peptides and 38 receptor-like protein kinases encoding genes were involved in LN response.

A population of 184 recombinant inbred lines (RILs) was derived from the cross E36-1 x SPV570. The RILs and parents were phenotyped for yield component traits in field and root traits in an above ground rhizotron. High heritability and genetic advance were estimated for all root traits and most yield traits.

In class U, CqGSTU22, CqGSTU67, CqGSTU43, CqGSTU42, and CqGSTU15 were strongly expressed in various tissues and organs. In class L, CqGSTL1 and CqGSTL5 were strongly expressed. In class Z, CqGSTZ5 was strongly expressed. In DHAR class, CqDHAR2 and CqDHAR3 were strongly expressed. CqGSTU66, CqGSTF20, and CqGSTF23 were not expressed in the tested tissues or organs.

The R2R3-MYB transcription factor FtMYB43 positively regulates procyanidin synthesis. The glycoside hydrolase gene FtSAGH1 is responsible for releasing active salicylic acid, which is a precursor of aspirin and is involved in plant defence.

FtMYB13, a R2R3-MYB from Tartary buckwheat, is located in the nucleus and is not a transcriptional activator. Compared to wild type (WT), transgenic Arabidopsis overexpressing FtMYB13 showed lower ABA sensitivity and improved drought/salt tolerance. This was due to higher proline content, greater photosynthetic efficiency, higher transcript abundance of stress-related genes, and smaller amounts of reactive oxygen species (ROS) and malondialdehyde (MDA).

Drought tolerance of mung bean cultivars/lines was examined based on seed germination characteristics, antioxidative potential, and nutrient uptake. PEG-induced drought adversely affected germination enzyme activities, seed germination attributes, seedling biomass production, and nutrient uptake. Total soluble proteins (TSP) and malondialdehyde (MDA) contents increased.

The genes are distributed across seven chromosomes and classified into ten subfamilies. The encoded proteins vary in length, physicochemical properties, and predicted subcellular localization, with most members containing the conserved PP2Cc catalytic domain.

QJy-sbi06 was located in a 21.2 kb region on chromosome 6. The candidate gene sobic.006G147400, encoding an NAC transcription factor, was identified in this region. Association analysis and sequencing showed that XL52 has a 1.8 kb transposon insertion in the NAC gene, which interrupts and inactivates the juice yield gene.
300 quinoa accessions were screened for adaptation to low-altitude areas using seventeen traits. The experiment was conducted in Taigu District, Shanxi Province in 2021.
Marker Xtxp211 significantly affected seedling vigor and emergence in both populations. Loci interactions were also significant.

Candidate genes are associated with QTL for insect resistance. The study genetically mapped the candidate genes and identified their association with shoot fly resistance. It updated the genetic map with new markers to locate additional QTL.

Water deficit stress affects mung bean growth and reduces yield. A field experiment evaluated four water stress-tolerant genotypes: BARI Mung-8, BMX-08010-2, BMX-010015, and BMX-08009-7, along with two check varieties, BARI Mung-6 and BARI Mung-7, across five environments.

It has a short life cycle, high harvest index, low planting costs, strong adaptability and resistance. This study reveals sodium ion (Na+) toxicity resistance strategies from germination to seedling stage, root to leaf, and phenotype to mechanism.

Expression analysis found that SbHSP60-3 is implicated in drought tolerance, SbHSP60-9 in salt tolerance, SbHSP60-9 and SbHSP60-24 in heat tolerance, and SbHSP60-3, SbHSP60-9, and SbHSP10-2 in cold stress tolerance. These genes are key regulatory genes.
114 CqGST genes were identified from the quinoa genome. The phylogenetic tree showed these genes divided into seven subgroups.

QTL VaSdt4.1 for seed thickness (SDT) and three QTLs for branch number on the main stem were detected on chromosome 4. QTLs for maximum leaf width (LFMW) and stem internode length were mapped to chromosomes 2 and 9. Genes controlling seed coat, pod, stem and flower color were mapped to chromosomes 3 and 1.

text: Haplotype analysis linked three SbPaO genes to thousand-grain weight (TGW). Superior haplotypes SbPaO1-hap4, SbPaO3-hap5, and SbPaO4-hap4 increased TGW. Single-gene improvements increased TGW by 10.57-17.20%, dual-gene aggregation by 18.78-24.75%, and three-gene aggregation by 29.09%.

RT-qPCR characterized AsNAC gene expression in different tissues and its response to abiotic stresses. Collinearity analysis identified segmental duplication as the main driver of oat NAC gene family expansion. The functions of AsNAC109 in regulating abiotic stress tolerance were clarified in Arabidopsis.
In the XiNong 9943 genotype, SOD, POD, CAT, and APX levels at 30% PEG were 2.01, 2.19, 4.92, and 4.46 times higher, respectively, than under normal growth condition (T0).

Understanding the genetic and molecular factors regulating carbohydrate metabolism is necessary to improve legume starch content, quality, and quantity. This review assessed the genetic and molecular basis of legume carbohydrate components, focusing on seed starch content. It provided an overview of starch biosynthesis in heterotrophic organs, the chemical composition of major consumable legumes, factors influencing starch digestibility, and advances in genetic, transcriptomic, and metabolomic studies in important legume crops.

Three QTNs were associated with days to 50% flowering; two each with leaf-to-stem ratio and number of nodes per plant; and one each with plant height, leaf width, number of leaves per plant, stem girth, and internodal length. Six candidate genes were associated with days to 50% flowering, two each with leaf width, stem girth, leaf-to-stem ratio, and number of nodes per plant, and one each with plant height, number of leaves per plant, and internodal length. FarmCPU was the most suitable model for controlling false positives and false negatives. Analysis of the QTNs may identify new candidate genes for the trait.
HvASR2 and HvASR3 expression in barley seedlings was influenced by salt, osmotic, and ABA treatments. HvASR2 is a transcriptional repressor, and HvASR3 is a transcriptional activator.
Most CqAP2/ERF genes showed distinct expression patterns in different tissues and developmental stages. Five CqAP2/ERF genes responded to drought, high temperature, salt, and low phosphorus.

A QTL (OLRNIII-g864) for lateral root number is near TP3371 and flanked by TP5093 and TP6072 SNP markers. Another QTL (QSRL(IV_61.63)) for specific root length is near TP1873 and flanked by F7XEM6b SRAP and TP1035 SNP markers.
Map-based cloning showed an eight-base pair deletion in the sixth exon of wp1.a in LOC101786849, causing premature termination. WP1 encodes phytoene synthase.
The 2S albumin from pine nut (Pin p 1.0101) was recognized by IgE of 75% of sera.
High contribution QTLs were distributed on seven out of 11 linkage groups. Many small contribution QTLs were found.
Selected silicoDArT markers were used for chromosomal localization of resistance genes. Five of 71 segregating silicoDArT markers for the Pm11 gene were found on the oat consensus genetic map.

Gene editing generated three new haplotypes of the sdw1/denso candidate gene gibberellin 20-oxidase2 (GA20ox2). These haplotypes induced a dwarfing phenotype, enhanced yield potential, and promoted seed dormancy to reduce pre-harvest sprouting. Beta-amylase activity in the grains of the mutant lines was significantly increased, which benefits malt quality.

Drought interactions with abiotic stresses and biotic factors can increase crop vulnerability or enhance drought tolerance. Modern plant breeding techniques can develop locally adapted, drought tolerant, and nutrient rich sorghum cultivars.

Kruskall-Wallis and multiple-QTL model mapping identified QTL associated with drought response phenotypes. The QTL were reproducible between stg and rdw under greenhouse conditions. Field studies confirmed all ten drought-response QTL observed under greenhouse conditions.

The expression levels of three SWEET genes and four PIP genes were higher in YG than in An. This allocated more photosynthetic sugar to the roots. The high expression levels of SiPIPs enhanced root water transport capacity.

We evaluated simulated traits from maize (Zea mays L.) and sorghum [Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench] diversity panels using ridge-regression best linear unbiased prediction (RR-BLUP) models. The models included fixed-effect covariates tagging peak GWAS signals. The covariates increased genomic selection (GS) prediction accuracy in the RR-BLUP model under various genetic architectures and genomic backgrounds.

Three major stable pleiotropic QTLs (qSCS-1, qRLS-1, and qLNN-1) were associated with SCR, RL, and RN in both normal and salinity treatments. Two major stable pleiotropic QTLs (qSNN-3 and qLWS-3) associated with SN and LW are promising for MAS. Two major-effect QTLs (SCot8-B-CAAT5-D and HVM54-Bmag0571) on chromosomes 1 and 2 were characterized for their positive allele effect, which can be used to develop barley varieties for drought conditions.
